item.title: $R$-equivalence on Cubic Surfaces I: Existing Cases with Non-Trivial Universal Equivalence
item.content:encoded: Let $V$ be a smooth cubic surface over a $p$-adic field $k$ with good reduction. Swinnerton-Dyer (1981) proved that $R$-equivalence is trivial on $V(k)$ except perhaps if $V$ is one of three special types--those whose $R$-equivalence he could not bound by proving the universal (admissible) equivalence is trivial. We consider all surfaces $V$ currently known to have non-trivial universal equivalence. Beyond being intractable to Swinnerton-Dyer's approach, we observe that if these surfaces also had non-trivial $R$-equivalence, they would contradict Colliot-Thélène and Sansuc's conjecture regarding the $k$-rationality of universal torsors for geometrically rational surfaces. By devising new methods to study $R$-equivalence, we prove that for 2-adic surfaces with all-Eckardt reductions (the third special type, which contains every existing case of non-trivial universal equivalence), $R$-equivalence is trivial or of exponent 2. For the explicit cases, we confirm triviality: the diagonal cubic $X^3+Y^3+Z^3+ζ_3 T^3=0$ over $\mathbb{Q}_2(ζ_3)$--answering a long-standing question of Manin's (Cubic Forms, 1972)--and the cubic with universal equivalence of exponent 2 (Kanevsky, 1982). This is the first in a series of works derived from a year of interactions with generative AI models such as AlphaEvolve and Gemini 3 Deep Think, with the latter proving many of our lemmas. We disclose the timeline and nature of their use towards this paper, and describe our broader AI-assisted research program in a companion report (in preparation).

item.title: Improving Joint Audio-Video Generation with Cross-Modal Context Learning
item.content:encoded: The dual-stream transformer architecture-based joint audio-video generation method has become the dominant paradigm in current research. By incorporating pre-trained video diffusion models and audio diffusion models, along with a cross-modal interaction attention module, high-quality, temporally synchronized audio-video content can be generated with minimal training data. In this paper, we first revisit the dual-stream transformer paradigm and further analyze its limitations, including model manifold variations caused by the gating mechanism controlling cross-modal interactions, biases in multi-modal background regions introduced by cross-modal attention, and the inconsistencies in multi-modal classifier-free guidance (CFG) during training and inference, as well as conflicts between multiple conditions. To alleviate these issues, we propose Cross-Modal Context Learning (CCL), equipped with several carefully designed modules. Temporally Aligned RoPE and Partitioning (TARP) effectively enhances the temporal alignment between audio latent and video latent representations. The Learnable Context Tokens (LCT) and Dynamic Context Routing (DCR) in the Cross-Modal Context Attention (CCA) module provide stable unconditional anchors for cross-modal information, while dynamically routing based on different training tasks, further enhancing the model's convergence speed and generation quality. During inference, Unconditional Context Guidance (UCG) leverages the unconditional support provided by LCT to facilitate different forms of CFG, improving train-inference consistency and further alleviating conflicts. Through comprehensive evaluations, CCL achieves state-of-the-art performance compared with recent academic methods while requiring substantially fewer resources.

item.title: BVSIMC: Bayesian Variable Selection-Guided Inductive Matrix Completion for Improved and Interpretable Drug Discovery
item.content:encoded: Recent advances in drug discovery have demonstrated that incorporating side information (e.g., chemical properties about drugs and genomic information about diseases) often greatly improves prediction performance. However, these side features can vary widely in relevance and are often noisy and high-dimensional. We propose Bayesian Variable Selection-Guided Inductive Matrix Completion (BVSIMC), a new Bayesian model that enables variable selection from side features in drug discovery. By learning sparse latent embeddings, BVSIMC improves both predictive accuracy and interpretability. We validate our method through simulation studies and two drug discovery applications: 1) prediction of drug resistance in Mycobacterium tuberculosis, and 2) prediction of new drug-disease associations in computational drug repositioning. On both synthetic and real data, BVSIMC outperforms several other state-of-the-art methods in terms of prediction. In our two real examples, BVSIMC further reveals the most clinically meaningful side features.

item.title: TopoChunker: Topology-Aware Agentic Document Chunking Framework
item.content:encoded: Current document chunking methods for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) typically linearize text. This forced linearization strips away intrinsic topological hierarchies, creating ``semantic fragmentation'' that degrades downstream retrieval quality. In this paper, we propose TopoChunker, an agentic framework that maps heterogeneous documents onto a Structured Intermediate Representation (SIR) to explicitly preserve cross-segment dependencies. To balance structural fidelity with computational cost, TopoChunker employs a dual-agent architecture. An Inspector Agent dynamically routes documents through cost-optimized extraction paths, while a Refiner Agent performs capacity auditing and topological context disambiguation to reconstruct hierarchical lineage. Evaluated on unstructured narratives (GutenQA) and complex reports (GovReport), TopoChunker demonstrates state-of-the-art performance. It outperforms the strongest LLM-based baseline by 8.0% in absolute generation accuracy and achieves an 83.26% Recall@3, while simultaneously reducing token overhead by 23.5%, offering a scalable approach for structure-aware RAG.

item.title: Bridging Semantic and Kinematic Conditions with Diffusion-based Discrete Motion Tokenizer
item.content:encoded: Prior motion generation largely follows two paradigms: continuous diffusion models that excel at kinematic control, and discrete token-based generators that are effective for semantic conditioning. To combine their strengths, we propose a three-stage framework comprising condition feature extraction (Perception), discrete token generation (Planning), and diffusion-based motion synthesis (Control). Central to this framework is MoTok, a diffusion-based discrete motion tokenizer that decouples semantic abstraction from fine-grained reconstruction by delegating motion recovery to a diffusion decoder, enabling compact single-layer tokens while preserving motion fidelity. For kinematic conditions, coarse constraints guide token generation during planning, while fine-grained constraints are enforced during control through diffusion-based optimization. This design prevents kinematic details from disrupting semantic token planning. On HumanML3D, our method significantly improves controllability and fidelity over MaskControl while using only one-sixth of the tokens, reducing trajectory error from 0.72 cm to 0.08 cm and FID from 0.083 to 0.029. Unlike prior methods that degrade under stronger kinematic constraints, ours improves fidelity, reducing FID from 0.033 to 0.014.

item.title: HISR: Hindsight Information Modulated Segmental Process Rewards For Multi-turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning
item.content:encoded: While large language models excel in diverse domains, their performance on complex longhorizon agentic decision-making tasks remains limited. Most existing methods concentrate on designing effective reward models (RMs) to advance performance via multi-turn reinforcement learning. However, they suffer from delayed propagation in sparse outcome rewards and unreliable credit assignment with potentially overly fine-grained and unfocused turnlevel process rewards. In this paper, we propose (HISR) exploiting Hindsight Information to modulate Segmental process Rewards, which closely aligns rewards with sub-goals and underscores significant segments to enhance the reliability of credit assignment. Specifically, a segment-level process RM is presented to assign rewards for each sub-goal in the task, avoiding excessively granular allocation to turns. To emphasize significant segments in the trajectory, a hindsight model is devised to reflect the preference of performing a certain action after knowing the trajectory outcome. With this characteristic, we design the ratios of sequence likelihoods between hindsight and policy model to measure action importance. The ratios are subsequently employed to aggregate segment importance scores, which in turn modulate segmental process rewards, enhancing credit assignment reliability. Extensive experimental results on three publicly benchmarks demonstrate the validity of our method.

item.title: The Spillover Effects of Peer AI Rinsing on Corporate Green Innovation
item.content:encoded: At a time when the phenomenon of 'AI washing' is quietly spreading, an increasing number of enterprises are using the label of artificial intelligence merely as a cosmetic embellishment in their annual reports, rather than as a genuine engine driving transformation. A test regarding the essence of innovation and the authenticity of information disclosure has arrived. This paper employs large language models to conduct semantic analysis on the text of annual reports from Chinese A-share listed companies from 2006 to 2024, systematically examining the impact of corporate AI washing behaviour on their green innovation. The research reveals that corporate AI washing exerts a significant crowding-out effect on green innovation, with this negative relationship transmitted through dual channels in both product and capital markets. Furthermore, this crowding-out effect exhibits heterogeneity across firms and industries, with private enterprises, small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), and firms in highly competitive sectors suffering more severe negative impacts from AI washing. Simulation results indicate that a combination of policy tools can effectively improve market equilibrium. Based on this, this paper proposes that the government should design targeted support tools to 'enhance market returns and alleviate financing constraints', adopt a differentiated regulatory strategy, and establish a disclosure mechanism combining 'professional identification and reputational sanctions' to curb such peer AI washing behaviour.

item.title: Attack by Unlearning: Unlearning-Induced Adversarial Attacks on Graph Neural Networks
item.content:encoded: Graph neural networks (GNNs) are widely used for learning from graph-structured data in domains such as social networks, recommender systems, and financial platforms. To comply with privacy regulations like the GDPR, CCPA, and PIPEDA, approximate graph unlearning, which aims to remove the influence of specific data points from trained models without full retraining, has become an increasingly important component of trustworthy graph learning. However, approximate unlearning often incurs subtle performance degradation, which may incur negative and unintended side effects. In this work, we show that such degradations can be amplified into adversarial attacks. We introduce the notion of \textbf{unlearning corruption attacks}, where an adversary injects carefully chosen nodes into the training graph and later requests their deletion. Because deletion requests are legally mandated and cannot be denied, this attack surface is both unavoidable and stealthy: the model performs normally during training, but accuracy collapses only after unlearning is applied. Technically, we formulate this attack as a bi-level optimization problem: to overcome the challenges of black-box unlearning and label scarcity, we approximate the unlearning process via gradient-based updates and employ a surrogate model to generate pseudo-labels for the optimization. Extensive experiments across benchmarks and unlearning algorithms demonstrate that small, carefully designed unlearning requests can induce significant accuracy degradation, raising urgent concerns about the robustness of GNN unlearning under real-world regulatory demands. The source code will be released upon paper acceptance.

item.title: SAVeS: Steering Safety Judgments in Vision-Language Models via Semantic Cues
item.content:encoded: Vision-language models (VLMs) are increasingly deployed in real-world and embodied settings where safety decisions depend on visual context. However, it remains unclear which visual evidence drives these judgments. We study whether multimodal safety behavior in VLMs can be steered by simple semantic cues. We introduce a semantic steering framework that applies controlled textual, visual, and cognitive interventions without changing the underlying scene content. To evaluate these effects, we propose SAVeS, a benchmark for situational safety under semantic cues, together with an evaluation protocol that separates behavioral refusal, grounded safety reasoning, and false refusals. Experiments across multiple VLMs and an additional state-of-the-art benchmark show that safety decisions are highly sensitive to semantic cues, indicating reliance on learned visual-linguistic associations rather than grounded visual understanding. We further demonstrate that automated steering pipelines can exploit these mechanisms, highlighting a potential vulnerability in multimodal safety systems.

item.title: Evaluating Model-Free Policy Optimization in Masked-Action Environments via an Exact Blackjack Oracle
item.content:encoded: Infinite-shoe casino blackjack provides a rigorous, exactly verifiable benchmark for discrete stochastic control under dynamically masked actions. Under a fixed Vegas-style ruleset (S17, 3:2 payout, dealer peek, double on any two, double after split, resplit to four), an exact dynamic programming (DP) oracle was derived over 4,600 canonical decision cells. This oracle yielded ground-truth action values, optimal policy labels, and a theoretical expected value (EV) of -0.00161 per hand. To evaluate sample-efficient policy recovery, three model-free optimizers were trained via simulated interaction: masked REINFORCE with a per-cell exponential moving average baseline, simultaneous perturbation stochastic approximation (SPSA), and the cross-entropy method (CEM). REINFORCE was the most sample-efficient, achieving a 46.37% action-match rate and an EV of -0.04688 after 10^6 hands, outperforming CEM (39.46%, 7.5x10^6 evaluations) and SPSA (38.63%, 4.8x10^6 evaluations). However, all methods exhibited substantial cell-conditional regret, indicating persistent policy-level errors despite smooth reward convergence. This gap shows that tabular environments with severe state-visitation sparsity and dynamic action masking remain challenging, while aggregate reward curves can obscure critical local failures. As a negative control, it was proven and empirically confirmed that under i.i.d. draws without counting, optimal bet sizing collapses to the table minimum. In addition, larger wagers strictly increased volatility and ruin without improving expectation. These results highlight the need for exact oracles and negative controls to avoid mistaking stochastic variability for genuine algorithmic performance.

item.title: GEAR: Geography-knowledge Enhanced Analog Recognition Framework in Extreme Environments
item.content:encoded: The Mariana Trench and the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau exhibit significant similarities in geological origins and microbial metabolic functions. Given that deep-sea biological sampling faces prohibitive costs, recognizing structurally homologous terrestrial analogs of the Mariana Trench on the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau is of great significance. Yet, no existing model adequately addresses cross-domain topographic similarity retrieval, either neglecting geographical knowledge or sacrificing computational efficiency. To address these challenges, we present \underline{\textbf{G}}eography-knowledge \underline{\textbf{E}}nhanced \underline{\textbf{A}}nalog \underline{\textbf{R}}ecognition (\textbf{GEAR}) Framework, a three-stage pipeline designed to efficiently retrieve analogs from 2.5 million square kilometers of the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau: (1) Skeleton guided Screening and Clipping: Recognition of candidate valleys and initial screening based on size and linear morphological criteria. (2) Physics aware Filtering: The Topographic Waveform Comparator (TWC) and Morphological Texture Module (MTM) evaluate the waveform and texture and filter out inconsistent candidate valleys. (3) Graph based Fine Recognition: We design a \underline{\textbf{M}}orphology-integrated \underline{\textbf{S}}iamese \underline{\textbf{G}}raph \underline{\textbf{N}}etwork (\textbf{MSG-Net}) based on geomorphological metrics. Correspondingly, we release an expert-annotated topographic similarity dataset targeting tectonic collision zones. Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of every stage. Besides, MSG-Net achieved an F1-Score 1.38 percentage points higher than the SOTA baseline. Using features extracted by MSG-Net, we discovered a significant correlation with biological data, providing evidence for future biological analysis.

item.title: HORNet: Task-Guided Frame Selection for Video Question Answering with Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: Video question answering (VQA) with vision-language models (VLMs) depends critically on which frames are selected from the input video, yet most systems rely on uniform or heuristic sampling that cannot be optimized for downstream answering quality. We introduce \textbf{HORNet}, a lightweight frame selection policy trained with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to learn which frames a frozen VLM needs to answer questions correctly. With fewer than 1M trainable parameters, HORNet reduces input frames by up to 99\% and VLM processing time by up to 93\%, while improving answer quality on short-form benchmarks (+1.7\% F1 on MSVD-QA) and achieving strong performance on temporal reasoning tasks (+7.3 points over uniform sampling on NExT-QA). We formalize this as Select Any Frames (SAF), a task that decouples visual input curation from VLM reasoning, and show that GRPO-trained selection generalizes better out-of-distribution than supervised and PPO alternatives. HORNet's policy further transfers across VLM answerers without retraining, yielding an additional 8.5\% relative gain when paired with a stronger model. Evaluated across six benchmarks spanning 341,877 QA pairs and 114.2 hours of video, our results demonstrate that optimizing \emph{what} a VLM sees is a practical and complementary alternative to optimizing what it generates while improving efficiency. Code is available at https://github.com/ostadabbas/HORNet.

item.title: CRAFT: Aligning Diffusion Models with Fine-Tuning Is Easier Than You Think
item.content:encoded: Aligning Diffusion models has achieved remarkable breakthroughs in generating high-quality, human preference-aligned images. Existing techniques, such as supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and DPO-style preference optimization, have become principled tools for fine-tuning diffusion models. However, SFT relies on high-quality images that are costly to obtain, while DPO-style methods depend on large-scale preference datasets, which are often inconsistent in quality. Beyond data dependency, these methods are further constrained by computational inefficiency. To address these two challenges, we propose Composite Reward Assisted Fine-Tuning (CRAFT), a lightweight yet powerful fine-tuning paradigm that requires significantly reduced training data while maintaining computational efficiency. It first leverages a Composite Reward Filtering (CRF) technique to construct a high-quality and consistent training dataset and then perform an enhanced variant of SFT. We also theoretically prove that CRAFT actually optimizes the lower bound of group-based reinforcement learning, establishing a principled connection between SFT with selected data and reinforcement learning. Our extensive empirical results demonstrate that CRAFT with only 100 samples can easily outperform recent SOTA preference optimization methods with thousands of preference-paired samples. Moreover, CRAFT can even achieve 11-220$\times$ faster convergences than the baseline preference optimization methods, highlighting its extremely high efficiency.

item.title: Unmasking Algorithmic Bias in Predictive Policing: A GAN-Based Simulation Framework with Multi-City Temporal Analysis
item.content:encoded: Predictive policing systems that direct patrol resources based on algorithmically generated crime forecasts have been widely deployed across US cities, yet their tendency to encode and amplify racial disparities remains poorly understood in quantitative terms. We present a reproducible simulation framework that couples a Generative Adversarial Network GAN with a Noisy OR patrol detection model to measure how racial bias propagates through the full enforcement pipeline from crime occurrence to police contact. Using 145000 plus Part 1 crime records from Baltimore 2017 to 2019 and 233000 plus records from Chicago 2022, augmented with US Census ACS demographic data, we compute four monthly bias metrics across 264 city year mode observations: the Disparate Impact Ratio DIR, Demographic Parity Gap, Gini Coefficient, and a composite Bias Amplification Score. Our experiments reveal extreme and year variant bias in Baltimores detected mode, with mean annual DIR up to 15714 in 2019, moderate under detection of Black residents in Chicago DIR equals 0.22, and persistent Gini coefficients of 0.43 to 0.62 across all conditions. We further demonstrate that a Conditional Tabular GAN CTGAN debiasing approach partially redistributes detection rates but cannot eliminate structural disparity without accompanying policy intervention. Socioeconomic regression analysis confirms strong correlations between neighborhood racial composition and detection likelihood Pearson r equals 0.83 for percent White and r equals negative 0.81 for percent Black. A sensitivity analysis over patrol radius, officer count, and citizen reporting probability reveals that outcomes are most sensitive to officer deployment levels. The code and data are publicly available at this repository.

item.title: Computational and Statistical Hardness of Calibration Distance
item.content:encoded: The distance from calibration, introduced by Błasiok, Gopalan, Hu, and Nakkiran (STOC 2023), has recently emerged as a central measure of miscalibration for probabilistic predictors. We study the fundamental problems of computing and estimating this quantity, given either an exact description of the data distribution or only sample access to it. We give an efficient algorithm that exactly computes the calibration distance when the distribution has a uniform marginal and noiseless labels, which improves the $O(1/\sqrt{|\mathcal{X}|})$ additive approximation of Qiao and Zheng (COLT 2024) for this special case. Perhaps surprisingly, the problem becomes $\mathsf{NP}$-hard when either of the two assumptions is removed. We extend our algorithm to a polynomial-time approximation scheme for the general case. For the estimation problem, we show that $Θ(1/ε^3)$ samples are sufficient and necessary for the empirical calibration distance to be upper bounded by the true distance plus $ε$. In contrast, a polynomial dependence on the domain size -- incurred by the learning-based baseline -- is unavoidable for two-sided estimation. Our positive results are based on simple sparsifications of both the distribution and the target predictor, which significantly reduce the search space for computation and lead to stronger concentration for the estimation problem. To prove the hardness results, we introduce new techniques for certifying lower bounds on the calibration distance -- a problem that is hard in general due to its $\textsf{co-NP}$-completeness.

item.title: Cross-Modal Rationale Transfer for Explainable Humanitarian Classification on Social Media
item.content:encoded: Advances in social media data dissemination enable the provision of real-time information during a crisis. The information comes from different classes, such as infrastructure damages, persons missing or stranded in the affected zone, etc. Existing methods attempted to classify text and images into various humanitarian categories, but their decision-making process remains largely opaque, which affects their deployment in real-life applications. Recent work has sought to improve transparency by extracting textual rationales from tweets to explain predicted classes. However, such explainable classification methods have mostly focused on text, rather than crisis-related images. In this paper, we propose an interpretable-by-design multimodal classification framework. Our method first learns the joint representation of text and image using a visual language transformer model and extracts text rationales. Next, it extracts the image rationales via the mapping with text rationales. Our approach demonstrates how to learn rationales in one modality from another through cross-modal rationale transfer, which saves annotation effort. Finally, tweets are classified based on extracted rationales. Experiments are conducted over CrisisMMD benchmark dataset, and results show that our proposed method boosts the classification Macro-F1 by 2-35% while extracting accurate text tokens and image patches as rationales. Human evaluation also supports the claim that our proposed method is able to retrieve better image rationale patches (12%) that help to identify humanitarian classes. Our method adapts well to new, unseen datasets in zero-shot mode, achieving an accuracy of 80%.

item.title: Generalized Hand-Object Pose Estimation with Occlusion Awareness
item.content:encoded: Generalized 3D hand-object pose estimation from a single RGB image remains challenging due to the large variations in object appearances and interaction patterns, especially under heavy occlusion. We propose GenHOI, a framework for generalized hand-object pose estimation with occlusion awareness. GenHOI integrates hierarchical semantic knowledge with hand priors to enhance model generalization under challenging occlusion conditions. Specifically, we introduce a hierarchical semantic prompt that encodes object states, hand configurations, and interaction patterns via textual descriptions. This enables the model to learn abstract high-level representations of hand-object interactions for generalization to unseen objects and novel interactions while compensating for missing or ambiguous visual cues. To enable robust occlusion reasoning, we adopt a multi-modal masked modeling strategy over RGB images, predicted point clouds, and textual descriptions. Moreover, we leverage hand priors as stable spatial references to extract implicit interaction constraints. This allows reliable pose inference even under significant variations in object shapes and interaction patterns. Extensive experiments on the challenging DexYCB and HO3Dv2 benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in hand-object pose estimation.

item.title: Foundations and Architectures of Artificial Intelligence for Motor Insurance
item.content:encoded: This handbook presents a systematic treatment of the foundations and architectures of artificial intelligence for motor insurance, grounded in large-scale real-world deployment. It formalizes a vertically integrated AI paradigm that unifies perception, multimodal reasoning, and production infrastructure into a cohesive intelligence stack for automotive risk assessment and claims processing. At its core, the handbook develops domain-adapted transformer architectures for structured visual understanding, relational vehicle representation learning, and multimodal document intelligence, enabling end-to-end automation of vehicle damage analysis, claims evaluation, and underwriting workflows. These components are composed into a scalable pipeline operating under practical constraints observed in nationwide motor insurance systems in Thailand. Beyond model design, the handbook emphasizes the co-evolution of learning algorithms and MLOps practices, establishing a principled framework for translating modern artificial intelligence into reliable, production-grade systems in high-stakes industrial environments.

item.title: Transformers Learn Robust In-Context Regression under Distributional Uncertainty
item.content:encoded: Recent work has shown that Transformers can perform in-context learning for linear regression under restrictive assumptions, including i.i.d. data, Gaussian noise, and Gaussian regression coefficients. However, real-world data often violate these assumptions: the distributions of inputs, noise, and coefficients are typically unknown, non-Gaussian, and may exhibit dependency across the prompt. This raises a fundamental question: can Transformers learn effectively in-context under realistic distributional uncertainty? We study in-context learning for noisy linear regression under a broad range of distributional shifts, including non-Gaussian coefficients, heavy-tailed noise, and non-i.i.d. prompts. We compare Transformers against classical baselines that are optimal or suboptimal under the corresponding maximum-likelihood criteria. Across all settings, Transformers consistently match or outperform these baselines, demonstrating robust in-context adaptation beyond classical estimators.

item.title: FASTER: Rethinking Real-Time Flow VLAs
item.content:encoded: Real-time execution is crucial for deploying Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models in the physical world. Existing asynchronous inference methods primarily optimize trajectory smoothness, but neglect the critical latency in reacting to environmental changes. By rethinking the notion of reaction in action chunking policies, this paper presents a systematic analysis of the factors governing reaction time. We show that reaction time follows a uniform distribution determined jointly by the Time to First Action (TTFA) and the execution horizon. Moreover, we reveal that the standard practice of applying a constant schedule in flow-based VLAs can be inefficient and forces the system to complete all sampling steps before any movement can start, forming the bottleneck in reaction latency. To overcome this issue, we propose Fast Action Sampling for ImmediaTE Reaction (FASTER). By introducing a Horizon-Aware Schedule, FASTER adaptively prioritizes near-term actions during flow sampling, compressing the denoising of the immediate reaction by tenfold (e.g., in $π_{0.5}$ and X-VLA) into a single step, while preserving the quality of long-horizon trajectory. Coupled with a streaming client-server pipeline, FASTER substantially reduces the effective reaction latency on real robots, especially when deployed on consumer-grade GPUs. Real-world experiments, including a highly dynamic table tennis task, prove that FASTER unlocks unprecedented real-time responsiveness for generalist policies, enabling rapid generation of accurate and smooth trajectories.

item.title: 基于无监督的大型语言模型功能模块发现方法
item.content:encoded:
提出ULCMOD框架，实现LLM功能模块的无监督发现与语义解析。



摘要： 本文研究了大型语言模型（LLM）内部功能模块的组织方式，提出了一个无监督的跨层模块发现框架ULCMOD。该框架通过引入新的目标函数和迭代解耦算法，能够同时将LLM中的神经元划分为功能模块，并识别与这些模块相关的输入样本主题。实验表明，该方法能发现高质量、解耦的功能模块，捕捉更丰富的语义信息，并在多个下游任务中表现优异。定性分析还显示，这些模块具有语义一致性、可解释性和清晰的空间层次结构，为LLM的可解释性研究提供了新工具。

item.title: L2A：提升大模型长序列处理能力的注意力机制
item.content:encoded:
L2A通过条件性全局注意力提升模型长序列处理能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为L2A的注意力机制，能够根据需要选择性地调用全局注意力，从而在不牺牲性能的前提下显著延长模型的有效上下文长度。实验表明，L2A使Qwen 2.5和Qwen 3模型的有效上下文从32K扩展到128K，同时减少了约80%的全局注意力计算。此外，L2A还支持后训练剪枝，可减少高达50%的KV缓存内存消耗，且性能损失极小。研究团队还设计了高效的Triton内核以优化GPU上的实现，提升了训练吞吐量和首次生成时间。

item.title: YOLO训练优化：基于反遗忘采样策略的高效训练方法
item.content:encoded:
提出AFSS策略提升YOLO训练效率，减少冗余图像处理。



摘要： 本文针对YOLO检测器训练耗时问题，提出一种反遗忘采样策略（AFSS）。该策略通过评估每张训练图像的学习充分性，将其分为易、中、难三类，并动态调整采样方式。简单图像稀疏采样以减少冗余，中等图像部分采样确保覆盖，复杂图像则全量采样。实验表明，AFSS在多个检测数据集上实现了超过1.43倍的训练加速，同时提升了检测精度。

item.title: 基于关键帧和文本的生成中间帧方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出新方法提升生成中间帧的一致性与语义准确性。



摘要： 本文研究了生成中间帧（GI）技术，旨在合成真实感的中间帧，而不仅仅是简单的插值。随着序列变稀疏、运动变化更大，现有GI模型在帧一致性、节奏稳定性和语义对齐方面面临挑战。为此，作者引入了关键帧锚定的注意力偏置（Keyframe-anchored Attention Bias）来提供语义和时间引导，并通过重新缩放的时间RoPE增强帧一致性。此外，文章还提出了TGI-Bench，首个专门用于文本条件GI评估的基准测试，支持针对性分析。无需额外训练，该方法在多种挑战下均实现了最先进的帧一致性、语义保真度和节奏稳定性。

item.title: 基于GSA与DPO的连贯故事生成框架
item.content:encoded:
提出新框架提升故事可视化的一致性与质量。



摘要： 本文针对故事可视化中角色身份和视觉风格不一致的问题，提出一个两阶段框架。首先引入Group-Shared Attention（GSA）机制，实现跨样本的信息共享，增强角色身份一致性。其次采用Direct Preference Optimization（DPO），通过学习人类偏好数据提升视觉质量和叙事一致性。实验表明，该方法在ViStoryBench基准上取得显著提升，角色身份得分提高10.0，风格一致性提高18.7。

item.title: 基于多模态思维链的图像检索方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出MCoT-MVS方法提升图像检索准确率。



摘要： 本文针对图像检索任务中因文本修改导致语义线索提取困难的问题，提出一种基于多模态思维链推理的多层级视觉选择方法（MCoT-MVS）。该方法利用多模态大语言模型进行推理，生成文本线索以指导视觉注意力模块提取参考图像中的关键语义。通过加权分层融合模块，将多粒度视觉特征与修改后的文本及目标描述对齐，实验证明该方法在CIRR和FashionIQ两个基准数据集上均取得最佳性能。

item.title: 基于视觉-语言基础模型的材料分类方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出新框架提升材料分类准确性与泛化能力。



摘要： 材料分类在计算机视觉和图形学中具有重要意义，但因标注数据稀缺而面临挑战。本文提出一种结合视觉-语言基础模型的新框架，通过图像生成与自动标注流程构建高质量训练数据，并引入先验知识优化模型性能。实验表明该方法在多个数据集上均取得显著提升，合成数据能有效反映真实材料特性，且模型泛化能力得到增强。

item.title: Enterprise AI中的治理记忆架构设计与验证
item.content:encoded:
提出治理记忆架构解决AI代理的内存与治理问题。



摘要： 本文探讨了企业AI中由于代理节点缺乏共享内存和统一治理而产生的五大结构性挑战，包括内存孤岛、治理碎片化、非结构化记忆不可用、冗余上下文传递以及质量下降无反馈。为解决这些问题，作者提出了“治理记忆”架构，通过四种机制实现：双模态内存模型、分层治理路由、反射边界检索和闭环模式生命周期。实验验证显示该架构在多个指标上表现优异，如99.6%的事实召回率、92%的治理路由精度等，并在LoCoMo基准测试中达到74.8%的整体准确率，证明其有效性。

item.title: 因果表示学习模型评估与数据集分析
item.content:encoded:
研究分析CRL模型评估方法与数据集局限性，提出综合评价指标。



摘要： 本文针对因果表示学习（CRL）模型的评估与数据集问题进行了深入分析。CRL模型旨在通过潜在空间转换实现对高维数据的干预和反事实生成。目前已有多种合成和真实数据集用于模型开发与评估，但各有优劣。文章指出，CRL模型需在重建、解耦、因果发现和反事实推理等多个方向上表现稳健，并强调了多方向评估对模型比较的复杂性。此外，研究还关注了可重复性问题，要求公开代码并保证结果一致性。作者提出了适用于CRL模型开发的数据集应具备的关键特征，并引入一个综合性能指标，以统一评估模型在不同方向的表现。最后，对现有文献中的实现进行了回顾与评估，指出了当前领域的差距与最佳实践。

item.title: FoMo-X：提升零样本异常检测可解释性的框架
item.content:encoded:
FoMo-X增强PFN的可解释性，实现高效诊断。



摘要： 本文提出FoMo-X框架，为基于Prior-Data Fitted Networks（PFNs）的零样本异常检测模型提供轻量级诊断能力。通过在预训练模型的嵌入层上添加辅助头部，FoMo-X能够有效捕捉和量化不确定性，并生成可解释的风险等级。该方法利用预先训练的模型结构，无需额外训练即可实现快速推理，且在多个基准测试中表现出色，显著提升了模型的可解释性和实用性。

item.title: 基于视频扩散变换器中激活信号的结构化引导方法
item.content:encoded:
研究视频扩散模型中的高幅激活，提出STAS提升视频生成质量。



摘要： 本文研究了视频扩散变换器中罕见的高幅隐藏状态激活（MAs），发现其在不同视觉标记中呈现层级结构。第一帧标记具有最大MA幅度，边界标记次之，内部标记相对较低。基于此，作者提出结构化激活引导（STAS）方法，在不增加计算开销的情况下提升视频质量和时间连贯性。

item.title: 基于符号回归的可解释异常检测方法SYRAN
item.content:encoded:
SYRAN通过符号回归学习可读方程实现异常检测。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为SYRAN的无监督异常检测方法，该方法基于符号回归技术，学习一组描述符号不变量的人类可读方程。这些方程在正常数据上近似恒定，偏离这些不变量会产生异常得分，从而实现可解释的异常检测。实验结果表明，SYRAN不仅具有高度可解释性，还能提供与已知科学或医学关系对应的方程，同时保持与最先进方法相当的异常检测性能。

item.title: 基于QIG的细粒度量化策略提升大视觉语言模型性能
item.content:encoded:
提出细粒度量化方法提升LVLM性能。



摘要： 本文针对大视觉语言模型（LVLM）在计算和内存上的高消耗问题，提出一种基于量化感知集成梯度（QIG）的细粒度量化策略。该方法通过集成梯度量化评估每个token的敏感性，将量化粒度从模态层面细化到token层面，从而更精确地衡量量化误差。实验表明，在多种LVLM模型和不同量化设置下，该方法显著提升了模型精度，同时保持低延迟。例如，在3位权重量化下，LLaVA-onevision-7B的平均准确率提高了1.60%，与全精度模型的差距缩小至1.33%。

item.title: SkeletonLLM：将骨骼数据转化为视觉模态的多模态大语言模型
item.content:encoded:
SkeletonLLM通过视觉转换实现骨骼数据的通用理解。



摘要： 本文提出SkeletonLLM，一种能够将任意骨骼序列转化为多模态大语言模型（MLLM）原生视觉模态的方法。其核心是DrAction，一个可微、格式无关的渲染器，能将骨骼运动转化为紧凑的图像序列。该方法支持端到端训练，使MLLM梯度直接指导渲染过程，提升任务相关视觉标记的质量。此外，引入了因果推理蒸馏和判别性微调策略，增强模型的推理能力。实验表明，SkeletonLLM在多种任务中表现出色，包括识别、描述生成、推理和跨格式迁移，为MLLM在非视觉模态中的应用提供了可行路径。

item.title: 基于深度学习的SLE患者气道体积差异研究
item.content:encoded:
深度学习技术揭示SLE-ILD患者气道体积显著扩大。



摘要： 本研究利用深度学习方法对系统性红斑狼疮（SLE）合并间质性肺病（ILD）与非ILD患者的高分辨率胸部CT图像进行分析，通过U-Net架构自动分割气道结构并测量其体积。结果显示，SLE-ILD患者在右上叶和左上叶的气道体积明显增大，部分段区如R1、R3和L3也存在显著差异，主要集中在上肺区域。研究证明该AI方法能有效量化气道体积变化，揭示SLE-ILD特有的解剖特征，提示气道结构改变可能作为疾病标志物，有助于早期诊断与个体化管理。

item.title: GuidedSAC：基于大语言模型的强化学习算法
item.content:encoded:
GuidedSAC利用大语言模型提升强化学习探索效率。



摘要： 本文提出GuidedSAC，一种结合大语言模型（LLM）作为智能监督者的强化学习算法，用于在大规模状态-动作空间中实现高效探索。LLM通过分析最近轨迹和视觉回放提供动作级指导，从而增强探索效果。理论分析表明，GuidedSAC在保持SAC收敛性的同时提高了收敛速度。实验结果表明，在离散和连续控制任务中，GuidedSAC在样本效率和最终性能方面均优于标准SAC及其他先进方法。

item.title: DSS-GAN：基于Mamba的噪声到图像生成方法
item.content:encoded:
DSS-GAN采用Mamba作为生成器，提升图像生成效果。



摘要： 本文提出DSS-GAN，这是首个将Mamba作为分层生成器骨干用于噪声到图像合成的生成对抗网络。核心贡献是方向潜在路由（DLR），一种新的条件机制，将潜在向量分解为特定方向的子向量，并与类别嵌入联合投影以产生特征上的仿射调制。相比传统全局信号注入方式，DLR在特征图的不同空间轴上结合类别身份和潜在结构，且在所有生成尺度上一致应用。实验表明，DSS-GAN在多个数据集上优于StyleGAN2-ADA，在FID、KID和精确率-召回率指标上表现更优。潜空间分析显示，方向子向量具有可测量的专业化特征，单个组件的扰动会产生结构化的方向相关变化。

item.title: Omni-3DEdit：一种统一的3D编辑学习模型
item.content:encoded:
Omni-3DEdit通过学习实现统一的3D编辑，提升效率。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Omni-3DEdit的统一学习模型，用于解决传统3D编辑方法依赖2D模型、任务定制化强且耗时的问题。该模型通过合成多视角数据集和引入双流LoRA模块，实现了对多种3D编辑任务的隐式泛化。与传统方法相比，Omni-3DEdit无需迭代优化，可在一次前向传播中完成任务，显著减少推理时间。实验表明其在效果和效率上均表现优异。

item.title: 基于高斯点云的动态场景重建与高效流式传输方法
item.content:encoded:
提出EGS方法提升动态场景重建效率与质量。



摘要： 本文针对动态场景重建中的计算资源分配问题，提出Efficient Gaussian Streaming (EGS) 方法，通过强化学习策略替代传统固定锚点选择方式，实现更高效的计算资源利用。EGS在保持原有高斯流式重建框架不变的前提下，根据场景复杂度动态调整锚点数量和选择，平衡了重建质量和运行效率。实验表明，在快速渲染和高质量优化两种模式下，EGS均优于传统FPS方法，在减少锚点数量的同时提升了PSNR并加快了运行速度。代码和预训练模型将在论文接受后发布。

item.title: 基于混序和部分有序有限状态自动机的重叠语音对齐与说话人归因方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种单次处理多说话人重叠语音的方法。



摘要： 本文提出利用混序和部分有序有限状态自动机（FSAs）对重叠语音进行对齐和说话人归因。通过在子词、词和短语层面的序列上进行边缘化，以总得分作为损失函数进行训练。为了减少图的规模，引入时间约束构建部分有序FSAs，并直接建模（标记，说话人）元组实现说话人归因。通过混序FSA的Viterbi对齐实现单次处理。实验在合成的LibriSpeech重叠数据集上进行，这是首个支持多说话人录音单次对齐的算法，所有算法均使用k2/Icefall实现。

item.title: Robust-ComBat在神经影像数据协调中的应用与优化
item.content:encoded:
Robust-ComBat有效解决神经影像数据中的病理异常干扰问题。



摘要： 本文探讨了传统数据协调方法ComBat在处理扩散MRI数据时的局限性，特别是在存在神经系统疾病患者数据的情况下。由于这些患者的扩散指标与健康对照组差异显著，导致站点效应估计失真。研究评估了多种异常值剔除方法，并发现许多策略在病理数据存在时效果不佳。相比之下，基于简单MLP的Robust-ComBat表现出更强的异常值补偿能力，能够在保留疾病相关信号的同时实现可靠的数据协调。实验结果表明，Robust-ComBat在多中心数据集上优于传统方法，具有更低的协调误差。

item.title: 视觉语言模型中的越狱行为分析与防御方法
item.content:encoded:
视觉语言模型越狱行为源于视觉模态的表征偏移，提出JRS-Rem防御方法。



摘要： 本文研究了大型视觉语言模型（VLMs）在整合视觉模态后安全对齐能力下降的问题。即使文本提示包含明显有害意图，添加图像也会显著提高越狱成功率。研究发现，VLMs能够区分良性输入和有害输入，并且在有害输入中，越狱样本与拒绝样本具有可分离的内部状态。这表明越狱并非由于无法识别有害意图，而是视觉模态将表征推向特定越狱状态，导致拒绝机制未能触发。作者定义了越狱相关偏移（JRS），并提出一种在推理阶段移除该偏移的防御方法（JRS-Rem），实验证明其在多个场景中有效提升安全性，同时保持良性任务性能。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的手术联合管理自动分诊系统研究
item.content:encoded:
AI辅助分诊系统可有效提升手术联合管理效率。



摘要： 本文研究了一种基于大语言模型（LLM）和电子健康记录（EHR）集成的手术联合管理（SCM）分诊工具——SCM Navigator。该工具通过术前文档、结构化数据和临床标准对患者进行分类，判断其是否适合SCM。研究结果显示，SCM Navigator具有高敏感性（0.94）和中等特异性（0.74），且大部分分诊差异源于临床标准或流程问题，而非AI错误。该系统展示了AI在自动化医疗流程中的潜力，有助于提升临床工作效率。

item.title: 基于权重相对排名的大型语言模型压缩与鲁棒性研究
item.content:encoded:
通过权重聚类实现LLM高效压缩，保持模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于权重相对排名的大型语言模型（LLM）压缩方法。研究表明，权重的相对大小关系比具体数值更重要，因此通过K均值聚类将权重矩阵替换为少量共享值，可大幅减少存储需求而不影响模型性能。实验显示，在Llama 3.1-8B-Instruct和SmolLM2-135M上，仅用16-64个不同值即可保持强准确性。进一步优化仅调整聚类中心可恢复部分性能损失。研究还发现，打乱权重相对排名会显著降低模型质量，而保持排名的随机化影响较小。此外，引入仿射变换可有效延缓尺度漂移问题。该方法为模型压缩与鲁棒性提供了新视角。

GigaWorld-Policy提升机器人策略学习效率与成功率。



摘要： 本文提出GigaWorld-Policy，一种以动作为中心的世界-行动模型（WAM），旨在解决现有WAM在推理效率和动作预测准确性方面的瓶颈。该模型通过同时预测未来动作序列和生成视频，提供更丰富的学习信号，并通过因果设计确保动作预测的高效性。实验表明，GigaWorld-Policy在真实机器人平台上运行速度比基准模型快9倍，任务成功率提高7%，并在RoboTwin 2.0上性能提升95%。

item.title: GenAI在数字设计教育中的应用与实践
item.content:encoded:
GenAI助力数字设计教育，提供可运行的课程资源。



摘要： GenAI Units In Digital Design Education (GUIDE) 是一个开放课程资源库，包含可运行的 Google Colab 实验和教学材料。其架构基于标准化教学单元，包括幻灯片、短视频、可运行实验和相关论文，确保学生学习体验一致性和教师重复使用与评分的便利性。文章展示了三个代表性单元：VeriThoughts 用于推理和形式验证支持的 RTL 生成，增强型 LLM 辅助测试平台生成，以及 LLMPirate 用于 IP 窃取检测。此外，还提供了四个课程实例，如 GUIDE4HardwareSecurity，包含基于 LLM 的硬件木马插入项目，并已在课堂和 CSAW 竞赛中成功部署。同时，还举办了 NYU Cognichip Hackathon，促进国际团队参与 AI 辅助 RTL 设计。

item.title: 基于自适应反馈的雷达数据压缩方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种自适应雷达数据压缩方法，实现100倍特征尺寸缩减。



摘要： 本文针对自动驾驶系统中高维雷达数据带来的通信带宽压力，提出了一种基于自适应反馈的雷达数据压缩方法。该方法通过梯度下降动态调整压缩率，并利用零阶梯度近似处理非可微操作，避免传输梯度张量。同时，通过对雷达数据立方体进行离散余弦变换并选择性剪枝，有效保留关键频率成分。结合缩放量化技术，实现了特征尺寸减少超过100倍，性能损失小于1%。实验验证表明该方法在多个雷达数据集上均具有良好的效果。

item.content:encoded:
DeepCORO-CLIP在冠状动脉造影分析中表现出色，提升诊断准确性。

item.title: 基于语义层次的3D形状令牌化方法LoST
item.content:encoded:
LoST通过语义显著性排序提升3D生成效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于语义层次的3D形状令牌化方法LoST，旨在优化自回归模型中的3D生成。传统方法依赖几何层次结构，但存在令牌效率低和语义不连贯的问题。LoST通过按语义显著性排序令牌，使早期前缀能够生成完整且具有主要语义的形状，后续令牌则细化细节。为训练LoST，作者引入了Relational Inter-Distance Alignment（RIDA）损失，将3D形状潜在空间与语义特征空间对齐。实验表明，LoST在几何和语义重建指标上均优于现有方法，且在使用更少令牌的情况下实现高质量的3D生成和下游任务。

item.title: rSDNet: Unified Robust Neural Learning against Label Noise and Adversarial Attacks
item.content:encoded: Neural networks are central to modern artificial intelligence, yet their training remains highly sensitive to data contamination. Standard neural classifiers are trained by minimizing the categorical cross-entropy loss, corresponding to maximum likelihood estimation under a multinomial model. While statistically efficient under ideal conditions, this approach is highly vulnerable to contaminated observations including label noises corrupting supervision in the output space, and adversarial perturbations inducing worst-case deviations in the input space. In this paper, we propose a unified and statistically grounded framework for robust neural classification that addresses both forms of contamination within a single learning objective. We formulate neural network training as a minimum-divergence estimation problem and introduce rSDNet, a robust learning algorithm based on the general class of $S$-divergences. The resulting training objective inherits robustness properties from classical statistical estimation, automatically down-weighting aberrant observations through model probabilities. We establish essential population-level properties of rSDNet, including Fisher consistency, classification calibration implying Bayes optimality, and robustness guarantees under uniform label noise and infinitesimal feature contamination. Experiments on three benchmark image classification datasets show that rSDNet improves robustness to label corruption and adversarial attacks while maintaining competitive accuracy on clean data, Our results highlight minimum-divergence learning as a principled and effective framework for robust neural classification under heterogeneous data contamination.

item.title: AERR-Nav: Adaptive Exploration-Recovery-Reminiscing Strategy for Zero-Shot Object Navigation
item.content:encoded: Zero-Shot Object Navigation (ZSON) in unknown multi-floor environments presents a significant challenge. Recent methods, mostly based on semantic value greedy waypoint selection, spatial topology-enhanced memory, and Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) as a decision-making framework, have led to improvements. However, these architectures struggle to balance exploration and exploitation for ZSON when encountering unseen environments, especially in multi-floor settings, such as robots getting stuck at narrow intersections, endlessly wandering, or failing to find stair entrances. To overcome these challenges, we propose AERR-Nav, a Zero-Shot Object Navigation framework that dynamically adjusts its state based on the robot's environment. Specifically, AERR-Nav has the following two key advantages: (1) An Adaptive Exploration-Recovery-Reminiscing Strategy, enables robots to dynamically transition between three states, facilitating specialized responses to diverse navigation scenarios. (2) An Adaptive Exploration State featuring Fast and Slow-Thinking modes helps robots better balance exploration, exploitation, and higher-level reasoning based on evolving environmental information. Extensive experiments on the HM3D and MP3D benchmarks demonstrate that our AERR-Nav achieves state-of-the-art performance among zero-shot methods. Comprehensive ablation studies further validate the efficacy of our proposed strategy and modules.

item.title: Temporal Gains, Spatial Costs: Revisiting Video Fine-Tuning in Multimodal Large Language Models
item.content:encoded: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are typically trained in multiple stages, with video-based supervised fine-tuning (Video-SFT) serving as a key step for improving visual understanding. Yet its effect on the fine-grained evolution of visual capabilities, particularly the balance between spatial and temporal understanding, remains poorly understood. In this paper, we systematically study how Video-SFT reshapes visual capabilities in MLLMs. Across architectures, parameter scales, and frame sampling settings, we observe a consistent pattern: Video-SFT reliably improves video performance, but often yields limited gains or even degradation on static image benchmarks. We further show that this trade-off is closely tied to temporal budget: increasing the number of sampled frames generally improves video performance, but does not reliably improve static image performance. Motivated by this finding, we study an instruction-aware Hybrid-Frame strategy that adaptively allocates frame counts and partially mitigates the image-video trade-off. Our results indicate that Video-SFT is not a free lunch for MLLMs, and preserving spatial understanding remains a central challenge in joint image-video training.

item.title: AgentFactory: A Self-Evolving Framework Through Executable Subagent Accumulation and Reuse
item.content:encoded: Building LLM-based agents has become increasingly important. Recent works on LLM-based agent self-evolution primarily record successful experiences as textual prompts or reflections, which cannot reliably guarantee efficient task re-execution in complex scenarios. We propose AgentFactory, a new self-evolution paradigm that preserves successful task solutions as executable subagent code rather than textual experience. Crucially, these subagents are continuously refined based on execution feedback, becoming increasingly robust and efficient as more tasks are encountered. Saved subagents are pure Python code with standardized documentation, enabling portability across any Python-capable system. We demonstrate that AgentFactory enables continuous capability accumulation: its library of executable subagents grows and improves over time, progressively reducing the effort required for similar tasks without manual intervention. Our implementation is open-sourced at https://github.com/zzatpku/AgentFactory, and our demonstration video is available at https://youtu.be/iKSsuAXJHW0.

item.title: PanoVGGT: Feed-Forward 3D Reconstruction from Panoramic Imagery
item.content:encoded: Panoramic imagery offers a full 360° field of view and is increasingly common in consumer devices. However, it introduces non-pinhole distortions that challenge joint pose estimation and 3D reconstruction. Existing feed-forward models, built for perspective cameras, generalize poorly to this setting. We propose PanoVGGT, a permutation-equivariant Transformer framework that jointly predicts camera poses, depth maps, and 3D point clouds from one or multiple panoramas in a single forward pass. The model incorporates spherical-aware positional embeddings and a panorama-specific three-axis SO(3) rotation augmentation, enabling effective geometric reasoning in the spherical domain. To resolve inherent global-frame ambiguity, we further introduce a stochastic anchoring strategy during training. In addition, we contribute PanoCity, a large-scale outdoor panoramic dataset with dense depth and 6-DoF pose annotations. Extensive experiments on PanoCity and standard benchmarks demonstrate that PanoVGGT achieves competitive accuracy, strong robustness, and improved cross-domain generalization. Code and dataset will be released.

item.title: SpiderCam: Low-Power Snapshot Depth from Differential Defocus
item.content:encoded: We introduce SpiderCam, an FPGA-based snapshot depth-from-defocus camera which produces 480x400 sparse depth maps in real-time at 32.5 FPS over a working range of 52 cm while consuming 624 mW of power in total. SpiderCam comprises a custom camera that simultaneously captures two differently focused images of the same scene, processed with a SystemVerilog implementation of depth from differential defocus (DfDD) on a low-power FPGA. To achieve state-of-the-art power consumption, we present algorithmic improvements to DfDD that overcome challenges caused by low-power sensors, and design a memory-local implementation for streaming depth computation on a device that is too small to store even a single image pair. We report the first sub-Watt total power measurement for passive FPGA-based 3D cameras in the literature.

item.title: TDAD: Test-Driven Agentic Development - Reducing Code Regressions in AI Coding Agents via Graph-Based Impact Analysis
item.content:encoded: AI coding agents can resolve real-world software issues, yet they frequently introduce regressions, breaking tests that previously passed. Current benchmarks focus almost exclusively on resolution rate, leaving regression behavior under-studied. This paper presents TDAD (Test-Driven Agentic Development), an open-source tool and benchmark methodology that combines abstract-syntax-tree (AST) based code-test graph construction with weighted impact analysis to surface the tests most likely affected by a proposed change. Evaluated on SWE-bench Verified with two local models (Qwen3-Coder 30B on 100 instances and Qwen3.5-35B-A3B on 25 instances), TDAD's GraphRAG workflow reduced test-level regressions by 70% (6.08% to 1.82%) and improved resolution from 24% to 32% when deployed as an agent skill. A surprising finding is that TDD prompting alone increased regressions (9.94%), revealing that smaller models benefit more from contextual information (which tests to verify) than from procedural instructions (how to do TDD). An autonomous auto-improvement loop raised resolution from 12% to 60% on a 10-instance subset with 0% regression. These findings suggest that for AI agent tool design, surfacing contextual information outperforms prescribing procedural workflows. All code, data, and logs are publicly available at https://github.com/pepealonso95/TDAD.

item.title: Facial Movement Dynamics Reveal Workload During Complex Multitasking
item.content:encoded: Real-time cognitive workload monitoring is crucial in safety-critical environments, yet established measures are intrusive, expensive, or lack temporal resolution. We tested whether facial movement dynamics from a standard webcam could provide a low-cost alternative. Seventy-two participants completed a multitasking simulation (OpenMATB) under varied load while facial keypoints were tracked via OpenPose. Linear kinematics (velocity, acceleration, displacement) and recurrence quantification features were extracted. Increasing load altered dynamics across timescales: movement magnitudes rose, temporal organisation fragmented then reorganised into complex patterns, and eye-head coordination weakened. Random forest classifiers trained on pose kinematics outperformed task performance metrics (85% vs. 55% accuracy) but generalised poorly across participants (43% vs. 33% chance). Participant-specific models reached 50% accuracy with minimal calibration (2 minutes per condition), improving continuously to 73% without plateau. Facial movement dynamics sensitively track workload with brief calibration, enabling adaptive interfaces using commodity cameras, though individual differences limit cross-participant generalisation.

item.title: Process Supervision for Chain-of-Thought Reasoning via Monte Carlo Net Information Gain
item.content:encoded: Multi-step reasoning improves the capabilities of large language models (LLMs) but increases the risk of errors propagating through intermediate steps. Process reward models (PRMs) mitigate this by scoring each step individually, enabling fine-grained supervision and improved reliability. Existing methods for training PRMs rely on costly human annotations or computationally intensive automatic labeling. We propose a novel approach to automatically generate step-level labels using Information Theory. Our method estimates how each reasoning step affects the likelihood of the correct answer, providing a signal of step quality. Importantly, it reduces computational complexity to $\mathcal{O}(N)$, improving over the previous $\mathcal{O}(N \log N)$ methods. We demonstrate that these labels enable effective chain-of-thought selection in best-of-$K$ evaluation settings across diverse reasoning benchmarks, including mathematics, Python programming, SQL, and scientific question answering. This work enables scalable and efficient supervision of LLM reasoning, particularly for tasks where error propagation is critical.

item.title: Towards Safer Large Reasoning Models by Promoting Safety Decision-Making before Chain-of-Thought Generation
item.content:encoded: Large reasoning models (LRMs) achieved remarkable performance via chain-of-thought (CoT), but recent studies showed that such enhanced reasoning capabilities are at the expense of significantly degraded safety capabilities. In this paper, we reveal that LRMs' safety degradation occurs only after CoT is enabled, and this degradation is not observed when CoT is disabled. This observation motivates us to consider encouraging LRMs to make safety decisions before CoT generation. To this end, we propose a novel safety alignment method that promotes the safety decision-making of LRMs before starting CoT generation. Specifically, we first utilize a Bert-based classifier to extract safety decision signals from a safe model (e.g., a CoT-disabled LRM) and then integrate these signals into LRMs' safety alignment as auxiliary supervision. In this way, the safety gradients can be backpropagated to the LRMs' latent representations, effectively strengthening the LRMs' safety decision-making abilities against CoT generation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method substantially improves the safety capabilities of LRMs while effectively maintaining LRMs' general reasoning performance.

item.title: A Proposal-Free Query-Guided Network for Grounded Multimodal Named Entity Recognition
item.content:encoded: Grounded Multimodal Named Entity Recognition (GMNER) identifies named entities, including their spans and types, in natural language text and grounds them to the corresponding regions in associated images. Most existing approaches split this task into two steps: they first detect objects using a pre-trained general-purpose detector and then match named entities to the detected objects. However, these methods face a major limitation. Because pre-trained general-purpose object detectors operate independently of textual entities, they tend to detect common objects and frequently overlook specific fine-grained regions required by named entities. This misalignment between object detectors and entities introduces imprecision and can impair overall system performance. In this paper, we propose a proposal-free Query-Guided Network (QGN) that unifies multimodal reasoning and decoding through text guidance and cross- modal interaction. QGN enables accurate grounding and robust performance in open-domain scenarios. Extensive experiments demonstrate that QGN achieves top performance among compared GMNER models on widely used benchmarks.

item.title: When Only the Final Text Survives: Implicit Execution Tracing for Multi-Agent Attribution
item.content:encoded: When a multi-agent system produces an incorrect or harmful answer, who is accountable if execution logs and agent identifiers are unavailable? Multi-agent language systems increasingly rely on structured interactions such as delegation and iterative refinement, yet the final output often obscures the underlying interaction topology and agent contributions. We introduce IET (Implicit Execution Tracing), a metadata-independent framework that enables token-level attribution directly from generated text and a simple mechanism for interaction topology reconstruction. During generation, agent-specific keyed signals are embedded into the token distribution, transforming the text into a self-describing execution trace detectable only with a secret key. At detection time, a transition-aware scoring method identifies agent handover points and reconstructs the interaction graph. Experiments show that IET recovers agent segments and coordination structure with high accuracy while preserving generation quality, enabling privacy-preserving auditing for multi-agent language systems.

item.title: Noise-Aware Misclassification Attack Detection in Collaborative DNN Inference
item.content:encoded: Collaborative inference of object classification Deep neural Networks (DNNs) where resource-constrained end-devices offload partially processed data to remote edge servers to complete end-to-end processing, is becoming a key enabler of edge-AI. However, such edge-offloading is vulnerable to malicious data injections leading to stealthy misclassifications that are tricky to detect, especially in the presence of environmental noise. In this paper, we propose a semi-gray-box and noise- aware anomaly detection framework fueled by a variational autoencoder (VAE) to capture deviations caused by adversarial manipulation. The proposed framework incorporates a robust noise-aware feature that captures the characteristic behavior of environmental noise to improve detection accuracy while reducing false alarm rates. Our evaluation with popular object classification DNNs demonstrate the robustness of the proposed detection (up to 90% AUROC across DNN configurations) under realistic noisy conditions while revealing limitations caused by feature similarity and elevated noise levels.

item.title: In Trust We Survive: Emergent Trust Learning
item.content:encoded: We introduce Emergent Trust Learning (ETL), a lightweight, trust-based control algorithm that can be plugged into existing AI agents. It enables these to reach cooperation in competitive game environments under shared resources. Each agent maintains a compact internal trust state, which modulates memory, exploration, and action selection. ETL requires only individual rewards and local observations and incurs negligible computational and communication overhead. We evaluate ETL in three environments: In a grid-based resource world, trust-based agents reduce conflicts and prevent long-term resource depletion while achieving competitive individual returns. In a hierarchical Tower environment with strong social dilemmas and randomised floor assignments, ETL sustains high survival rates and recovers cooperation even after extended phases of enforced greed. In the Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma, the algorithm generalises to a strategic meta-game, maintaining cooperation with reciprocal opponents while avoiding long-term exploitation by defectors. Code will be released upon publication.

item.title: On the Cone Effect and Modality Gap in Medical Vision-Language Embeddings
item.content:encoded: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) exhibit a characteristic "cone effect" in which nonlinear encoders map embeddings into highly concentrated regions of the representation space, contributing to cross-modal separation known as the modality gap. While this phenomenon has been widely observed, its practical impact on supervised multimodal learning -particularly in medical domains- remains unclear. In this work, we introduce a lightweight post-hoc mechanism that keeps pretrained VLM encoders frozen while continuously controlling cross-modal separation through a single hyperparameter {λ}. This enables systematic analysis of how the modality gap affects downstream multimodal performance without expensive retraining. We evaluate generalist (CLIP, SigLIP) and medically specialized (BioMedCLIP, MedSigLIP) models across diverse medical and natural datasets in a supervised multimodal settings. Results consistently show that reducing excessive modality gap improves downstream performance, with medical datasets exhibiting stronger sensitivity to gap modulation; however, fully collapsing the gap is not always optimal, and intermediate, task-dependent separation yields the best results. These findings position the modality gap as a tunable property of multimodal representations rather than a quantity that should be universally minimized.

item.title: scicode-lint: Detecting Methodology Bugs in Scientific Python Code with LLM-Generated Patterns
item.content:encoded: Methodology bugs in scientific Python code produce plausible but incorrect results that traditional linters and static analysis tools cannot detect. Several research groups have built ML-specific linters, demonstrating that detection is feasible. Yet these tools share a sustainability problem: dependency on specific pylint or Python versions, limited packaging, and reliance on manual engineering for every new pattern. As AI-generated code increases the volume of scientific software, the need for automated methodology checking (such as detecting data leakage, incorrect cross-validation, and missing random seeds) grows. We present scicode-lint, whose two-tier architecture separates pattern design (frontier models at build time) from execution (small local model at runtime). Patterns are generated, not hand-coded; adapting to new library versions costs tokens, not engineering hours. On Kaggle notebooks with human-labeled ground truth, preprocessing leakage detection reaches 65% precision at 100% recall; on 38 published scientific papers applying AI/ML, precision is 62% (LLM-judged) with substantial variation across pattern categories; on a held-out paper set, precision is 54%. On controlled tests, scicode-lint achieves 97.7% accuracy across 66 patterns.

item.title: Gesture-Aware Pretraining and Token Fusion for 3D Hand Pose Estimation
item.content:encoded: Estimating 3D hand pose from monocular RGB images is fundamental for applications in AR/VR, human-computer interaction, and sign language understanding. In this work we focus on a scenario where a discrete set of gesture labels is available and show that gesture semantics can serve as a powerful inductive bias for 3D pose estimation. We present a two-stage framework: gesture-aware pretraining that learns an informative embedding space using coarse and fine gesture labels from InterHand2.6M, followed by a per-joint token Transformer guided by gesture embeddings as intermediate representations for final regression of MANO hand parameters. Training is driven by a layered objective over parameters, joints, and structural constraints. Experiments on InterHand2.6M demonstrate that gesture-aware pretraining consistently improves single-hand accuracy over the state-of-the-art EANet baseline, and that the benefit transfers across architectures without any modification.

item.title: Objective Mispricing Detection for Shortlisting Undervalued Football Players via Market Dynamics and News Signals
item.content:encoded: We present a practical, reproducible framework for identifying undervalued football players grounded in objective mispricing. Instead of relying on subjective expert labels, we estimate an expected market value from structured data (historical market dynamics, biographical and contract features, transfer history) and compare it to the observed valuation to define mispricing. We then assess whether news-derived Natural Language Processing (NLP) features (i.e., sentiment statistics and semantic embeddings from football articles) complement market signals for shortlisting undervalued players. Using a chronological (leakage-aware) evaluation, gradient-boosted regression explains a large share of the variance in log-transformed market value. For undervaluation shortlisting, ROC-AUC-based ablations show that market dynamics are the primary signal, while NLP features provide consistent, secondary gains that improve robustness and interpretability. SHAP analyses suggest the dominance of market trends and age, with news-derived volatility cues amplifying signals in high-uncertainty regimes. The proposed pipeline is designed for decision support in scouting workflows, emphasizing ranking/shortlisting over hard classification thresholds, and includes a concise reproducibility and ethics statement.

item.title: Toward Phonology-Guided Sign Language Motion Generation: A Diffusion Baseline and Conditioning Analysis
item.content:encoded: Generating natural, correct, and visually smooth 3D avatar sign language motion conditioned on the text inputs continues to be very challenging. In this work, we train a generative model of 3D body motion and explore the role of phonological attribute conditioning for sign language motion generation, using ASL-LEX 2.0 annotations such as hand shape, hand location and movement. We first establish a strong diffusion baseline using an Human Motion MDM-style diffusion model with SMPL-X representation, which outperforms SignAvatar, a state-of-the-art CVAE method, on gloss discriminability metrics. We then systematically study the role of text conditioning using different text encoders (CLIP vs. T5), conditioning modes (gloss-only vs. gloss+phonological attributes), and attribute notation format (symbolic vs. natural language). Our analysis reveals that translating symbolic ASL-LEX notations to natural language is a necessary condition for effective CLIP-based attribute conditioning, while T5 is largely unaffected by this translation. Furthermore, our best-performing variant (CLIP with mapped attributes) outperforms SignAvatar across all metrics. These findings highlight input representation as a critical factor for text-encoder-based attribute conditioning, and motivate structured conditioning approaches where gloss and phonological attributes are encoded through independent pathways.

item.title: GMT: Goal-Conditioned Multimodal Transformer for 6-DOF Object Trajectory Synthesis in 3D Scenes
item.content:encoded: Synthesizing controllable 6-DOF object manipulation trajectories in 3D environments is essential for enabling robots to interact with complex scenes, yet remains challenging due to the need for accurate spatial reasoning, physical feasibility, and multimodal scene understanding. Existing approaches often rely on 2D or partial 3D representations, limiting their ability to capture full scene geometry and constraining trajectory precision. We present GMT, a multimodal transformer framework that generates realistic and goal-directed object trajectories by jointly leveraging 3D bounding box geometry, point cloud context, semantic object categories, and target end poses. The model represents trajectories as continuous 6-DOF pose sequences and employs a tailored conditioning strategy that fuses geometric, semantic, contextual, and goaloriented information. Extensive experiments on synthetic and real-world benchmarks demonstrate that GMT outperforms state-of-the-art human motion and human-object interaction baselines, such as CHOIS and GIMO, achieving substantial gains in spatial accuracy and orientation control. Our method establishes a new benchmark for learningbased manipulation planning and shows strong generalization to diverse objects and cluttered 3D environments. Project page: https://huajian- zeng.github. io/projects/gmt/.

item.title: 面向智能体工作流的高效LLM服务框架Helium
item.content:encoded:
Helium提升LLM智能体工作流效率1.56倍。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Helium的新型LLM服务框架，旨在优化智能体工作流中的冗余问题。传统LLM服务系统仅关注单次推理调用的优化，而忽略了跨调用的依赖关系。Helium从数据系统角度出发，将智能体工作流建模为查询计划，并将LLM调用作为第一类操作符进行处理。通过主动缓存和缓存感知调度，Helium最大化了提示词、KV状态和工作流之间的复用，实现了比现有系统更高的效率。实验表明，端到端的工作流优化对构建可扩展、高效的LLM智能体至关重要。

item.title: AI生成科学图像的学术规范与实践指南
item.content:encoded:
AI生成图像在科研中的应用与学术规范探讨



摘要： 随着生成式AI技术的发展，科学图像、图形摘要和数据可视化工具不断涌现。然而，学术出版机构对AI生成图像的政策不一，存在一致性不足的问题。本文调查了《自然》《科学》《细胞出版社》《爱思唯尔》和《PLOS》等主要期刊和出版商的相关立场，分析了其关注的核心问题，如可重复性、作者归属和视觉误导风险。结合SciDraw等实际工具案例，文章提出了研究人员在使用AI生成图像时应遵循的最佳实践指南。研究认为，在充分披露和质量控制的前提下，AI生成图像可以提升科学传播效率，同时不损害研究诚信。

item.title: 基于图的多智能体强化学习在无人机蜂群中的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出一种适用于部分可观测环境的无人机蜂群协作框架。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于图的多智能体强化学习框架，用于无人机蜂群在部分可观测和间歇性通信条件下的任务执行。该框架采用集中训练、分散执行（CTDE）模式，在训练阶段使用全局状态和集中式评论家，而在执行阶段每个无人机仅依赖局部观测和邻近节点信息进行决策。通过引入代理-实体注意力模块和距离限制的通信图进行消息聚合，该方法在协作中继部署任务（DroneConnect）和对抗性任务（DroneCombat）中均表现出色，实现了高覆盖率和良好的泛化能力。

item.title: 基于GATS框架的4D点云视频理解方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出GATS框架提升4D点云视频理解性能。



摘要： 本文针对4D点云视频理解中的时间尺度偏差和分布不确定性问题，提出了一种新的双不变框架Gaussian Aware Temporal Scaling (GATS)。该框架包含Uncertainty Guided Gaussian Convolution (UGGC)和Temporal Scaling Attention (TSA)两个模块，分别解决密度变化、噪声和遮挡下的邻域聚合问题以及不同帧率下的时间距离归一化问题。实验表明，该方法在MSR-Action3D、NTU RGBD和Synthia4D等多个基准数据集上均取得显著提升，具有更高的准确性和鲁棒性。

item.title: Interact3D：生成物理合理交互的3D组合对象的新框架
item.content:encoded:
Interact3D解决3D组合物体生成中的几何细节和空间关系问题。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Interact3D的新框架，用于从单张图像生成具有物理合理交互的3D组合对象。该方法通过高质量的个体资产生成和稳健的两阶段组合流程，有效解决了隐藏区域几何细节丢失和物体间空间关系不一致的问题。首先利用先进的生成先验构建统一的3D引导场景，随后通过基于SDF的优化和闭环自修正策略减少碰撞，提升几何保真度与空间一致性。实验表明，Interact3D在碰撞感知的3D组合生成方面表现优异。

item.title: 基于约束马尔可夫决策过程的分层指令遵循方法
item.content:encoded:
HIPO通过约束强化学习提升大模型对分层指令的遵守与用户满意度。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为HIPO的新对齐框架，将分层指令遵循问题建模为约束马尔可夫决策过程。该方法将系统提示从输入上下文提升为严格的算法边界，并通过原始-对偶安全强化学习动态强制执行系统提示合规性，从而在可行区域内最大化用户效用。实验表明，HIPO在多种模型架构中显著提升了系统合规性和用户满意度。机制分析显示，该方法促使模型关注长距离系统标记，为复杂工作流中的可靠大语言模型部署提供了理论基础。

item.title: ViT-AdaLA：高效适配线性注意力视觉模型的框架
item.content:encoded:
ViT-AdaLA提升线性注意力视觉模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出ViT-AdaLA，一种用于将视觉基础模型的知识有效迁移至线性注意力视觉Transformer的框架。该框架包含三个阶段：注意力对齐、特征对齐和监督微调。通过在每个块中对齐线性注意力与原始softmax注意力，减少误差累积，并通过微调使最终特征与冻结的softmax VFM教师对齐，从而提升下游任务表现。实验表明ViT-AdaLA在分类和分割任务中优于现有线性注意力方法。

item.title: 基于LeJEPA的EEG基础模型Laya提升脑电表征学习
item.content:encoded:
Laya通过潜变量预测提升EEG表征性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于LeJEPA的新型EEG基础模型Laya，旨在解决传统基于信号重建的自监督学习方法在脑电表征学习中的局限性。研究发现，现有方法在下游任务中表现有限，且对微调策略敏感。Laya采用潜变量预测机制，而非直接重建原始信号，从而更关注任务相关的神经结构。实验表明，在多个EEG基准测试中，Laya在线性探测任务中优于基于重建的基线模型，证明了潜变量预测目标在学习可迁移、高层次EEG表征方面的潜力。

item.title: HGFNet：一种高效且鲁棒的高光谱图像分类方法
item.content:encoded:
HGFNet通过融合3D卷积与频域滤波提升高光谱图像分类性能。



摘要： 本文提出HGFNet，一种结合3D卷积和频域全局过滤的高光谱图像分类架构。该方法引入三种针对高光谱数据的频域变换，以捕捉丰富的空间-光谱特征。3D卷积层提取局部结构，而傅里叶变换模块则建模长距离依赖关系并抑制噪声。此外，HGFNet采用自适应焦点损失（AFL）缓解类别不平衡问题，提升对少数类的识别能力。

item.title: 基于云边协同的人形机器人语言控制框架ECHO
item.content:encoded:
ECHO实现人形机器人高效语言驱动的全身控制。



摘要： 本文提出ECHO，一个用于人形机器人语言驱动全身控制的云边框架。云端模块通过扩散模型将自然语言指令转化为运动参考轨迹，边缘端模块则通过强化学习执行这些轨迹。两者通过38维运动表示连接，包含关节角度、根部速度、高度和6D方向，无需重新映射人体模型，直接兼容低级PD控制。生成器采用1D卷积UNet结合CLIP文本特征，推理时在云端GPU上快速生成动作序列。追踪器采用教师-学生范式，通过证据适应模块提升仿真到现实的迁移能力，并结合形态对称约束和领域随机化增强鲁棒性。系统还包含基于IMU的跌倒检测与恢复机制。ECHO在HumanML3D基准上表现优异，真实世界实验验证了其稳定性与多样性。

item.title: 基于几何的视频扩散模型增强方法
item.content:encoded:
提出几何奖励模型提升视频生成一致性与鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文针对视频扩散模型在训练过程中缺乏显式几何监督导致的物体变形、空间漂移和深度违规等问题，提出一种基于几何的奖励模型。该模型利用预训练的几何基础模型，通过跨帧重投影误差评估多视角一致性，并在点对点层面计算误差，提高物理合理性和鲁棒性。此外，还引入几何感知采样策略，聚焦于具有可靠对应关系的几何有意义区域。该方法通过后训练双向模型或推理时优化因果视频模型，有效提升视频生成质量，且无需大量重新训练资源。

item.title: 面向物联网的双阶段意图感知框架提升LLM交互可靠性
item.content:encoded:
提出DS-IA框架提升LLM在物联网中的交互效率与可靠性。



摘要： 随着大型语言模型（LLMs）从信息提供者转变为物联网中的实体代理，其可靠性和交互效率面临挑战。本文提出双阶段意图感知（DS-IA）框架，通过分离高层用户意图理解和低层物理执行，有效减少无效指令和实体幻觉问题。该框架在HomeBench和SAGE基准测试中表现出色，精确匹配率高达58.56%，并显著提高无效指令的拒绝率至87.04%。此外，DS-IA在SAGE基准上成功解决交互频率困境，提升自主成功率至71.43%，同时保持对必须人工澄清的模糊性的高精度识别，从而减少用户干扰。

item.title: 基于Aitchison几何的多类分类高斯过程模型
item.content:encoded:
提出一种结合Aitchison几何的多类分类高斯过程模型。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于Aitchison几何的共轭且校准的高斯过程（GP）模型，用于多类分类任务。该方法通过将概率单形中的类别概率映射到无约束的欧几里得空间，将分类问题转化为具有更少潜在维度的GP回归问题。这种方法实现了共轭推理和可靠的预测概率，而无需依赖模型构建中的分布近似。该方法兼容标准的稀疏GP回归技术，能够在更大数据集上实现可扩展的推理。实验结果表明，该方法在合成和真实数据集上均表现出良好的校准性和竞争力。

item.title: 基于多视角基础模型的单目SLAM系统M^3
item.content:encoded:
M^3提升单目视频重建精度与稳定性。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为M^3的新型单目SLAM系统，通过将多视角基础模型与SLAM框架结合，解决动态环境中高精度位姿估计和计算效率的问题。M^3引入专用匹配头以生成细粒度密集对应关系，并集成到鲁棒的单目高斯泼溅SLAM中。同时，通过动态区域抑制和交叉推理内在对齐提升跟踪稳定性。实验表明，M^3在多个室内和室外基准数据集上表现优异，相比VGGT-SLAM 2.0将ATE RMSE降低64.3%，在ScanNet++数据集上PSNR指标优于ARTDECO 2.11 dB。

item.title: 流形对齐的自编码器正则化方法研究
item.content:encoded:
流形对齐自编码器在保持邻近距离方面优于其他方法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种简单的无监督正则化方法——流形对齐自编码器（MMAE），通过最小化潜在空间与输入空间中成对距离的均方误差来实现对齐。这种方法不依赖坐标对齐，而是基于距离对齐，因此可以扩展到低维表示，提升了方法的灵活性。实验表明，MMAE在保留最近邻距离和基于持久同调的度量方面优于其他类似方法，并能提供多维标度（MDS）的可扩展近似。

item.title: 评估大语言模型在不同语境中应用用户偏好的能力
item.content:encoded:
研究分析大语言模型在不同场景下应用用户偏好时的适应性问题。



摘要： 本文提出BenchPreS，用于评估大语言模型在不同通信情境中是否恰当应用或抑制用户偏好。通过Misapplication Rate（MR）和Appropriate Application Rate（AAR）两个指标发现，即使是先进的大语言模型也难以根据语境合理应用偏好。偏好遵循性强的模型表现出更高的过度应用率，而推理能力和提示防御措施并不能完全解决这一问题。研究结果表明，当前大语言模型将个性化偏好视为全局规则，而非依赖语境的规范信号。

item.title: 基于多专家架构的医学图像检索方法HMAR
item.content:encoded:
HMAR提升医学图像检索精度，优于现有方法。



摘要： 医学图像检索（MIR）在辅助诊断中至关重要，但现有系统存在特征编码单一、相似性度量模糊和缺乏细粒度区域检索等缺陷。本文提出HMAR框架，采用多专家（MoE）架构，结合全局特征提取与局部病变区域识别，通过两阶段对比学习和滑动窗口匹配实现高效检索。使用KAN层生成哈希码以支持快速汉明距离搜索。实验表明，HMAR在RadioImageNet-CT数据集上分别达到0.711和0.724的mAP，优于ACIR方法。

item.title: DualPrim：一种结构化3D重建框架
item.content:encoded:
DualPrim通过正负超二次曲面实现高效结构化3D重建。



摘要： 文章介绍了一种名为DualPrim的3D重建框架，旨在解决传统神经重构方法在结构性和保真度之间的权衡问题。DualPrim采用正负超二次曲面表示形状，其中正超二次曲面构建基础结构，负超二次曲面通过可微运算符进行局部体积雕刻，从而实现拓扑感知的孔洞和凹陷建模。该设计在保持紧凑性和可微性的同时提升了表达能力，并集成到体素可微渲染器中，支持端到端学习和无缝网格导出。实验表明，DualPrim在精度和输出结构性方面优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于深度学习的Doherty功率放大器逆向设计方法
item.content:encoded:
深度学习用于Doherty功放逆向设计，提升效率与性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于深度学习的Doherty功率放大器逆向设计方法，采用深度卷积神经网络作为电磁场仿真代理模型，快速预测像素化输出合成器的S参数。结合黑盒Doherty框架和遗传算法优化器，成功设计出具有扩展回退效率范围的复杂Doherty合成器。实验中，两个三端口像素化合成器原型在2.75 GHz频率下实现超过74%的漏极效率和44.1 dBm输出功率，并在9 dB回退功率下保持52%以上的效率。使用5G NR波形测试后，经数字预失真处理，平均功率附加效率超过51%，邻道泄漏比优于-60.8 dBc。

item.title: 神经网络在组合泛化中的失败与 functorial 解码器的构建
item.content:encoded:
神经网络无法有效进行组合泛化，而 functorial 解码器可提升性能。



摘要： 本文探讨了神经网络在面对新组合的已知部分时表现不佳的问题，并指出这一失败源于架构缺陷。研究提出，组合泛化等价于解码器的 functorial 性，通过将高阶归纳类型（HIT）规范编译为神经架构，利用单子函子将目标空间的路径群胚映射到参数映射类别，实现了结构化的组合方式。实验表明，基于结构拼接的 functorial 解码器在多个空间中均优于非 functorial 方法，尤其是在复杂拓扑结构中表现显著提升。

item.title: 基于视频扩散Transformer的3D材质生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于视频扩散Transformer的3D材质生成方法。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于视频扩散Transformer架构的3D形状物理材质生成方法。该方法通过输入几何形状和文本描述，联合建模多种材质属性（如基础颜色、粗糙度、金属度和高度图），以生成物理上合理的材质。研究还引入了一个自定义的变分自编码器，将多种材质模态编码到紧凑的潜在空间中，从而在不增加标记数量的情况下实现多模态联合生成。该流程能够根据文本提示生成高质量的3D材质，并兼容常见的内容创作工具。

item.title: 比特币交易网络中欧几里得与双曲图神经网络的比较研究
item.content:encoded:
对比分析欧几里得与双曲GNN在比特币交易网络中的表现。



摘要： 本文研究了在大规模比特币交易图中，欧几里得空间和切空间双曲图神经网络（GNN）在节点分类任务中的表现。通过固定模型架构和维度，仅改变邻域范围，分析两种嵌入空间的差异。研究还探讨了学习率和曲率联合选择对高维双曲嵌入稳定性的影响，为构建大规模交易网络提供了关于嵌入几何和邻域深度的实用见解。

item.title: 基于事件视觉传感器的自监督学习框架 SpikeCLR
item.content:encoded:
SpikeCLR 提升 SNN 在无标签数据下的视觉表征能力。



摘要： 本文提出 SpikeCLR，一种用于事件视觉传感器的对比自监督学习框架，旨在解决 SNN 在无标签数据下训练困难的问题。通过引入空间、时间和极性变换等事件特定增强方法，并结合代理梯度训练，SpikeCLR 能够在 CIFAR10-DVS、N-Caltech101 等多个基准上实现优于监督学习的效果，尤其在小样本和半监督场景中表现突出。实验表明，结合空间与时间增强对学习有效的时空不变性至关重要，且模型表征具有跨数据集迁移能力。

item.title: SegviGen：利用3D生成模型进行3D部件分割的新框架
item.content:encoded:
SegviGen通过颜色化实现高效3D部件分割，性能优于现有方法。



摘要： SegviGen是一种新框架，将预训练的3D生成模型用于3D部件分割任务。与传统方法相比，它避免了跨视图不一致和边界模糊的问题，并减少了对大规模标注数据的依赖。该方法通过在几何对齐的重建中预测部件指示性颜色，实现了交互式、全量及带2D引导的分割。实验表明，SegviGen在交互式分割上比现有最佳方法提升40%，在全分割上提升15%，且仅需0.32%的标注数据。这证明了预训练3D生成先验在3D部件分割中的有效性。

item.title: Parallel-ICL：提升多模态上下文学习效率的算法
item.content:encoded:
提出Parallel-ICL算法，提升多模态任务推理速度。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Parallel-ICL的多模态上下文学习算法，旨在解决传统方法在增加演示示例时导致的推理延迟问题。该方法通过将长上下文分割为多个较短块并行处理，再通过加权专家集成方式融合结果，从而在保持性能的同时显著提升推理速度。实验表明，Parallel-ICL在VQA、图像描述和分类任务中表现接近全上下文方法，但计算效率更高，有效平衡了准确性与效率之间的矛盾。

item.title: LICA数据集：大规模多层图形设计标注推动视觉语言模型研究
item.content:encoded:
LICA是包含150万张多层图形设计的大型数据集，用于推动结构化布局理解与生成。



摘要： LICA是一个包含1,550,244个多层图形设计组成的大型数据集，涵盖20个设计类别和971,850个独特模板。该数据集不仅提供渲染后的PNG图像，还以分层结构表示每个设计，包含文本、图像、矢量和组元素，并配有丰富的元数据。此外，LICA引入了动画图形设计视频作为新的研究挑战，包含27,261个带有关键帧和运动参数的动画布局。该数据集为层感知修复、结构化布局生成、控制性设计编辑和时间感知生成建模等任务提供了新范式，支持基于设计结构而非仅像素的模型研究。

item.title: 基于UOT的单步生成模型类遗忘方法
item.content:encoded:
提出UOT-Unlearn框架实现单步生成模型的高效类遗忘。



摘要： 本文针对单步生成模型（如流图模型）在安全性方面的不足，提出了一种基于非平衡最优传输（UOT）的类遗忘框架UOT-Unlearn。该方法通过平衡遗忘成本和f散度惩罚，实现目标类别的有效移除，同时保持生成质量。与传统多步扩散遗忘方法不同，UOT-Unlearn能够在单次前向传播中完成遗忘过程，避免生成低质量或噪声样本。实验结果表明，该框架在CIFAR-10和ImageNet-256数据集上均优于现有基线方法。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的雨滴与反光联合去除方法
item.content:encoded:
提出UR$^3$任务和RDRF数据集，解决雨天拍摄图像中的雨滴与反光问题。



摘要： 文章针对雨天通过玻璃或挡风玻璃拍摄时雨滴与反光导致的图像可见性下降问题，首次定义了统一去除雨滴与反光（UR$^3$）任务，并构建了真实场景数据集RDRF。随后提出基于扩散模型的DiffUR$^3$框架，有效解决了这两种退化问题。实验表明该方法在基准数据集和真实图像上均取得领先效果，相关数据集和代码将公开。

item.title: AI代理生态系统中的多智能体学习现象与教育启示
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示AI代理在无干预下形成学习行为，为多智能体教育系统设计提供新视角。



摘要： 本文探讨了AI代理平台中形成的多智能体学习现象，发现人类通过配置代理经历双向支架过程，代理之间自发形成学习网络，共享记忆架构，并展现出信任动态和平台寿命问题。这些自然现象为多智能体教育系统的设计提供了重要启示。作者提出了一种基于‘教AI队友’的课程设计，并指出了未来研究方向和开放性问题。

item.title: 基于正常化流的场景图异常关系检测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出BUSSARD模型，用于检测场景图中的异常关系。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于正常化流的场景图异常关系检测方法（BUSSARD），通过将场景图中的对象和关系嵌入语言模型，利用现实世界的语义知识进行学习。该模型通过正常化流学习双射变换，将对象-关系-对象三元组映射到简单分布，从而实现基于似然估计的异常检测。在SARD数据集上评估显示，该方法在AUROC指标上比现有最佳模型提升约10%，且运行速度提高五倍。消融实验表明其在同义词处理方面具有更高的鲁棒性和通用性。
