item.title: Delayed Backdoor Attacks: Exploring the Temporal Dimension as a New Attack Surface in Pre-Trained Models
item.content:encoded: Backdoor attacks against pre-trained models (PTMs) have traditionally operated under an ``immediacy assumption,'' where malicious behavior manifests instantly upon trigger occurrence. This work revisits and challenges this paradigm by introducing \textit{\textbf{Delayed Backdoor Attacks (DBA)}}, a new class of threats in which activation is temporally decoupled from trigger exposure. We propose that this \textbf{temporal dimension} is the key to unlocking a previously infeasible class of attacks: those that use common, everyday words as triggers. To examine the feasibility of this paradigm, we design and implement a proof-of-concept prototype, termed \underline{D}elayed Backdoor Attacks Based on \underline{N}onlinear \underline{D}ecay (DND). DND embeds a lightweight, stateful logic module that postpones activation until a configurable threshold is reached, producing a distinct latency phase followed by a controlled outbreak. We derive a formal model to characterize this latency behavior and propose a dual-metric evaluation framework (ASR and ASR$_{delay}$) to empirically measure the delay effect. Extensive experiments on four (natural language processing)NLP benchmarks validate the core capabilities of DND: it remains dormant for a controllable duration, sustains high clean accuracy ($\ge$94\%), and achieves near-perfect post-activation attack success rates ($\approx$99\%, The average of other methods is below 95\%.). Moreover, DND exhibits resilience against several state-of-the-art defenses. This study provides the first empirical evidence that the temporal dimension constitutes a viable yet unprotected attack surface in PTMs, underscoring the need for next-generation, stateful, and time-aware defense mechanisms.

item.title: Temporal Straightening for Latent Planning
item.content:encoded: Learning good representations is essential for latent planning with world models. While pretrained visual encoders produce strong semantic visual features, they are not tailored to planning and contain information irrelevant -- or even detrimental -- to planning. Inspired by the perceptual straightening hypothesis in human visual processing, we introduce temporal straightening to improve representation learning for latent planning. Using a curvature regularizer that encourages locally straightened latent trajectories, we jointly learn an encoder and a predictor. We show that reducing curvature this way makes the Euclidean distance in latent space a better proxy for the geodesic distance and improves the conditioning of the planning objective. We demonstrate empirically that temporal straightening makes gradient-based planning more stable and yields significantly higher success rates across a suite of goal-reaching tasks.

item.title: Manifold-Optimal Guidance: A Unified Riemannian Control View of Diffusion Guidance
item.content:encoded: Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) serves as the de facto control mechanism for conditional diffusion, yet high guidance scales notoriously induce oversaturation, texture artifacts, and structural collapse. We attribute this failure to a geometric mismatch: standard CFG performs Euclidean extrapolation in ambient space, inadvertently driving sampling trajectories off the high-density data manifold. To resolve this, we present Manifold-Optimal Guidance (MOG), a framework that reformulates guidance as a local optimal control problem. MOG yields a closed-form, geometry-aware Riemannian update that corrects off-manifold drift without requiring retraining. Leveraging this perspective, we further introduce Auto-MOG, a dynamic energy-balancing schedule that adaptively calibrates guidance strength, effectively eliminating the need for manual hyperparameter tuning. Extensive validation demonstrates that MOG yields superior fidelity and alignment compared to baselines, with virtually no added computational overhead.

item.title: AS-Bridge: A Bidirectional Generative Framework Bridging Next-Generation Astronomical Surveys
item.content:encoded: The upcoming decade of observational cosmology will be shaped by large sky surveys, such as the ground-based LSST at the Vera C. Rubin Observatory and the space-based Euclid mission. While they promise an unprecedented view of the Universe across depth, resolution, and wavelength, their differences in observational modality, sky coverage, point-spread function, and scanning cadence make joint analysis beneficial, but also challenging. To facilitate joint analysis, we introduce A(stronomical)S(urvey)-Bridge, a bidirectional generative model that translates between ground- and space-based observations. AS-Bridge learns a diffusion model that employs a stochastic Brownian Bridge process between the LSST and Euclid observations. The two surveys have overlapping sky regions, where we can explicitly model the conditional probabilistic distribution between them. We show that this formulation enables new scientific capabilities beyond single-survey analysis, including faithful probabilistic predictions of missing survey observations and inter-survey detection of rare events. These results establish the feasibility of inter-survey generative modeling. AS-Bridge is therefore well-positioned to serve as a complementary component of future LSST-Euclid joint data pipelines, enhancing the scientific return once data from both surveys become available. Data and code are available at \href{https://github.com/ZHANG7DC/AS-Bridge}{https://github.com/ZHANG7DC/AS-Bridge}.

item.title: O3N: Omnidirectional Open-Vocabulary Occupancy Prediction
item.content:encoded: Understanding and reconstructing the 3D world through omnidirectional perception is an inevitable trend in the development of autonomous agents and embodied intelligence. However, existing 3D occupancy prediction methods are constrained by limited perspective inputs and predefined training distribution, making them difficult to apply to embodied agents that require comprehensive and safe perception of scenes in open world exploration. To address this, we present O3N, the first purely visual, end-to-end Omnidirectional Open-vocabulary Occupancy predictioN framework. O3N embeds omnidirectional voxels in a polar-spiral topology via the Polar-spiral Mamba (PsM) module, enabling continuous spatial representation and long-range context modeling across 360°. The Occupancy Cost Aggregation (OCA) module introduces a principled mechanism for unifying geometric and semantic supervision within the voxel space, ensuring consistency between the reconstructed geometry and the underlying semantic structure. Moreover, Natural Modality Alignment (NMA) establishes a gradient-free alignment pathway that harmonizes visual features, voxel embeddings, and text semantics, forming a consistent "pixel-voxel-text" representation triad. Extensive experiments on multiple models demonstrate that our method not only achieves state-of-the-art performance on QuadOcc and Human360Occ benchmarks but also exhibits remarkable cross-scene generalization and semantic scalability, paving the way toward universal 3D world modeling. The source code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/MengfeiD/O3N.

item.title: Hybrid Energy-Aware Reward Shaping: A Unified Lightweight Physics-Guided Methodology for Policy Optimization
item.content:encoded: Deep reinforcement learning excels in continuous control but often requires extensive exploration, while physics-based models demand complete equations and suffer cubic complexity. This study proposes Hybrid Energy-Aware Reward Shaping (H-EARS), unifying potential-based reward shaping with energy-aware action regularization. H-EARS constrains action magnitude while balancing task-specific and energy-based potentials via functional decomposition, achieving linear complexity O(n) by capturing dominant energy components without full dynamics. We establish a theoretical foundation including: (1) functional independence for separate task/energy optimization; (2) energy-based convergence acceleration; (3) convergence guarantees under function approximation; and (4) approximate potential error bounds. Lyapunov stability connections are analyzed as heuristic guides. Experiments across baselines show improved convergence, stability, and energy efficiency. Vehicle simulations validate applicability in safety-critical domains under extreme conditions. Results confirm that integrating lightweight physics priors enhances model-free RL without complete system models, enabling transfer from lab research to industrial applications.

item.title: Causal Prosody Mediation for Text-to-Speech:Counterfactual Training of Duration, Pitch, and Energy in FastSpeech2
item.content:encoded: We propose a novel causal prosody mediation framework for expressive text-to-speech (TTS) synthesis. Our approach augments the FastSpeech2 architecture with explicit emotion conditioning and introduces counterfactual training objectives to disentangle emotional prosody from linguistic content. By formulating a structural causal model of how text (content), emotion, and speaker jointly influence prosody (duration, pitch, energy) and ultimately the speech waveform, we derive two complementary loss terms: an Indirect Path Constraint (IPC) to enforce that emotion affects speech only through prosody, and a Counterfactual Prosody Constraint (CPC) to encourage distinct prosody patterns for different emotions. The resulting model is trained on multi-speaker emotional corpora (LibriTTS, EmoV-DB, VCTK) with a combined objective that includes standard spectrogram reconstruction and variance prediction losses alongside our causal losses. In evaluations on expressive speech synthesis, our method achieves significantly improved prosody manipulation and emotion rendering, with higher mean opinion scores (MOS) and emotion accuracy than baseline FastSpeech2 variants. We also observe better intelligibility (low WER) and speaker consistency when transferring emotions across speakers. Extensive ablations confirm that the causal objectives successfully separate prosody attribution, yielding an interpretable model that allows controlled counterfactual prosody editing (e.g. "same utterance, different emotion") without compromising naturalness. We discuss the implications for identifiability in prosody modeling and outline limitations such as the assumption that emotion effects are fully captured by pitch, duration, and energy. Our work demonstrates how integrating causal learning principles into TTS can improve controllability and expressiveness in generated speech.

item.title: An Automatic Text Classification Method Based on Hierarchical Taxonomies, Neural Networks and Document Embedding: The NETHIC Tool
item.content:encoded: This work describes an automatic text classification method implemented in a software tool called NETHIC, which takes advantage of the inner capabilities of highly-scalable neural networks combined with the expressiveness of hierarchical taxonomies. As such, NETHIC succeeds in bringing about a mechanism for text classification that proves to be significantly effective as well as efficient. The tool had undergone an experimentation process against both a generic and a domain-specific corpus, outputting promising results. On the basis of this experimentation, NETHIC has been now further refined and extended by adding a document embedding mechanism, which has shown improvements in terms of performance on the individual networks and on the whole hierarchical model.

item.title: SPEGC: Continual Test-Time Adaptation via Semantic-Prompt-Enhanced Graph Clustering for Medical Image Segmentation
item.content:encoded: In medical image segmentation tasks, the domain gap caused by the difference in data collection between training and testing data seriously hinders the deployment of pre-trained models in clinical practice. Continual Test-Time Adaptation (CTTA) aims to enable pre-trained models to adapt to continuously changing unlabeled domains, providing an effective approach to solving this problem. However, existing CTTA methods often rely on unreliable supervisory signals, igniting a self-reinforcing cycle of error accumulation that culminates in catastrophic performance degradation. To overcome these challenges, we propose a CTTA via Semantic-Prompt-Enhanced Graph Clustering (SPEGC) for medical image segmentation. First, we design a semantic prompt feature enhancement mechanism that utilizes decoupled commonality and heterogeneity prompt pools to inject global contextual information into local features, alleviating their susceptibility to noise interference under domain shift. Second, based on these enhanced features, we design a differentiable graph clustering solver. This solver reframes global edge sparsification as an optimal transport problem, allowing it to distill a raw similarity matrix into a refined and high-order structural representation in an end-to-end manner. Finally, this robust structural representation is used to guide model adaptation, ensuring predictions are consistent at a cluster-level and dynamically adjusting decision boundaries. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SPEGC outperforms other state-of-the-art CTTA methods on two medical image segmentation benchmarks. The source code is available at https://github.com/Jwei-Z/SPEGC-for-MIS.

item.title: LABSHIELD: A Multimodal Benchmark for Safety-Critical Reasoning and Planning in Scientific Laboratories
item.content:encoded: Artificial intelligence is increasingly catalyzing scientific automation, with multimodal large language model (MLLM) agents evolving from lab assistants into self-driving lab operators. This transition imposes stringent safety requirements on laboratory environments, where fragile glassware, hazardous substances, and high-precision laboratory equipment render planning errors or misinterpreted risks potentially irreversible. However, the safety awareness and decision-making reliability of embodied agents in such high-stakes settings remain insufficiently defined and evaluated. To bridge this gap, we introduce LABSHIELD, a realistic multi-view benchmark designed to assess MLLMs in hazard identification and safety-critical reasoning. Grounded in U.S. Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) standards and the Globally Harmonized System (GHS), LABSHIELD establishes a rigorous safety taxonomy spanning 164 operational tasks with diverse manipulation complexities and risk profiles. We evaluate 20 proprietary models, 9 open-source models, and 3 embodied models under a dual-track evaluation framework. Our results reveal a systematic gap between general-domain MCQ accuracy and Semi-open QA safety performance, with models exhibiting an average drop of 32.0% in professional laboratory scenarios, particularly in hazard interpretation and safety-aware planning. These findings underscore the urgent necessity for safety-centric reasoning frameworks to ensure reliable autonomous scientific experimentation in embodied laboratory contexts. The full dataset will be released soon.

item.title: WORKSWORLD: A Domain for Integrated Numeric Planning and Scheduling of Distributed Pipelined Workflows
item.content:encoded: This work pursues automated planning and scheduling of distributed data pipelines, or workflows. We develop a general workflow and resource graph representation that includes both data processing and sharing components with corresponding network interfaces for scheduling. Leveraging these graphs, we introduce WORKSWORLD, a new domain for numeric domain-independent planners designed for permanently scheduled workflows, like ingest pipelines. Our framework permits users to define data sources, available workflow components, and desired data destinations and formats without explicitly declaring the entire workflow graph as a goal. The planner solves a joint planning and scheduling problem, producing a plan that both builds the workflow graph and schedules its components on the resource graph. We empirically show that a state-of-the-art numeric planner running on commodity hardware with one hour of CPU time and 30GB of memory can solve linear-chain workflows of up to 14 components across eight sites.

item.title: Efficient Generative Modeling with Unitary Matrix Product States Using Riemannian Optimization
item.content:encoded: Tensor networks, which are originally developed for characterizing complex quantum many-body systems, have recently emerged as a powerful framework for capturing high-dimensional probability distributions with strong physical interpretability. This paper systematically studies matrix product states (MPS) for generative modeling and shows that unitary MPS, which is a tensor-network architecture that is both simple and expressive, offers clear benefits for unsupervised learning by reducing ambiguity in parameter updates and improving efficiency. To overcome the inefficiency of standard gradient-based MPS training, we develop a Riemannian optimization approach that casts probabilistic modeling as an optimization problem with manifold constraints, and further derive an efficient space-decoupling algorithm. Experiments on Bars-and-Stripes and EMNIST datasets demonstrate fast adaptation to data structure, stable updates, and strong performance while maintaining the efficiency and expressive power of MPS.

item.title: Locating Demographic Bias at the Attention-Head Level in CLIP's Vision Encoder
item.content:encoded: Standard fairness audits of foundation models quantify that a model is biased, but not where inside the network the bias resides. We propose a mechanistic fairness audit that combines projected residual-stream decomposition, zero-shot Concept Activation Vectors, and bias-augmented TextSpan analysis to locate demographic bias at the level of individual attention heads in vision transformers. As a feasibility case study, we apply this pipeline to the CLIP ViT-L-14 encoder on 42 profession classes of the FACET benchmark, auditing both gender and age bias. For gender, the pipeline identifies four terminal-layer heads whose ablation reduces global bias (Cramer's V: 0.381 -> 0.362) while marginally improving accuracy (+0.42%); a layer-matched random control confirms that this effect is specific to the identified heads. A single head in the final layer contributes to the majority of the reduction in the most stereotyped classes, and class-level analysis shows that corrected predictions shift toward the correct occupation. For age, the same pipeline identifies candidate heads, but ablation produces weaker and less consistent effects, suggesting that age bias is encoded more diffusely than gender bias in this model. These results provide preliminary evidence that head-level bias localisation is feasible for discriminative vision encoders and that the degree of localisability may vary across protected attributes. keywords: Bias . CLIP . Mechanistic Interpretability . Vision Transformer . Fairness

item.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration for Automated Design Exploration on High Performance Computing Systems
item.content:encoded: Today's scientific challenges, from climate modeling to Inertial Confinement Fusion design to novel material design, require exploring huge design spaces. In order to enable high-impact scientific discovery, we need to scale up our ability to test hypotheses, generate results, and learn from them rapidly. We present MADA (Multi-Agent Design Assistant), a Large Language Model (LLM) powered multi-agent framework that coordinates specialized agents for complex design workflows. A Job Management Agent (JMA) launches and manages ensemble simulations on HPC systems, a Geometry Agent (GA) generates meshes, and an Inverse Design Agent (IDA) proposes new designs informed by simulation outcomes. While general purpose, we focus development and validation on Richtmyer--Meshkov Instability (RMI) suppression, a critical challenge in Inertial Confinement Fusion. We evaluate on two complementary settings: running a hydrodynamics simulations on HPC systems, and using a pre-trained machine learning surrogate for rapid design exploration. Our results demonstrate that the MADA system successfully executes iterative design refinement, automatically improving designs toward optimal RMI suppression with minimal manual intervention. Our framework reduces cumbersome manual workflow setup, and enables automated design exploration at scale. More broadly, it demonstrates a reusable pattern for coupling reasoning, simulation, specialized tools, and coordinated workflows to accelerate scientific discovery.

item.title: Large Language Models for Biomedical Article Classification
item.content:encoded: This work presents a systematic and in-depth investigation of the utility of large language models as text classifiers for biomedical article classification. The study uses several small and mid-size open source models, as well as selected closed source ones, and is more comprehensive than most prior work with respect to the scope of evaluated configurations: different types of prompts, output processing methods for generating both class and class probability predictions, as well as few-shot example counts and selection methods. The performance of the most successful configurations is compared to that of conventional classification algorithms. The obtained average PR AUC over 15 challenging datasets above 0.4 for zero-shot prompting and nearly 0.5 for few-shot prompting comes close to that of the naïve Bayes classifier (0.5), the random forest algorithm (0.5 with default settings or 0.55 with hyperparameter tuning) and fine-tuned transformer models (0.5). These results confirm the utility of large language models as text classifiers for non-trivial domains and provide practical recommendations of the most promising setups, including in particular using output token probabilities for class probability prediction.

item.title: EVATok: Adaptive Length Video Tokenization for Efficient Visual Autoregressive Generation
item.content:encoded: Autoregressive (AR) video generative models rely on video tokenizers that compress pixels into discrete token sequences. The length of these token sequences is crucial for balancing reconstruction quality against downstream generation computational cost. Traditional video tokenizers apply a uniform token assignment across temporal blocks of different videos, often wasting tokens on simple, static, or repetitive segments while underserving dynamic or complex ones. To address this inefficiency, we introduce $\textbf{EVATok}$, a framework to produce $\textbf{E}$fficient $\textbf{V}$ideo $\textbf{A}$daptive $\textbf{Tok}$enizers. Our framework estimates optimal token assignments for each video to achieve the best quality-cost trade-off, develops lightweight routers for fast prediction of these optimal assignments, and trains adaptive tokenizers that encode videos based on the assignments predicted by routers. We demonstrate that EVATok delivers substantial improvements in efficiency and overall quality for video reconstruction and downstream AR generation. Enhanced by our advanced training recipe that integrates video semantic encoders, EVATok achieves superior reconstruction and state-of-the-art class-to-video generation on UCF-101, with at least 24.4% savings in average token usage compared to the prior state-of-the-art LARP and our fixed-length baseline.

item.title: Deep Learning Network-Temporal Models For Traffic Prediction
item.content:encoded: Time series analysis is critical for emerging net- work intelligent control and management functions. However, existing statistical-based and shallow machine learning models have shown limited prediction capabilities on multivariate time series. The intricate topological interdependency and complex temporal patterns in network data demand new model approaches. In this paper, based on a systematic multivariate time series model study, we present two deep learning models aiming for learning both temporal patterns and network topological correlations at the same time: a customized network-temporal graph attention network (GAT) model and a fine-tuned multi-modal large language model (LLM) with a clustering overture. Both models are studied against an LSTM model that already outperforms the statistical methods. Through extensive training and performance studies on a real-world network dataset, the LLM-based model demonstrates superior overall prediction and generalization performance, while the GAT model shows its strength in reducing prediction variance across the time series and horizons. More detailed analysis also reveals important insights into correlation variability and prediction distribution discrepancies over time series and different prediction horizons.

item.title: AstroSplat: Physics-Based Gaussian Splatting for Rendering and Reconstruction of Small Celestial Bodies
item.content:encoded: Image-based surface reconstruction and characterization are crucial for missions to small celestial bodies (e.g., asteroids), as it informs mission planning, navigation, and scientific analysis. Recent advances in Gaussian splatting enable high-fidelity neural scene representations but typically rely on a spherical harmonic intensity parameterization that is strictly appearance-based and does not explicitly model material properties or light-surface interactions. We introduce AstroSplat, a physics-based Gaussian splatting framework that integrates planetary reflectance models to improve the autonomous reconstruction and photometric characterization of small-body surfaces from in-situ imagery. The proposed framework is validated on real imagery taken by NASA's Dawn mission, where we demonstrate superior rendering performance and surface reconstruction accuracy compared to the typical spherical harmonic parameterization.

item.title: Deactivating Refusal Triggers: Understanding and Mitigating Overrefusal in Safety Alignment
item.content:encoded: Safety alignment aims to ensure that large language models (LLMs) refuse harmful requests by post-training on harmful queries paired with refusal answers. Although safety alignment is widely adopted in industry, the overrefusal problem where aligned LLMs also reject benign queries after safety alignment post-training, remains insufficiently studied. Such an issue degrades the usability of safety alignment in real-world applications. In this paper, we examine how overrefusal arises under safety alignment, and propose a mitigation strategy inspired by our findings. We define refusal triggers as linguistic cues in the training data that elicit refusal responses, safety alignment encourages LLMs to associate refusal triggers within a training sample with refusal responses, leading aligned LLMs to refuse harmful queries. However, the refusal triggers include not only harmful linguistic cues but also non-harmful cues, therefore causing overrefusal to benign queries. Building on this mechanistic analysis, we propose a method that explicitly considers refusal triggers in the safety alignment fine-tuning. Empirical results demonstrate that our approach achieves a more favorable trade-off between defense against jailbreak attacks and responsiveness to benign queries, outperforming prior methods. Warning: this paper contains harmful and biased sentences.

item.title: OSM-based Domain Adaptation for Remote Sensing VLMs
item.content:encoded: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) adapted to remote sensing rely heavily on domain-specific image-text supervision, yet high-quality annotations for satellite and aerial imagery remain scarce and expensive to produce. Prevailing pseudo-labeling pipelines address this gap by distilling knowledge from large frontier models, but this dependence on large teachers is costly, limits scalability, and caps achievable performance at the ceiling of the teacher. We propose OSMDA: a self-contained domain adaptation framework that eliminates this dependency. Our key insight is that a capable base VLM can serve as its own annotation engine: by pairing aerial images with rendered OpenStreetMap (OSM) tiles, we leverage optical character recognition and chart comprehension capabilities of the model to generate captions enriched by OSM's vast auxiliary metadata. The model is then fine-tuned on the resulting corpus with satellite imagery alone, yielding OSMDA-VLM, a domain-adapted VLM that requires no manual labeling and no stronger external model. We conduct exhaustive evaluations spanning 10 benchmarks across image-text-to-text tasks and comparing against 9 competitive baselines. When equally mixed with real data, our method achieves state-of-the-art results, while being substantially cheaper to train than teacher-dependent alternatives. These results suggest that, given a strong foundation model, alignment with crowd-sourced geographic data is a practical and scalable path towards remote sensing domain adaptation. Dataset and model weights will be made publicly available.

item.title: 基于时空解耦的自监督视频去噪方法F2R
item.content:encoded:
F2R通过时空解耦提升视频去噪效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Frames2Residual（F2R）的自监督视频去噪框架，该框架通过将训练过程分为两个阶段：盲时序一致性建模和非盲空间纹理恢复，以解决现有方法在时空关联性上的不足。第一阶段通过帧级盲策略学习帧间一致性，生成时序一致的锚点；第二阶段利用该锚点安全地重新引入中心帧，恢复高频空间残差并保持时序稳定性。实验表明，F2R在sRGB和原始视频基准测试中均优于现有方法。

item.title: G-STAR：一种端到端的长时多说话人语音识别系统
item.content:encoded:
G-STAR实现多说话人语音的精确时间标注与身份一致性。



摘要： 本文提出G-STAR，一种用于长时多说话人语音的端到端系统，结合时间感知的说话人追踪模块和语音大模型。该系统通过结构化说话人提示和时间定位，提升语音转录的准确性，并支持组件优化和联合训练，适应不同监督条件和领域变化。实验分析了提示融合、局部与长上下文权衡以及分层目标。

item.title: GLM-OCR：高效多模态文档理解模型
item.content:encoded:
GLM-OCR是高效的0.9B参数多模态文档理解模型。



摘要： GLM-OCR是一款0.9B参数的紧凑多模态模型，用于现实世界中的文档理解。它结合了0.4B参数的CogViT视觉编码器和0.5B参数的GLM语言解码器，在计算效率和识别性能之间取得良好平衡。为提升OCR任务的解码效率，GLM-OCR引入了多标记预测机制（MTP），每步预测多个标记，提高解码吞吐量并保持低内存开销。系统采用两阶段流程：先进行布局分析，再进行区域级并行识别。在公共基准和工业场景中评估显示，GLM-OCR在文档解析、文本和公式转录、表格结构恢复和关键信息提取方面表现出色，适合资源受限的边缘部署和大规模生产系统。

item.title: 基于知识图谱的药物处方审核系统PharmGraph-Auditor
item.content:encoded:
PharmGraph-Auditor提升处方审核安全性与效率。



摘要： 本文提出PharmGraph-Auditor，一种基于可信混合药物知识库（HPKB）的处方审核系统。该系统采用虚拟知识图谱架构，结合关系型与图结构以增强推理能力。通过迭代模式优化算法构建HPKB，并引入基于知识库的验证链（CoV），将大语言模型转化为透明推理引擎，提升处方审核的准确性与可追溯性。实验表明该系统具有强大的知识提取能力，有助于药师实现更安全、高效的处方审核。

item.title: LLM评估的共识幻觉与动态评分框架研究
item.content:encoded:
LLM评估共识可能不真实，动态评分框架提升评估质量。



摘要： 本文挑战了LLM作为评估者的传统假设，指出高评估者一致性可能是虚假的。通过大规模实验，发现模型层面的高一致性掩盖了样本层面的脆弱性，并揭示了高质量输出反而获得最不一致评价的现象。其次，研究提出MERG框架，通过结合领域知识生成评分标准，显著提升了评估的一致性和意义，尤其在教育和学术领域效果显著。该成果对RLAIF中的奖励建模具有重要启示。

item.title: 基于高斯混合过程的多模态条件密度估计方法
item.content:encoded:
提出GGMP方法解决多模态条件密度估计问题。



摘要： 文章介绍了通用高斯混合过程（GGMP），这是一种基于高斯过程（GP）的方法，用于在每个输入可能对应复杂输出分布而非单一标量响应的情况下进行多模态条件密度估计。GGMP结合了局部高斯混合拟合、跨输入成分对齐和每成分异方差GP训练，生成闭式高斯混合预测密度。该方法具有可计算性，兼容标准GP求解器和可扩展方法，避免了传统多模态GP方法中指数级增长的潜在分配结构。实验表明，GGMP在具有明显非高斯性和多模态性的合成和真实数据集上表现更优。

item.title: WalkGPT：基于像素的行人导航引导模型
item.content:encoded:
WalkGPT解决复杂城市场景下的可访问性导航问题。



摘要： 本文提出WalkGPT，一种结合语言推理与分割的像素接地大型视觉语言模型，用于生成深度感知的可访问性导航指导。该模型通过多尺度查询投影器和校准文本投影器，实现细粒度的语义与空间 grounding，无需用户提供的提示或锚点。WalkGPT在包含41k行人视角图像的PAVE基准上进行了测试，表现出强大的语义推理和分割性能。

item.title: 基于人类推理的语音深度伪造检测框架
item.content:encoded:
提出HIR-SDD框架提升语音深度伪造检测效果与可解释性。



摘要： 本文提出一种新型语音深度伪造检测框架HIR-SDD，结合大音频语言模型与人类标注数据集中的链式推理方法。该方法有效提升了对新音频领域和生成器的泛化能力，并增强了预测的可解释性，能够提供符合人类认知的判断依据。实验结果表明，该方法在检测效果和解释性方面均表现出色。

item.title: 轻量级Transformer点云架构的高效训练与评估
item.content:encoded:
轻量模型在点云任务中表现优于大型模型。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于Transformer的轻量级点云架构，仅使用3.9万组点云数据进行训练，却在性能上超越了依赖更多数据的大型基础模型。实验表明，该模型接近于使用百万级多模态数据训练的先进模型，验证了精心设计的训练方案和架构的有效性。研究还进行了全面的复现实验，统一评估标准并比较多种点云架构，证明简单结构也能取得优异效果。相关代码和预训练模型已开源。

item.title: 人类提升研究在前沿AI系统决策中的挑战与应用
item.content:encoded:
人类提升研究在高风险决策中面临方法论挑战。



摘要： 文章探讨了人类提升研究（测量AI对人类表现的影响）在前沿AI系统部署和治理中的应用。尽管该方法基于成熟的因果推断框架，但其与快速演进的AI系统、变化的基准线及用户能力等特性之间的互动仍缺乏深入研究。通过对16位专家的访谈，文章揭示了标准因果假设与实际研究对象之间的张力，并分析了在生物安全、网络安全、教育和劳动力等领域中，这些挑战如何影响研究结果的内部、外部和结构有效性。文章还总结了从业者提出的解决方案，明确了人类提升研究证据在高风险决策中的局限性和适用范围。

item.title: 基于历史共识训练的变分自编码器方法防止后验崩溃
item.content:encoded:
通过GMM聚类约束防止VAE后验崩溃，无需额外稳定性条件。



摘要： 本文提出一种新的方法来解决变分自编码器（VAEs）中的后验崩溃问题。传统方法依赖于架构限制或超参数调整，而本文通过利用高斯混合模型（GMM）的多重聚类特性，引入了历史共识训练（Historical Consensus Training）。该方法通过迭代优化和选择过程逐步改进候选GMM先验，使模型在多个聚类约束下训练，从而形成一个稳定区域，排除后验崩溃的可能性。实验表明，无论解码器方差或正则化强度如何，该方法都能获得非崩溃的表示。此方法不依赖显式的稳定性条件，适用于任意神经网络架构。

item.title: 面向可及性与包容性的AI驱动数字人建模框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种平台无关的AI驱动DHM框架，支持多模态交互研究。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种平台无关的数字人建模（DHM）框架，旨在促进AI驱动的多模态交互研究。该框架通过分离感知、交互建模和推理准备，提升可重复性、可扩展性和伦理合规性。它整合了OpenBCI Galea脑电采集设备，提供同步的EEG、EMG、EOG、PPG和惯性数据，并结合SuperTux游戏环境进行交互建模。生理信号以结构化时间对齐的可观测量形式表示，便于后续AI方法应用。框架支持计算任务原语和时间戳事件标记，确保跨异构传感器和平台的一致性。技术验证确认了数据完整性与同步性，未涉及人类受试者评估或AI推理。文章还讨论了可扩展性问题，并展示了其在无障碍交互设计和自适应系统研究中的应用潜力。

item.title: 提升网络入侵检测系统对抗攻击鲁棒性的方法研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种多层防御机制以增强NIDS对对抗样本的抵抗力。



摘要： 本文研究了对抗样本对基于机器学习的网络入侵检测系统（NIDS）的威胁，并提出一种多层防御机制来提高其鲁棒性。通过生成对抗网络（GAN）和快速梯度符号法（FGSM）生成恶意流量，评估了该防御机制的有效性。该方案包含一个堆叠分类器层和一个基于自编码器的第二层，用于验证分类结果的准确性。此外，还引入了对抗训练以进一步提升系统安全性。实验在UNSW-NB15和NSL-KDD数据集上进行，结果表明该方法显著增强了系统对对抗攻击的抵抗能力。

item.title: 基于多智能体强化学习的无人机医疗配送协调框架
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种MARL框架用于无人机医疗配送协调。



摘要： 本文研究了在不确定环境下，利用多智能体强化学习（MARL）协调无人机舰队进行医疗物资配送的问题。该框架将问题建模为部分可观测马尔可夫决策过程（POMDP），并采用近端策略优化（PPO）作为主要算法。通过真实地理数据验证，结果表明经典PPO在协调性能上优于异步和顺序学习策略，展示了强化学习在医疗物流中的应用潜力。

item.title: GeoFM在遥感影像嵌入设计中的系统分析
item.content:encoded:
研究GeoFM嵌入设计对遥感任务性能的影响。



摘要： 本文系统分析了基于地理基础模型（GeoFM）的遥感影像嵌入设计，探讨了主干架构、预训练策略、表示深度、空间聚合和表示组合对任务性能的影响。通过NeuCo-Bench实验平台，研究发现使用Transformer主干和均值池化可提供强大的默认嵌入，中间ResNet层可能优于最终层，自监督目标在特定任务中表现优异，结合不同目标的嵌入可提高鲁棒性。研究还展示了嵌入在保持任务性能的同时，可以显著压缩数据规模。

item.title: SearchLLM：面向开放性生成搜索的大型语言模型
item.content:encoded:
SearchLLM提升生成搜索质量与用户参与度。



摘要： 文章介绍了SearchLLM，这是首个面向开放性生成搜索的大型语言模型。该模型通过分层多维奖励系统，将基本约束与行为优化目标分离，以提升答案的准确性和用户满意度。其奖励模型结合规则检查和人工校准的LLM判断，生成可解释的评分向量。通过Gated Aggregation Strategy和GRPO方法进行训练，并在RedNote的AI搜索入口部署。实验结果显示，SearchLLM提升了生成质量，用户参与度提高，同时保持了严格的安全性和可靠性。

item.title: 自然历史博物馆数字化数据的自然语言交互系统设计
item.content:encoded:
通过对话式AI实现对百万级标本数据的高效查询与探索。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种基于对话式人工智能的系统设计，用于查询澳大利亚博物馆近170万份数字化标本记录。该系统结合了交互式地图和自然语言对话代理，使用户能够通过自然语言进行科学数据的探索与获取。系统利用大型语言模型的功能调用能力，从外部API动态检索结构化数据，实现实时、高效的数据库交互。该研究为自然历史博物馆的大规模数据开放提供了新思路，并为未来科学AI代理的设计提供了参考。

item.title: 神经网络训练中最佳学习率调度形状的探索
item.content:encoded:
研究发现最佳学习率调度需包含预热和衰减，常见调度非最优。



摘要： 本文探讨了神经网络训练中最佳学习率调度形状的问题。研究指出，学习率调度对训练成功至关重要，但目前缺乏统一标准。作者设计了一种搜索方法，在不同调度家族中寻找最佳形状，并发现预热和衰减是良好调度的稳健特征。实验表明，常用调度家族在所选任务中并非最优，且权重衰减等其他超参数也会影响最优调度形状。这是目前关于深度神经网络训练近似最优调度形状最全面的研究。

item.title: 基于最近邻距离比的内在维度估计方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的内在维度估计方法，具有通用性且效果优异。



摘要： 本文研究了数据内在维度估计问题，该问题在机器学习和计算机视觉中具有重要意义。现有方法通常依赖几何或分布假设，当这些假设不成立时性能会显著下降。为此，作者提出了一种基于最近邻距离比的新估计方法，计算简单且效果优于现有技术。更重要的是，理论分析证明该方法具有通用性，即无论数据分布如何都能收敛到真实内在维度。实验结果表明，该方法在基准流形和真实数据集上表现良好。

item.title: 基于深度学习的参数估计模型研究
item.content:encoded:
深度学习用于参数估计，提升模型准确性与鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文探讨了利用深度学习模型分析参数统计模型的方法，重点研究了基于模拟框架的贝叶斯和频率模型。通过使用总结网络和归一化流，模型能够有效逼近后验分布并解决逆问题。研究提出了一种分枝网络结构，通过降维层和全连接层实现参数估计，并在模拟中验证了其在有限样本、数据污染和算法近似方面的优势。该方法在遗传数据的EM算法中表现出色，展示了模拟仅方法在复杂建模任务中的潜力。

item.title: 基于LLM-as-a-Judge的文本评估框架与多模态聚类方法
item.content:encoded:
提出MultiwayPAM方法优化LLM评估文本质量的效率与公平性。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为LLM-as-a-Judge的灵活文本评估框架，该框架通过调整提示模板，可以从不同角度对文本质量进行评分。文章指出，使用该框架面临两大挑战：大规模文本评估时的计算成本较高，以及LLM评估者的内在偏差。为解决这些问题，作者提出了一种新的张量聚类方法MultiwayPAM，能够同时估计数据张量中各模式的聚类成员和中心点。通过分析MultiwayPAM得到的中心点，可以深入了解各个问题、回答者和评估者的聚类归属。实验表明，该方法在两个实际数据集上具有良好的效果。

item.title: 基于领域自适应的健康指标建模方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新框架提升健康指标建模性能。



摘要： 本文针对健康指标建模中因工况变化导致的分布不匹配问题，提出了一种结合退化阶段同步采样和跨域对齐融合大自编码器的领域自适应框架。该框架通过检测退化阶段的核变化点，实现源域与目标域样本在故障阶段上的同步采样，有效缓解了因阶段不对齐导致的损失误导。同时，利用大卷积核和跨注意力机制增强时间特征提取能力，提升了模型的域不变表示学习效果。实验结果表明，该方法在两个轴承数据集上相比现有方法平均提升24.1%。

item.title: 基于Med-DualLoRA的联邦微调在3D心脏磁共振疾病检测中的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出Med-DualLoRA框架提升3D CMR疾病检测性能与效率。



摘要： 本文研究了在多中心3D心脏磁共振（CMR）疾病检测任务中，使用联邦微调方法对基础模型进行优化。针对传统方法在异构数据下的性能瓶颈和通信开销问题，作者提出Med-DualLoRA框架，通过解耦全局共享和局部低秩适配模块，实现更高效的模型个性化与隐私保护。实验表明，该方法在ACDC和M&Ms数据集上取得了显著的性能提升，同时保持了良好的通信效率，为医疗基础模型的分布式适应提供了可行方案。

item.title: 多语言推理训练场：跨语言推理问题生成与研究支持
item.content:encoded:
多语言推理训练场生成跨语言推理问题，支持模型研究。



摘要： 本文介绍了多语言推理训练场（Multilingual Reasoning Gym），这是对原有Reasoning Gym的扩展，能够在14种语言中程序化生成可验证的推理问题。通过在10种语言中进行母语者验证并进行代码或模板适配，确保了语言自然性。该平台保留了原Reasoning Gym的核心优势，如无限问题生成和难度可调，并适用于基于可验证奖励的强化学习和评估设置。由于其程序化特性，该训练场支持大规模跨语言数据生成，有助于多语言推理模型的研究。

item.title: 基于语义地标的农业环境精准定位方法研究
item.content:encoded:
SLPF提升葡萄园中重复环境下的定位精度。



摘要： 本文针对葡萄园中因行间结构相似导致的定位误差问题，提出一种结合语义地标的粒子滤波定位方法（SLPF）。该方法利用LiDAR检测树干和杆件，并将其转化为语义墙以增强相邻行之间的区分度。同时引入GNSS作为轻量先验信息，在语义观测稀疏时保持定位稳定。实验表明，SLPF在10行葡萄园中相比几何定位、视觉定位和GNSS基线分别提升了22%-65%的定位精度，显著提高了行间定位正确率和横向误差控制。

item.title: 提升计算机图形质量评估的视觉语言模型方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出新框架提升VLM对CG质量的评估能力。



摘要： 本文针对现有计算机图形质量评估方法的不足，提出从用户视角识别六种关键感知维度，并构建包含3500张CG图像及其质量描述的数据集。通过构建问答基准测试现有视觉语言模型（VLMs）的表现，发现其在细粒度CG质量判断上存在不足。研究进一步提出基于检索增强生成的双流框架，有效提升VLMs在CG质量评估任务中的性能。

item.title: 基于在线强化学习的图生成模型优化方法Graph-GRPO
item.content:encoded:
Graph-GRPO提升图生成模型性能，实现高效采样与质量优化。



摘要： 本文提出Graph-GRPO，一种用于训练图生成模型（GFM）的在线强化学习框架。该方法通过推导GFM的转移概率表达式，替代蒙特卡洛采样，实现端到端的RL训练。同时引入局部优化策略，通过随机扰动节点和边来提升生成质量。实验表明，Graph-GRPO在合成和真实数据集上表现优异，在仅50步去噪的情况下，分别获得95.0%和97.5%的有效性、唯一性和新颖性评分，并在分子优化任务中超越现有方法。

item.title: 深度神经网络的不确定性量化方法比较研究
item.content:encoded:
比较蒙特卡洛Dropout与置信预测在深度学习中的不确定性评估效果。



摘要： 本文对比了两种常用的不确定性量化方法：基于蒙特卡洛Dropout的贝叶斯近似和非参数置信预测框架。通过在Fashion-MNIST数据集上对H-CNN VGG16和GoogLeNet进行实验，发现虽然H-CNN VGG16具有更高的预测准确率，但其表现出明显的过度自信；而GoogLeNet则提供了更可靠的不确定性估计。此外，置信预测方法在统计上具有保证的预测集，显示出在高风险决策场景中的实际价值。研究强调了在评估模型性能时应超越准确率，以提升深度学习系统的可靠性与可信度。

item.title: 基于VERI-DPO的临床摘要生成方法改进
item.content:encoded:
VERI-DPO提升临床摘要准确性与可靠性。



摘要： 本文提出VERI-DPO方法，通过声明验证和直接偏好优化提升临床摘要质量。研究使用MIMIC-III-Ext-VeriFact-BHC数据集，训练检索增强验证器以标记声明与证据的关系。该方法通过聚合句级声明得分，生成长度可控、矛盾锚定的偏好对，有效减少未支持声明比例，提高摘要有效性。

item.title: 基于数据驱动的MHD状态重建方法研究
item.content:encoded:
SHRED网络实现MHD状态高效重建。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于数据驱动的MHD状态重建框架，结合奇异值分解（SVD）与SHRED神经网络，从有限传感器测量数据中恢复全时空状态。该方法应用于压缩铅锂流在阶梯通道中的MHD测试案例，仅需三个温度传感器输入即可准确重建速度、压力和温度场。通过30种随机传感器配置验证了其鲁棒性，结果表明SHRED在训练集外磁场强度下仍能保持高精度，为核聚变多物理场问题提供了高效的替代建模策略，适用于实时监测与控制。

item.title: DynVLA: Learning World Dynamics for Action Reasoning in Autonomous Driving
item.content:encoded: We propose DynVLA, a driving VLA model that introduces a new CoT paradigm termed Dynamics CoT. DynVLA forecasts compact world dynamics before action generation, enabling more informed and physically grounded decision-making. To obtain compact dynamics representations, DynVLA introduces a Dynamics Tokenizer that compresses future evolution into a small set of dynamics tokens. Considering the rich environment dynamics in interaction-intensive driving scenarios, DynVLA decouples ego-centric and environment-centric dynamics, yielding more accurate world dynamics modeling. We then train DynVLA to generate dynamics tokens before actions through SFT and RFT, improving decision quality while maintaining latency-efficient inference. Compared to Textual CoT, which lacks fine-grained spatiotemporal understanding, and Visual CoT, which introduces substantial redundancy due to dense image prediction, Dynamics CoT captures the evolution of the world in a compact, interpretable, and efficient form. Extensive experiments on NAVSIM, Bench2Drive, and a large-scale in-house dataset demonstrate that DynVLA consistently outperforms Textual CoT and Visual CoT methods, validating the effectiveness and practical value of Dynamics CoT.

item.title: Too Vivid to Be Real? Benchmarking and Calibrating Generative Color Fidelity
item.content:encoded: Recent advances in text-to-image (T2I) generation have greatly improved visual quality, yet producing images that appear visually authentic to real-world photography remains challenging. This is partly due to biases in existing evaluation paradigms: human ratings and preference-trained metrics often favor visually vivid images with exaggerated saturation and contrast, which make generations often too vivid to be real even when prompted for realistic-style images. To address this issue, we present Color Fidelity Dataset (CFD) and Color Fidelity Metric (CFM) for objective evaluation of color fidelity in realistic-style generations. CFD contains over 1.3M real and synthetic images with ordered levels of color realism, while CFM employs a multimodal encoder to learn perceptual color fidelity. In addition, we propose a training-free Color Fidelity Refinement (CFR) that adaptively modulates spatial-temporal guidance scale in generation, thereby enhancing color authenticity. Together, CFD supports CFM for assessment, whose learned attention further guides CFR to refine T2I fidelity, forming a progressive framework for assessing and improving color fidelity in realistic-style T2I generation. The dataset and code are available at https://github.com/ZhengyaoFang/CFM.

item.title: IH-Challenge: A Training Dataset to Improve Instruction Hierarchy on Frontier LLMs
item.content:encoded: Instruction hierarchy (IH) defines how LLMs prioritize system, developer, user, and tool instructions under conflict, providing a concrete, trust-ordered policy for resolving instruction conflicts. IH is key to defending against jailbreaks, system prompt extractions, and agentic prompt injections. However, robust IH behavior is difficult to train: IH failures can be confounded with instruction-following failures, conflicts can be nuanced, and models can learn shortcuts such as overrefusing. We introduce IH-Challenge, a reinforcement learning training dataset, to address these difficulties. Fine-tuning GPT-5-Mini on IH-Challenge with online adversarial example generation improves IH robustness by +10.0% on average across 16 in-distribution, out-of-distribution, and human red-teaming benchmarks (84.1% to 94.1%), reduces unsafe behavior from 6.6% to 0.7% while improving helpfulness on general safety evaluations, and saturates an internal static agentic prompt injection evaluation, with minimal capability regression. We release the IH-Challenge dataset (https://huggingface.co/datasets/openai/ih-challenge) to support future research on robust instruction hierarchy.

item.title: Ergodicity in reinforcement learning
item.content:encoded: In reinforcement learning, we typically aim to optimize the expected value of the sum of rewards an agent collects over a trajectory. However, if the process generating these rewards is non-ergodic, the expected value, i.e., the average over infinitely many trajectories with a given policy, is uninformative for the average over a single, but infinitely long trajectory. Thus, if we care about how the individual agent performs during deployment, the expected value is not a good optimization objective. In this paper, we discuss the impact of non-ergodic reward processes on reinforcement learning agents through an instructive example, relate the notion of ergodic reward processes to more widely used notions of ergodic Markov chains, and present existing solutions that optimize long-term performance of individual trajectories under non-ergodic reward dynamics.

item.title: eLasmobranc Dataset: An Image Dataset for Elasmobranch Species Recognition and Biodiversity Monitoring
item.content:encoded: Elasmobranch populations are experiencing significant global declines, and several species are currently classified as threatened. Reliable monitoring and species-level identification are essential to support conservation and spatial planning initiatives such as Important Shark and Ray Areas (ISRAs). However, existing visual datasets are predominantly detection-oriented, underwater-acquired, or limited to coarse-grained categories, restricting their applicability to fine-grained morphological classification. We present the eLasmobranc Dataset, a curated and publicly available image collection from seven ecologically relevant elasmobranch species inhabiting the eastern Spanish Mediterranean coast, a region where two ISRAs have been identified. Images were obtained through dedicated data collection, including field campaigns and collaborations with local fish markets and projects, as well as from open-access public sources. The dataset was constructed predominantly from images acquired outside the aquatic environment under standardized protocols to ensure clear visualization of diagnostic morphological traits. It integrates expert-validated species annotations, structured spatial and temporal metadata, and complementary species-level information. The eLasmobranc Dataset is specifically designed to support supervised species-level classification, population studies, and the development of artificial intelligence systems for biodiversity monitoring. By combining morphological clarity, taxonomic reliability, and public accessibility, the dataset addresses a critical gap in fine-grained elasmobranch identification and promotes reproducible research in conservation-oriented computer vision. The dataset is publicly available at https://zenodo.org/records/18549737.

item.title: Designing Service Systems from Textual Evidence
item.content:encoded: Designing service systems requires selecting among alternative configurations -- choosing the best chatbot variant, the optimal routing policy, or the most effective quality control procedure. In many service systems, the primary evidence of performance quality is textual -- customer support transcripts, complaint narratives, compliance review reports -- rather than the scalar measurements assumed by classical optimization methods. Large language models (LLMs) can read such textual evidence and produce standardized quality scores, but these automated judges exhibit systematic biases that vary across alternatives and evaluation instances. Human expert review remains accurate but costly. We study how to identify the best service configuration with high confidence while minimizing expensive human audits, given that automated evaluation is cheap but biased. We formalize this as a sequential decision problem where a biased proxy score is observed for every evaluation, and a verified outcome can be acquired selectively at additional cost. We prove that LLM-only selection fails under arm-dependent bias, and that naive selective-audit estimators can be asymptotically biased. We develop an estimator combining proxy scores with inverse-propensity-weighted residuals and construct anytime-valid confidence sequences. Our algorithm, PP-LUCB, jointly decides which alternatives to evaluate and whether to request human audits, concentrating reviews where the LLM judge is least reliable. We prove correctness and establish instance-dependent cost bounds showing near-optimal efficiency. On a customer support ticket classification task, our algorithm correctly identifies the best model in 40/40 trials while achieving 90\% audit cost reduction.

item.title: PEEM: Prompt Engineering Evaluation Metrics for Interpretable Joint Evaluation of Prompts and Responses
item.content:encoded: Prompt design is a primary control interface for large language models (LLMs), yet standard evaluations largely reduce performance to answer correctness, obscuring why a prompt succeeds or fails and providing little actionable guidance. We propose PEEM (Prompt Engineering Evaluation Metrics), a unified framework for joint and interpretable evaluation of both prompts and responses. PEEM defines a structured rubric with 9 axes: 3 prompt criteria (clarity/structure, linguistic quality, fairness) and 6 response criteria (accuracy, coherence, relevance, objectivity, clarity, conciseness), and uses an LLM-based evaluator to output (i) scalar scores on a 1-5 Likert scale and (ii) criterion-specific natural-language rationales grounded in the rubric. Across 7 benchmarks and 5 task models, PEEM's accuracy axis strongly aligns with conventional accuracy while preserving model rankings (aggregate Spearman rho about 0.97, Pearson r about 0.94, p < 0.001). A multi-evaluator study with four models shows consistent relative judgments (pairwise rho = 0.68-0.85), supporting evaluator-agnostic deployment. Beyond alignment, PEEM captures complementary linguistic failure modes and remains informative under prompt perturbations: prompt-quality trends track downstream accuracy under iterative rewrites, semantic adversarial manipulations induce clear score degradation, and meaning-preserving paraphrases yield high stability (robustness rate about 76.7-80.6%). Finally, using only PEEM scores and rationales as feedback, a zero-shot prompt rewriting loop improves downstream accuracy by up to 11.7 points, outperforming supervised and RL-based prompt-optimization baselines. Overall, PEEM provides a reproducible, criterion-driven protocol that links prompt formulation to response behavior and enables systematic diagnosis and optimization of LLM interactions.

item.title: From Images to Words: Efficient Cross-Modal Knowledge Distillation to Language Models from Black-box Teachers
item.content:encoded: Knowledge distillation (KD) methods are pivotal in compressing large pre-trained language models into smaller models, ensuring computational efficiency without significantly dropping performance. Traditional KD techniques assume homogeneity in modalities between the teacher (source) and the student (target) models. On the other hand, existing multimodal knowledge distillation methods require modality-specific pre-training of the teacher model, which is computationally infeasible in most cases. In this paper, we introduce ARMADA, an efficient cross-modal knowledge distillation framework designed to transfer knowledge from large vision-language models, including black-box models, to language-only models. Unlike existing KD techniques that rely on the internal structures of multimodal teachers or require computationally expensive pre-training, ARMADA leverages novel alignment techniques to distil knowledge without altering the teacher model, ensuring efficiency and scalability. We empirically validate ARMADA on twelve natural language understanding, eight complex generative reasoning and five instruction-tuning tasks, demonstrating consistent performance improvements in large models such as DeBERTa-v2-1.4B, OPT-1.3B, LLaMA-{3B, 7B, 8B}. ARMADA achieves up to 3.4% improvement on language understanding tasks and 2.6% boost in generative reasoning, all without requiring expensive multimodal pre-training or fine-tuning of the teacher model. Our findings challenge conventional knowledge distillation paradigms by demonstrating that even vision-language models, despite lacking direct textual understanding, can significantly enhance language models when distilled appropriately.

item.title: MoXaRt: Audio-Visual Object-Guided Sound Interaction for XR
item.content:encoded: In Extended Reality (XR), complex acoustic environments often overwhelm users, compromising both scene awareness and social engagement due to entangled sound sources. We introduce MoXaRt, a real-time XR system that uses audio-visual cues to separate these sources and enable fine-grained sound interaction. MoXaRt's core is a cascaded architecture that performs coarse, audio-only separation in parallel with visual detection of sources (e.g., faces, instruments). These visual anchors then guide refinement networks to isolate individual sources, separating complex mixes of up to 5 concurrent sources (e.g., 2 voices + 3 instruments) with ~2 second processing latency. We validate MoXaRt through a technical evaluation on a new dataset of 30 one-minute recordings featuring concurrent speech and music, and a 22-participant user study. Empirical results indicate that our system significantly enhances speech intelligibility, yielding a 36.2% (p < 0.01) increase in listening comprehension within adversarial acoustic environments while substantially reducing cognitive load (p < 0.001), thereby paving the way for more perceptive and socially adept XR experiences.

item.title: Optimal Expert-Attention Allocation in Mixture-of-Experts: A Scalable Law for Dynamic Model Design
item.content:encoded: This paper presents a novel extension of neural scaling laws to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models, focusing on the optimal allocation of compute between expert and attention sub-layers. As MoE architectures have emerged as an efficient method for scaling model capacity without proportionally increasing computation, determining the optimal expert-attention compute ratio becomes critical. We define the ratio $r$ as the fraction of total FLOPs per token dedicated to the expert layers versus the attention layers, and explore how this ratio interacts with the overall compute budget and model sparsity. Through extensive experiments with GPT-style MoE Transformers, we empirically find that the optimal ratio $r^*$ follows a power-law relationship with total compute and varies with sparsity. Our analysis leads to an explicit formula for $r^*$, enabling precise control over the expert-attention compute allocation. We generalize the Chinchilla scaling law by incorporating this architectural parameter, providing a new framework for tuning MoE models beyond size and data. Our findings offer practical guidelines for designing efficient MoE models, optimizing performance while respecting fixed compute budgets.

item.title: Mitigating Translationese Bias in Multilingual LLM-as-a-Judge via Disentangled Information Bottleneck
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) have become a standard for multilingual evaluation, yet they exhibit a severe systematic translationese bias. In this paper, translationese bias is characterized as LLMs systematically favoring machine-translated text over human-authored references, particularly in low-resource languages. We attribute this bias to spurious correlations with (i) latent manifold alignment with English and (ii) cross-lingual predictability. To mitigate this bias, we propose DIBJudge, a robust fine-tuning framework that learns a minimally sufficient, judgment-critical representation via variational information compression, while explicitly isolating spurious factors into the dedicated bias branch. Furthermore, we incorporate a cross-covariance penalty that explicitly suppresses statistical dependence between robust and bias representations, thereby encouraging effective disentanglement. Extensive evaluations on multilingual reward modeling benchmarks and a dedicated translationese bias evaluation suite demonstrate that the proposed DIBJudge consistently outperforms strong baselines and substantially mitigates translationese bias.

item.title: Disentangling Similarity and Relatedness in Topic Models
item.content:encoded: The recent advancement of large language models has spurred a growing trend of integrating pre-trained language model (PLM) embeddings into topic models, fundamentally reshaping how topics capture semantic structure. Classical models such as Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) derive topics from word co-occurrence statistics, whereas PLM-augmented models anchor these statistics to pre-trained embedding spaces, imposing a prior that also favours clustering of semantically similar words. This structural difference can be captured by the psycholinguistic dimensions of thematic relatedness and taxonomic similarity of the topic words. To disentangle these dimensions in topic models, we construct a large synthetic benchmark of word pairs using LLM-based annotation to train a neural scoring function. We apply this scorer to a comprehensive evaluation across multiple corpora and topic model families, revealing that different model families capture distinct semantic structure in their topics. We further demonstrate that similarity and relatedness scores successfully predict downstream task performance depending on task requirements. This paper establishes similarity and relatedness as essential axes for topic model evaluation and provides a reliable pipeline for characterising these across model families and corpora.

item.title: Dynamic Knowledge Fusion for Multi-Domain Dialogue State Tracking
item.content:encoded: The performance of task-oriented dialogue models is strongly tied to how well they track dialogue states, which records and updates user information across multi-turn interactions. However, current multi-domain DST encounters two key challenges: the difficulty of effectively modeling dialogue history and the limited availability of annotated data, both of which hinder model performance. To tackle the aforementioned problems, we develop a dynamic knowledge fusion framework applicable to multi-domain DST. The model operates in two stages: first, an encoder-only network trained with contrastive learning encodes dialogue history and candidate slots, selecting relevant slots based on correlation scores; second, dynamic knowledge fusion leverages the structured information of selected slots as contextual prompts to enhance the accuracy and consistency of dialogue state tracking. This design enables more accurate integration of dialogue context and domain knowledge. Results obtained from multi-domain dialogue benchmarks indicate that our method notably improves both tracking accuracy and generalization, validating its capability in handling complex dialogue scenarios.

item.title: Sample-and-Search: An Effective Algorithm for Learning-Augmented k-Median Clustering in High dimensions
item.content:encoded: In this paper, we investigate the learning-augmented $k$-median clustering problem, which aims to improve the performance of traditional clustering algorithms by preprocessing the point set with a predictor of error rate $α\in [0,1)$. This preprocessing step assigns potential labels to the points before clustering. We introduce an algorithm for this problem based on a simple yet effective sampling method, which substantially improves upon the time complexities of existing algorithms. Moreover, we mitigate their exponential dependency on the dimensionality of the Euclidean space. Lastly, we conduct experiments to compare our method with several state-of-the-art learning-augmented $k$-median clustering methods. The experimental results suggest that our proposed approach can significantly reduce the computational complexity in practice, while achieving a lower clustering cost.

item.title: AlphaFlowTSE: One-Step Generative Target Speaker Extraction via Conditional AlphaFlow
item.content:encoded: In target speaker extraction (TSE), we aim to recover target speech from a multi-talker mixture using a short enrollment utterance as reference. Recent studies on diffusion and flow-matching generators have improved target-speech fidelity. However, multi-step sampling increases latency, and one-step solutions often rely on a mixture-dependent time coordinate that can be unreliable for real-world conversations. We present AlphaFlowTSE, a one-step conditional generative model trained with a Jacobian-vector product (JVP)-free AlphaFlow objective. AlphaFlowTSE learns mean-velocity transport along a mixture-to-target trajectory starting from the observed mixture, eliminating auxiliary mixing-ratio prediction, and stabilizes training by combining flow matching with an interval-consistency teacher-student target. Experiments on Libri2Mix and REAL-T confirm that AlphaFlowTSE improves target-speaker similarity and real-mixture generalization for downstream automatic speech recognition (ASR).

item.title: Resource-constrained Amazons chess decision framework integrating large language models and graph attention
item.content:encoded: Artificial intelligence has advanced significantly through the development of intelligent game-playing systems, providing rigorous testbeds for decision-making, strategic planning, and adaptive learning. However, resource-constrained environments pose critical challenges, as conventional deep learning methods heavily rely on extensive datasets and computational resources. In this paper, we propose a lightweight hybrid framework for the Game of the Amazons, which explores the paradigm of weak-to-strong generalization by integrating the structural reasoning of graph-based learning with the generative capabilities of large language models. Specifically, we leverage a Graph Attention Autoencoder to inform a multi-step Monte Carlo Tree Search, utilize a Stochastic Graph Genetic Algorithm to optimize evaluation signals, and harness GPT-4o-mini to generate synthetic training data. Unlike traditional approaches that rely on expert demonstrations, our framework learns from noisy and imperfect supervision. We demonstrate that the Graph Attention mechanism effectively functions as a structural filter, denoising the LLM's outputs. Experiments on a 10$\times$10 Amazons board show that our hybrid approach not only achieves a 15\%--56\% improvement in decision accuracy over baselines but also significantly outperforms its teacher model (GPT-4o-mini), achieving a competitive win rate of 45.0\% at N=30 nodes and a decisive 66.5\% at only N=50 nodes. These results verify the feasibility of evolving specialized, high-performance game AI from general-purpose foundation models under stringent computational constraints.

item.title: An Extreme Multi-label Text Classification (XMTC) Library Dataset: What if we took "Use of Practical AI in Digital Libraries" seriously?
item.content:encoded: Subject indexing is vital for discovery but hard to sustain at scale and across languages. We release a large bilingual (English/German) corpus of catalog records annotated with the Integrated Authority File (GND), plus a machine-actionable GND taxonomy. The resource enables ontology-aware multi-label classification, mapping text to authority terms, and agent-assisted cataloging with reproducible, authority-grounded evaluation. We provide a brief statistical profile and qualitative error analyses of three systems. We invite the community to assess not only accuracy but usefulness and transparency, toward authority-anchored AI co-pilots that amplify catalogers' work.

item.title: COMIC: Agentic Sketch Comedy Generation
item.content:encoded: We propose a fully automated AI system that produces short comedic videos similar to sketch shows such as Saturday Night Live. Starting with character references, the system employs a population of agents loosely based on real production studio roles, structured to optimize the quality and diversity of ideas and outputs through iterative competition, evaluation, and improvement. A key contribution is the introduction of LLM critics aligned with real viewer preferences through the analysis of a corpus of comedy videos on YouTube to automatically evaluate humor. Our experiments show that our framework produces results approaching the quality of professionally produced sketches while demonstrating state-of-the-art performance in video generation.

item.title: A Systematic Study of Pseudo-Relevance Feedback with LLMs
item.content:encoded: Pseudo-relevance feedback (PRF) methods built on large language models (LLMs) can be organized along two key design dimensions: the feedback source, which is where the feedback text is derived from and the feedback model, which is how the given feedback text is used to refine the query representation. However, the independent role that each dimension plays is unclear, as both are often entangled in empirical evaluations. In this paper, we address this gap by systematically studying how the choice of feedback source and feedback model impact PRF effectiveness through controlled experimentation. Across 13 low-resource BEIR tasks with five LLM PRF methods, our results show: (1) the choice of feedback model can play a critical role in PRF effectiveness; (2) feedback derived solely from LLM-generated text provides the most cost-effective solution; and (3) feedback derived from the corpus is most beneficial when utilizing candidate documents from a strong first-stage retriever. Together, our findings provide a better understanding of which elements in the PRF design space are most important.

item.title: P-GSVC: Layered Progressive 2D Gaussian Splatting for Scalable Image and Video
item.content:encoded: Gaussian splatting has emerged as a competitive explicit representation for image and video reconstruction. In this work, we present P-GSVC, the first layered progressive 2D Gaussian splatting framework that provides a unified solution for scalable Gaussian representation in both images and videos. P-GSVC organizes 2D Gaussian splats into a base layer and successive enhancement layers, enabling coarse-to-fine reconstructions. To effectively optimize this layered representation, we propose a joint training strategy that simultaneously updates Gaussians across layers, aligning their optimization trajectories to ensure inter-layer compatibility and a stable progressive reconstruction. P-GSVC supports scalability in terms of both quality and resolution. Our experiments show that the joint training strategy can gain up to 1.9 dB improvement in PSNR for video and 2.6 dB improvement in PSNR for image when compared to methods that perform sequential layer-wise training. Project page: https://longanwang-cs.github.io/PGSVC-webpage/

item.title: 基于最优控制理论的Transformer训练方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种符合结构约束的Transformer训练方法。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于最优控制理论的Transformer训练方法，考虑了执行过程中的输入独立性、问题的集合控制性质以及位置依赖性。将Transformer建模为具有共享动作的离散时间受控粒子系统，通过将其提升到概率测度空间，构建了一个完全可观测的马尔可夫决策过程。通过动态规划原理，在适度的紧致性假设下证明了全局最优策略的存在性，并展示了闭环策略与初始分布相关的开环策略等价性。进一步提出三重量化训练过程，确保模型在原始训练问题中接近最优。最后，验证了提升模型的稳定性与经验一致性。

item.title: 视觉语言模型在驾驶辅助中的时序推理能力研究
item.content:encoded:
研究VLM在驾驶辅助中是否具备时序推理能力。



摘要： 本文探讨了视觉语言模型（VLM）作为驾驶助手时，是否能够基于观察信息进行时间上连贯的推理，还是仅依赖训练时的记忆模式。尽管已有研究将VLM应用于自动驾驶，但大多侧重于场景理解和指令生成，隐含假设强视觉理解自然带来可靠未来推理。然而，本文指出VLM在响应一致性与时间推理方面存在局限，表现为输入微小变化导致答案不一致，或无法合理推断序列事件。此外，具有强大视觉理解能力的模型在需要时间推理的任务中未必表现最佳，表明其过度依赖预训练模式而非建模时间动态。为解决这些问题，作者引入了一个名为FutureVQA的人工标注基准数据集，并提出一种基于思维链的自监督调优方法，以提升响应一致性和时间推理能力。

item.title: CyberThreat-Eval：提升CTI报告生成的LLM评估基准
item.content:encoded:
提出CyberThreat-Eval基准以评估LLM在CTI工作流中的表现。



摘要： 本文介绍了CyberThreat-Eval，一个基于世界领先公司日常CTI工作流构建的专家标注基准。该基准针对现有LLM评估方法的不足，提供覆盖三阶段CTI流程（筛选、深度搜索、情报撰写）的实际任务，并采用以分析师为中心的指标，如事实准确性、内容质量和操作成本。实验表明，当前LLM在处理复杂细节和区分正确与错误信息方面存在明显局限，因此CTI流程结合了外部真实数据库和人工专家知识。该代码已在GitHub和HuggingFace上开源。

item.title: 基于密集视觉表示的导航世界模型研究
item.content:encoded:
提出RAE-NWM模型提升导航任务的结构稳定性和动作准确性。



摘要： 本文研究了视觉导航中世界模型的应用，指出传统方法在变分自编码器的压缩潜在空间中可能丢失细粒度结构信息，影响控制精度。通过线性动力学探测发现DINOv2特征具有更强的线性可预测性，由此提出RAE-NWM模型，在密集视觉表示空间中建模导航动态。该模型采用条件扩散Transformer和分离时间驱动门控模块，有效提升序列滚动的结构稳定性和动作准确性，对下游规划与导航有显著帮助。

item.title: KV-Lock：一种无需训练的视频编辑框架
item.content:encoded:
KV-Lock提升视频前景质量同时保持背景一致性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为KV-Lock的无需训练的视频编辑框架，旨在解决在增强前景质量的同时保持背景一致性的难题。该方法通过分析去噪预测的方差来量化生成多样性，并据此动态调整缓存背景键值对与新生成键值对的融合比例以及CFG尺度。当检测到幻觉风险时，KV-Lock会增强背景键值对锁定并增强条件引导，从而减少伪影并提高生成质量。实验表明，该方法在多种视频编辑任务中均优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于开源大语言模型的放射科报告纵向信息提取系统
item.content:encoded:
开源LLM实现放射科报告中肿瘤信息的高效提取。



摘要： 本文提出一个完全开源、可本地部署的放射科报告纵向信息提取管道，采用qwen2.5-72b模型，依据RECIST标准提取目标病灶、非目标病灶和新病灶数据。在50对荷兰胸部/腹部CT报告的评估中，系统在目标病灶、非目标病灶和新病灶的属性级准确率分别达到93.7%、94.9%和94.0%。该方法展示了开源LLM在多时间点肿瘤学任务中具有临床意义的性能，同时保障了数据隐私和可重复性，为从常规临床文本中提取结构化纵向数据提供了可行方案。

item.title: AlphaEvolve提升五种经典Ramsey数下界
item.content:encoded:
AlphaEvolve改进了五个经典Ramsey数的下界。



摘要： 本文介绍了AlphaEvolve这一基于大语言模型的代码变异代理，成功提升了五个经典Ramsey数的下界，包括R(3,13)、R(3,18)、R(4,13)、R(4,14)和R(4,15)。这些结果比之前记录的数值更高，同时AlphaEvolve还恢复了所有已知精确的Ramsey数下界，并在许多其他情况下达到了最佳下界。与以往需要定制搜索算法不同，AlphaEvolve是一个统一的元算法，能够生成适用于所有结果的搜索算法。

item.title: PromptDLA：一种面向文档布局分析的领域感知提示方法
item.content:encoded:
PromptDLA通过领域感知提示提升文档布局分析性能。



摘要： 本文提出PromptDLA，一种面向文档布局分析的领域感知提示方法。该方法通过定制化提示引导模型关注不同领域的关键特征，从而提升模型在多领域数据上的泛化能力。实验表明，PromptDLA在多个公开数据集上均取得了最先进的性能。研究强调了在数据融合过程中考虑领域差异的重要性，并展示了如何利用描述性知识作为提示来增强模型表现。

item.title: 面向极端边缘设备的深度神经网络本地训练框架TrainDeeploy
item.content:encoded:
TrainDeeploy实现极端边缘设备上CNN与Transformer模型的高效本地训练。



摘要： 本文提出TrainDeeploy，一个统一高效推理与本地训练的框架，适用于超低功耗异构系统芯片（SoC）。该框架支持卷积神经网络（CNN）和Transformer模型，并采用选择性逐层微调和低秩适应（LoRA）等策略。在基于RISC-V的SoC上，实现了紧凑型卷积Transformer（CCT）的端到端本地微调，每秒可处理11张图像。实验表明，LoRA相比全反向传播可减少23%动态内存使用、15倍可训练参数和梯度数量，以及1.6倍内存传输量。TrainDeeploy在CCT和Deep-AE模型上分别达到4.6和13.4 FLOP/周期，扩展了现有框架对极端边缘平台的支持范围。

item.title: 多智能体视角视频理解基准与模型研究
item.content:encoded:
研究多智能体视角视频理解问题，提出MA-EgoQA基准和EgoMAS模型。



摘要： 随着具身模型的发展，未来人类将与多个具身AI代理协作。为提升人机交互效果，需同时解析多源感知信息并建立上下文关联。本文提出MA-EgoQA基准，用于评估多视角长时序视频理解能力，并引入EgoMAS模型进行系统级记忆构建。实验表明当前方法难以有效处理多视角视频流，凸显了系统级理解的重要性。该工作为未来研究提供了数据和方法支持。

item.title: 深度学习与计算机视觉在自动驾驶汽车中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
深度学习提升自动驾驶感知与决策能力。



摘要： 本文探讨了深度学习和计算机视觉技术在自动驾驶汽车中的关键作用，重点介绍了通过预训练和定制神经网络提升交通标志识别、车辆检测、车道检测及行为克隆等任务性能的方法。研究结合几何变换、颜色增强、图像归一化和迁移学习等技术，应用于多种数据集以验证模型效果。结果表明，这些方法有效解决了自动驾驶中的多项挑战，为提高系统鲁棒性和可靠性提供了新思路，对推动更安全高效的自动驾驶技术发展具有重要意义。

item.title: 基于双路径架构的4D雷达场景理解模型DRIFT
item.content:encoded:
DRIFT通过双路径架构提升4D雷达的场景理解能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为DRIFT的模型，用于提升4D雷达在自动驾驶系统中的场景理解能力。该模型采用双路径架构，分别通过点路径和柱体路径提取局部和全局特征，并通过多阶段特征共享层进行融合，以充分利用两种表示。DRIFT在VoD数据集和内部数据集上进行了评估，结果表明其在目标检测和自由道路估计任务中优于现有基线方法，例如在VoD数据集上的mAP达到52.6%，高于CenterPoint的45.4%。

item.title: 面向地球观测的多模态模型评估基准OmniEarth
item.content:encoded:
OmniEarth为遥感视觉语言模型提供全面评估基准。



摘要： 本文提出OmniEarth，一个用于评估遥感视觉语言模型（RSVLMs）的基准测试平台。该基准涵盖感知、推理和鲁棒性三个能力维度，包含28个精细任务，支持多种任务形式，如多项选择VQA和开放式VQA。OmniEarth采用盲测协议和语义一致性要求以减少语言偏差，并包含9,275张高质量图像及44,210条人工验证指令。实验表明现有模型在复杂地理任务中仍存在明显不足，OmniEarth已公开供研究使用。

item.title: 揭示深度神经网络中缺失概念的可解释性人工智能方法
item.content:encoded:
研究提出改进XAI方法以识别神经网络中的缺失概念。



摘要： 本文探讨了可解释人工智能（XAI）在理解深度神经网络行为中的应用，特别关注于传统方法未能有效揭示的‘编码缺失’现象。现有XAI技术主要聚焦于输入与神经元激活之间的关系，而忽略了缺失概念可能引发的神经激活。研究指出，这类缺失概念在模型中普遍存在，且主流XAI方法难以识别。为此，作者提出了两种简单扩展方法，用于改进归因分析和特征可视化技术，以揭示这些缺失但重要的概念。实验表明，这些方法不仅能够有效识别缺失概念，还能提升模型的公平性和性能。

item.title: 基于诊断的生成引擎优化方法提升内容引用率
item.content:encoded:
提出诊断式GEO方法，提升AI响应中内容引用率。



摘要： 本文提出一种新的生成引擎优化（GEO）方法，通过诊断文档未被引用的原因并针对性干预，提高内容在AI生成响应中的可见性。该方法包含三个核心部分：首个引用失败模式分类体系、AgentGEO智能系统用于诊断和修复，以及以文档为中心的评估基准。实验表明，AgentGEO在仅修改5%内容的情况下，引用率提升超过40%，优于基线方法。研究还发现通用优化可能损害长尾内容，部分文档的问题无法仅靠优化解决，这对AI信息访问的公平性具有重要意义。

item.title: 多模态情感计算中的缺失率基准与公平性分析
item.content:encoded:
研究提出MissBench基准，评估多模态情感模型在数据缺失情况下的公平性和优化平衡。



摘要： 本文介绍了MissBench，一个用于多模态情感任务的基准和框架，旨在标准化共享和不平衡缺失率协议。该研究定义了两个诊断指标：Modality Equity Index (MEI) 和 Modality Learning Index (MLI)，分别用于衡量不同模态在缺失情况下的公平贡献以及训练过程中的优化不平衡。实验表明，在共享缺失率下表现稳健的模型，在不平衡条件下仍可能表现出显著的模态不公平和优化不平衡。MissBench及其指标为评估和测试真实场景中不完整模态设置下的多模态情感模型提供了实用工具。

item.title: 基于小波引导的三模态医学图像融合与超分辨率方法
item.content:encoded:
提出TriFusionSR框架，提升三模态医学图像融合与超分辨率效果。



摘要： 本文针对多模态医学图像融合中分辨率退化和模态差异的问题，提出TriFusionSR框架，结合小波变换和扩散模型实现三模态图像的联合融合与超分辨率。该方法通过2D离散小波变换分解多模态特征，引入校准策略和自适应空间-频率融合模块，有效提升图像质量，在多个上采样尺度下均取得显著性能提升。

item.title: 多模态大语言模型的模态差距分析与改进方法
item.content:encoded:
研究多模态模型在图像文本理解中的性能差异及提升策略。



摘要： 本文系统分析了多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在处理图像文本时的性能下降问题，通过在七种基准测试中评估七个模型，发现其表现受任务和数据类型影响显著。例如，数学任务在合成文本中性能下降超过60分，而真实文档图像表现接近或优于文本模式。研究还发现字体和分辨率等渲染因素对准确率有显著影响，并通过错误分析揭示图像模式会放大阅读错误但不影响知识和推理错误。基于此，作者提出一种自蒸馏方法，有效提升了图像模式下的性能，使GSM8K任务准确率从30.71%提升至92.72%。

item.title: 基于专家一致性学习的多模态医学图像分割方法
item.content:encoded:
提出CLoE框架提升多模态医学图像分割鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文针对多模态医学图像分割中模态缺失导致的专家预测不一致问题，提出了一种基于专家一致性学习的框架CLoE。该框架通过双分支一致性学习目标，增强专家预测的一致性，特别是在关键病灶区域。同时，利用轻量级门控网络将一致性评分映射为模态可靠性权重，实现特征重校准。实验表明，CLoE在BraTS 2020和MSD前列腺数据集上优于现有方法，具有良好的跨数据集泛化能力和对关键结构的鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于HR-pQCT的多区域自动分类框架在骨质疏松检测中的应用
item.content:encoded:
利用HR-pQCT和放射组学特征进行骨质疏松自动分类。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于高分辨率外周定量计算机断层扫描（HR-pQCT）的全自动骨质疏松分类框架。该方法通过SegFormer模型对胫骨、腓骨及周围软组织进行分割，并提取939个放射组学特征，经过降维后训练机器学习分类器。实验表明，使用肌腱组织特征在图像级别达到80.08%的准确率和0.85的AUROC，优于传统基于骨密度的方法。在患者层面，结合软组织放射组学特征可将AUROC提升至0.875，显示出整合多组织评估在骨质疏松检测中的重要性。

item.title: 基于视频的风驱动物体动力学建模方法DiffWind
item.content:encoded:
DiffWind通过物理信息框架实现风与物体动态建模和模拟。



摘要： 本文提出DiffWind，一种融合风-物体相互作用建模、视频重建和前向仿真的物理信息微分框架。该方法将风表示为基于网格的物理场，物体则通过3D高斯点云表示，并利用材料点法（MPM）建模其交互。通过可微渲染和仿真联合优化时空风场与物体运动，结合格子玻尔兹曼方法（LBM）确保物理合理性。该方法不仅支持风驱动场景的重建，还可在新风况下进行前向仿真，拓展了风驱动物体建模的应用。实验表明，该方法在重建精度和仿真保真度上优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于点线结构的鲁棒立体视觉惯性里程计系统
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型点线融合VIO系统，提升低纹理和光照变化场景下的定位精度与鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于点线结构的立体视觉惯性里程计（VIO）系统，旨在解决低纹理场景和突发光照变化下特征点稀疏不稳定的问题。该系统通过为线段分配深度描述符，并采用熵正则化最优传输方法实现全局一致的匹配，从而在存在歧义、异常值和部分观测的情况下仍能保持稳定。描述符计算无需训练，通过采样和池化网络特征图生成。此外，研究分析了线测量噪声的影响，并引入可靠性自适应加权以优化约束影响。实验结果表明，该系统在EuRoC和UMA-VI数据集以及真实环境中均表现出更高的精度和鲁棒性，同时保持实时性能。

item.title: Sentinel AI系统提升远程患者监测的紧急分诊效率
item.content:encoded:
Sentinel AI在RPM分诊中表现优于人类医生，提高紧急情况识别率。



摘要： 本文介绍了Sentinel AI系统，该系统利用Model Context Protocol (MCP)对远程患者监测（RPM）数据进行智能分诊。通过21种临床工具和多步骤推理，Sentinel在紧急情况识别和所有可操作警报的敏感性上分别达到95.8%和88.5%，且分类准确率高达69.4%。与人类医生相比，Sentinel在紧急敏感性和可操作敏感性方面均表现更优。尽管存在轻微过度分诊现象，但独立医生评估显示其在严重情况下的分诊准确性很高。Sentinel具有高自一致性，成本仅为0.34美元/分诊，为大规模应用提供了可行性。

item.title: SpaceSense-Bench：面向空间感知的大规模多模态基准数据集
item.content:encoded:
SpaceSense-Bench提供高精度多模态航天器感知数据，助力空间操作研究。



摘要： 本文介绍了SpaceSense-Bench，一个大规模多模态航天器感知基准数据集，包含136个卫星模型和约70GB数据。数据集包含同步的RGB图像、毫米级深度图、256束LiDAR点云以及像素和点级别的7类语义标签和精确的6-DoF姿态标注。该数据集通过Unreal Engine 5构建的高保真空间模拟生成，并经过自动化数据采集与质量控制流程。文章对五种代表性任务进行了基准测试，发现小部件感知和零样本泛化仍是当前方法的瓶颈，而增加训练卫星数量可显著提升性能，凸显大规模多样化数据集在空间感知研究中的价值。

item.title: RubiCap：基于强化学习的密集图像描述生成方法
item.content:encoded:
RubiCap通过强化学习提升图像描述生成质量与多样性。



摘要： 本文提出RubiCap，一种基于强化学习的密集图像描述生成框架。该方法通过LLM编写的评分标准提取细粒度奖励信号，增强模型生成描述的多样性和泛化能力。RubiCap在多个基准测试中表现优异，尤其在CapArena和CaptionQA任务中超越了监督蒸馏、传统RL方法及人类专家标注。实验表明，其小型模型（如3B）甚至优于大型模型，证明了该方法在预训练视觉语言模型中的有效性。

item.title: 提升足部运动重建的FootMR方法研究
item.content:encoded:
FootMR提升视频中足部运动重建精度。



摘要： 本文提出FootMR方法，用于改进从视频中恢复足部精细运动。现有方法在足部运动重建上存在不足，主要由于训练数据标注不准确和运动多样性不足。FootMR通过将2D足部关键点序列提升到3D，避免直接使用图像输入，从而利用大规模运动捕捉数据。该方法通过引入膝关节和足部运动作为上下文，仅预测残差足部运动，并采用全局旋转表示和数据增强来提高极端足部姿势的泛化能力。为评估足部运动重建，作者还引入了MOOF数据集。实验表明，FootMR在多个数据集上优于现有方法，特别是在MOYO数据集上将踝关节角度误差降低了30%。

item.title: 大型语言模型在常识理解上的局限性研究
item.content:encoded:
LLMs在道德推理中忽视常识矛盾，需提升常识理解能力。



摘要： 本文研究发现，当前大型语言模型（LLMs）倾向于优先进行道德推理，而忽视常识理解。为验证这一现象，作者构建了CoMoral数据集，包含嵌入道德困境中的常识矛盾。通过评估十种不同规模的LLMs，发现现有模型在没有先验信号的情况下难以识别这些矛盾。此外，研究还发现模型对次要角色而非主要叙述者所提出的常识矛盾更敏感。该研究强调需要改进训练方法以增强LLMs的常识推理能力。

item.title: 基于先验地图的具身导航方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出PM-Nav方法提升功能建筑导航性能。



摘要： 本文针对功能建筑中因特征高度相似导致的语言驱动具身导航难题，提出了一种基于先验地图的导航方法（PM-Nav）。该方法通过将环境地图转化为导航友好的语义先验地图，设计了带有注释先验地图的分层思维链提示模板，并构建了多模型协同动作输出机制，以实现精准路径规划和定位控制。实验结果表明，PM-Nav在模拟和真实环境中分别比SG-Nav和InstructNav提升了511%、1175%和650%、400%，展示了其在功能建筑导航中的巨大潜力。

item.title: 基于显式高斯表示的零样本MRI超分辨率方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效MRI超分辨率框架，平衡数据需求与计算效率。



摘要： 本文针对高分辨率磁共振成像（MRI）在临床诊断中的应用难题，提出一种基于显式高斯表示的零样本MRI超分辨率方法。该方法通过嵌入组织物理特性到高斯参数中，减少可学习参数数量，同时保持MRI信号保真度。结合物理驱动的体积渲染策略和基于砖块的并行化渲染方案，有效降低训练与推理成本。实验表明，该方法在两个公开MRI数据集上均表现出优越的重建质量和效率，具有良好的临床应用前景。

item.title: 基于滑片难度的多实例学习在前列腺癌分级中的应用
item.content:encoded:
引入滑片难度概念提升前列腺癌分级分类性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于专家与非专家病理医生之间分歧的Whole Slide Difficulty（WSD）概念，并设计了两种方法来利用WSD提升多实例学习（MIL）在前列腺癌Gleason分级中的表现。实验结果表明，结合WSD进行训练可有效提高不同特征编码器和MIL方法的分类性能，尤其在高Gleason等级（即较差诊断）中效果更显著。
