item.title: Ego: Embedding-Guided Personalization of Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: AI assistants that support humans in daily life are becoming increasingly feasible, driven by the rapid advancements in multimodal language models. A key challenge lies in overcoming the generic nature of these models to deliver personalized experiences. Existing approaches to personalizing large vision language models often rely on additional training stages, which limit generality and scalability, or on engineered pipelines with external pre-trained modules, which hinder deployment efficiency. In this work, we propose an efficient personalization method that leverages the model's inherent ability to capture personalized concepts. Specifically, we extract visual tokens that predominantly represent the target concept by utilizing the model's internal attention mechanisms. These tokens serve as a memory of that specific concept, enabling the model to recall and describe it when it appears in test images. We conduct a comprehensive and unified evaluation of our approach and SOTA methods across various personalization settings including single-concept, multi-concept, and video personalization, demonstrating strong performance gains with minimal personalization overhead.

item.title: Flow Field Reconstruction via Voronoi-Enhanced Physics-Informed Neural Networks with End-to-End Sensor Placement Optimization
item.content:encoded: (short version abstract, full in article)High-fidelity flow field reconstruction is important in fluid dynamics, but it is challenged by sparse and spatiotemporally incomplete sensor measurements, as well as failures of pre-deployed measurement points that can invalidate pre-trained reconstruction models. Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) alleviate dependence on large labeled datasets by incorporating governing physics, yet sensor placement optimization, a key factor in reconstruction accuracy and robustness, remains underexplored. In this study, we propose a PINN with Voronoi-enhanced Sensor Optimization (VSOPINN). VSOPINN enables differentiable soft Voronoi construction for sparse sensor data rasterization, end-to-end fusion of centroidal Voronoi tessellation (CVT) with PINNs for adaptive sensor placement, and unified layout optimization for multi-condition flow reconstruction through a shared encoder-multi-decoder architecture. We validate VSOPINN on three representative problems: lid-driven cavity flow, vascular flow, and annular rotating flow. Results show that VSOPINN significantly improves reconstruction accuracy across different Reynolds numbers, adaptively learns effective sensor layouts, and remains robust under partial sensor failure. The study clarifies the intrinsic relationship between sensor placement and reconstruction precision in PINN-based flow field reconstruction.

item.title: Time, Identity and Consciousness in Language Model Agents
item.content:encoded: Machine consciousness evaluations mostly see behavior. For language model agents that behavior is language and tool use. That lets an agent say the right things about itself even when the constraints that should make those statements matter are not jointly present at decision time. We apply Stack Theory's temporal gap to scaffold trajectories. This separates ingredient-wise occurrence within an evaluation window from co-instantiation at a single objective step. We then instantiate Stack Theory's Arpeggio and Chord postulates on grounded identity statements. This yields two persistence scores that can be computed from instrumented scaffold traces. We connect these scores to five operational identity metrics and map common scaffolds into an identity morphospace that exposes predictable tradeoffs. The result is a conservative toolkit for identity evaluation. It separates talking like a stable self from being organized like one.

item.title: Quantifying and extending the coverage of spatial categorization data sets
item.content:encoded: Variation in spatial categorization across languages is often studied by eliciting human labels for the relations depicted in a set of scenes known as the Topological Relations Picture Series (TRPS). We demonstrate that labels generated by large language models (LLMs) align relatively well with human labels, and show how LLM-generated labels can help to decide which scenes and languages to add to existing spatial data sets. To illustrate our approach we extend the TRPS by adding 42 new scenes, and show that this extension achieves better coverage of the space of possible scenes than two previous extensions of the TRPS. Our results provide a foundation for scaling towards spatial data sets with dozens of languages and hundreds of scenes.

item.title: EvoDriveVLA: Evolving Autonomous Driving Vision-Language-Action Model via Collaborative Perception-Planning Distillation
item.content:encoded: Vision-Language-Action models have shown great promise for autonomous driving, yet they suffer from degraded perception after unfreezing the visual encoder and struggle with accumulated instability in long-term planning. To address these challenges, we propose EvoDriveVLA-a novel collaborative perception-planning distillation framework that integrates self-anchored perceptual constraints and oracle-guided trajectory optimization. Specifically, self-anchored visual distillation leverages self-anchor teacher to deliver visual anchoring constraints, regularizing student representations via trajectory-guided key-region awareness. In parallel, oracle-guided trajectory distillation employs a future-aware oracle teacher with coarse-to-fine trajectory refinement and Monte Carlo dropout sampling to produce high-quality trajectory candidates, thereby selecting the optimal trajectory to guide the student's prediction. EvoDriveVLA achieves SOTA performance in open-loop evaluation and significantly enhances performance in closed-loop evaluation. Our code is available at: https://github.com/hey-cjj/EvoDriveVLA.

item.title: Decoupling Reasoning and Confidence: Resurrecting Calibration in Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards
item.content:encoded: Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) significantly enhances large language models (LLMs) reasoning but severely suffers from calibration degeneration, where models become excessively over-confident in incorrect answers. Previous studies devote to directly incorporating calibration objective into existing optimization target. However, our theoretical analysis demonstrates that there exists a fundamental gradient conflict between the optimization for maximizing policy accuracy and minimizing calibration error. Building on this insight, we propose DCPO, a simple yet effective framework that systematically decouples reasoning and calibration objectives. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our DCPO not only preserves accuracy on par with GRPO but also achieves the best calibration performance and substantially mitigates the over-confidence issue. Our study provides valuable insights and practical solution for more reliable LLM deployment.

item.title: Adaptive Active Learning for Online Reliability Prediction of Satellite Electronics
item.content:encoded: Accurate on-orbit reliability prediction for satellite electronics is often hindered by limited data availability, varying operational conditions, and considerable unit-to-unit variability. To overcome these obstacles, this paper proposes a novel integrated online reliability prediction framework. The main contributions are twofold. First, a Wiener process-based degradation model is developed, incorporating a generalized Arrhenius link function, individual random effects, and spatial correlations among adjacent units. A customized maximum likelihood estimation method is further devised to facilitate efficient and accurate parameter inference. Second, a two-stage active learning sampling scheme is designed to adaptively enhance prediction accuracy. This strategy initially selects representative units based on spatial configuration, and subsequently determines optimal sampling times using a comprehensive criterion that balances unit-specific information, model uncertainty, and degradation dynamics. Numerical experiments and a practical case study from the Tiangong space station demonstrate that the proposed method markedly improves reliability prediction accuracy while significantly reducing data requirements, offering an efficient solution for the prognostic and health management of complex satellite electronic systems.

item.title: Routing without Forgetting
item.content:encoded: Continual learning in transformers is commonly addressed through parameter-efficient adaptation: prompts, adapters, or LoRA modules are specialized per task while the backbone remains frozen. Although effective in controlled multi-epoch settings, these approaches rely on gradual gradient-based specialization and struggle in Online Continual Learning (OCL), where data arrive as a non-stationary stream and each sample may be observed only once. We recast continual learning in transformers as a routing problem: under strict online constraints, the model must dynamically select the appropriate representational subspace for each input without explicit task identifiers or repeated optimization. We thus introduce Routing without Forgetting (RwF), a transformer architecture augmented with energy-based associative retrieval layers inspired by Modern Hopfield Networks. Instead of storing or merging task-specific prompts, RwF generates dynamic prompts through single-step associative retrieval over the transformer token embeddings at each layer. Retrieval corresponds to the closed-form minimization of a strictly convex free-energy functional, enabling input-conditioned routing within each forward pass, independently of iterative gradient refinement. Across challenging class-incremental benchmarks, RwF improves over existing prompt-based methods. On Split-ImageNet-R and Split-ImageNet-S, RwF outperforms prior prompt-based approaches by a large margin, even in few-shot learning regimes. These results indicate that embedding energy-based associative routing directly within the transformer backbone provides a principled and effective foundation for OCL.

item.title: Progressive Representation Learning for Multimodal Sentiment Analysis with Incomplete Modalities
item.content:encoded: Multimodal Sentiment Analysis (MSA) seeks to infer human emotions by integrating textual, acoustic, and visual cues. However, existing approaches often rely on all modalities are completeness, whereas real-world applications frequently encounter noise, hardware failures, or privacy restrictions that result in missing modalities. There exists a significant feature misalignment between incomplete and complete modalities, and directly fusing them may even distort the well-learned representations of the intact modalities. To this end, we propose PRLF, a Progressive Representation Learning Framework designed for MSA under uncertain missing-modality conditions. PRLF introduces an Adaptive Modality Reliability Estimator (AMRE), which dynamically quantifies the reliability of each modality using recognition confidence and Fisher information to determine the dominant modality. In addition, the Progressive Interaction (ProgInteract) module iteratively aligns the other modalities with the dominant one, thereby enhancing cross-modal consistency while suppressing noise. Extensive experiments on CMU-MOSI, CMU-MOSEI, and SIMS verify that PRLF outperforms state-of-the-art methods across both inter- and intra-modality missing scenarios, demonstrating its robustness and generalization capability.

item.title: MM-Zero: Self-Evolving Multi-Model Vision Language Models From Zero Data
item.content:encoded: Self-evolving has emerged as a key paradigm for improving foundational models such as Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision Language Models (VLMs) with minimal human intervention. While recent approaches have demonstrated that LLM agents can self-evolve from scratch with little to no data, VLMs introduce an additional visual modality that typically requires at least some seed data, such as images, to bootstrap the self-evolution process. In this work, we present Multi-model Multimodal Zero (MM-Zero), the first RL-based framework to achieve zero-data self-evolution for VLM reasoning. Moving beyond prior dual-role (Proposer and Solver) setups, MM-Zero introduces a multi-role self-evolving training framework comprising three specialized roles: a Proposer that generates abstract visual concepts and formulates questions; a Coder that translates these concepts into executable code (e.g., Python, SVG) to render visual images; and a Solver that performs multimodal reasoning over the generated visual content. All three roles are initialized from the same base model and trained using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), with carefully designed reward mechanisms that integrate execution feedback, visual verification, and difficulty balancing. Our experiments show that MM-Zero improves VLM reasoning performance across a wide range of multimodal benchmarks. MM-Zero establishes a scalable path toward self-evolving multi-model systems for multimodal models, extending the frontier of self-improvement beyond the conventional two-model paradigm.

item.title: Differentiable Stochastic Traffic Dynamics: Physics-Informed Generative Modelling in Transportation
item.content:encoded: Macroscopic traffic flow is stochastic, but the physics-informed deep learning methods currently used in transportation literature embed deterministic PDEs and produce point-valued outputs; the stochasticity of the governing dynamics plays no role in the learned representation. This work develops a framework in which the physics constraint itself is distributional and directly derived from stochastic traffic-flow dynamics. Starting from an Ito-type Lighthill-Whitham-Richards model with Brownian forcing, we derive a one-point forward equation for the marginal traffic density at each spatial location. The spatial coupling induced by the conservation law appears as an explicit conditional drift term, which makes the closure requirement transparent. Based on this formulation, we derive an equivalent deterministic Probability Flow ODE that is pointwise evaluable and differentiable once a closure is specified. Incorporating this as a physics constraint, we then propose a score network with an advection-closure module, trainable by denoising score matching together with a Fokker-Planck residual loss. The resulting model targets a data-conditioned density distribution, from which point estimates, credible intervals, and congestion-risk measures can be computed. The framework provides a basis for distributional traffic-state estimation and for stochastic fundamental-diagram analysis in a physics-informed generative setting.

item.title: MedKCO: Medical Vision-Language Pretraining via Knowledge-Driven Cognitive Orchestration
item.content:encoded: Medical vision-language pretraining (VLP) models have recently been investigated for their generalization to diverse downstream tasks. However, current medical VLP methods typically force the model to learn simple and complex concepts simultaneously. This anti-cognitive process leads to suboptimal feature representations, especially under distribution shift. To address this limitation, we propose a Knowledge-driven Cognitive Orchestration for Medical VLP (MedKCO) that involves both the ordering of the pretraining data and the learning objective of vision-language contrast. Specifically, we design a two level curriculum by incorporating diagnostic sensitivity and intra-class sample representativeness for the ordering of the pretraining data. Moreover, considering the inter-class similarity of medical images, we introduce a self-paced asymmetric contrastive loss to dynamically adjust the participation of the pretraining objective. We evaluate the proposed pretraining method on three medical imaging scenarios in multiple vision-language downstream tasks, and compare it with several curriculum learning methods. Extensive experiments show that our method significantly surpasses all baselines. https://github.com/Mr-Talon/MedKCO.

item.title: PanoAffordanceNet: Towards Holistic Affordance Grounding in 360° Indoor Environments
item.content:encoded: Global perception is essential for embodied agents in 360° spaces, yet current affordance grounding remains largely object-centric and restricted to perspective views. To bridge this gap, we introduce a novel task: Holistic Affordance Grounding in 360° Indoor Environments. This task faces unique challenges, including severe geometric distortions from Equirectangular Projection (ERP), semantic dispersion, and cross-scale alignment difficulties. We propose PanoAffordanceNet, an end-to-end framework featuring a Distortion-Aware Spectral Modulator (DASM) for latitude-dependent calibration and an Omni-Spherical Densification Head (OSDH) to restore topological continuity from sparse activations. By integrating multi-level constraints comprising pixel-wise, distributional, and region-text contrastive objectives, our framework effectively suppresses semantic drift under low supervision. Furthermore, we construct 360-AGD, the first high-quality panoramic affordance grounding dataset. Extensive experiments demonstrate that PanoAffordanceNet significantly outperforms existing methods, establishing a solid baseline for scene-level perception in embodied intelligence. The source code and benchmark dataset will be made publicly available at https://github.com/GL-ZHU925/PanoAffordanceNet.

item.title: ConfCtrl: Enabling Precise Camera Control in Video Diffusion via Confidence-Aware Interpolation
item.content:encoded: We address the challenge of novel view synthesis from only two input images under large viewpoint changes. Existing regression-based methods lack the capacity to reconstruct unseen regions, while camera-guided diffusion models often deviate from intended trajectories due to noisy point cloud projections or insufficient conditioning from camera poses. To address these issues, we propose ConfCtrl, a confidence-aware video interpolation framework that enables diffusion models to follow prescribed camera poses while completing unseen regions. ConfCtrl initializes the diffusion process by combining a confidence-weighted projected point cloud latent with noise as the conditioning input. It then applies a Kalman-inspired predict-update mechanism, treating the projected point cloud as a noisy measurement and using learned residual corrections to balance pose-driven predictions with noisy geometric observations. This allows the model to rely on reliable projections while down-weighting uncertain regions, yielding stable, geometry-aware generation. Experiments on multiple datasets show that ConfCtrl produces geometrically consistent and visually plausible novel views, effectively reconstructing occluded regions under large viewpoint changes.

item.title: Efficient Reasoning at Fixed Test-Time Cost via Length-Aware Attention Priors and Gain-Aware Training
item.content:encoded: We study efficient reasoning under tight compute. We ask how to make structured, correct decisions without increasing test time cost. We add two training only components to small and medium Transformers that also transfer to broader differentiable optimizers. First, a length aware attention prior built via fuzzy regime position alignment, RPA, yields a normalized pre softmax bias that guides attention like a structured regularizer while adding no new inference parameters. Second, a minimal gain aware controller, Guardian, nudges attention sharpness only when validation improvements warrant it, following a two timescale policy gradient view of nonconvex optimization. It is disabled at inference. A KL perspective shows softmax of z plus log pi as MAP with KL regularization, grounding the prior in a principled objective. Under strict compute parity on WikiText 2, we reduce validation cross entropy while matching baseline latency and memory. At inference, we add a precomputed, cached prior B of T as a single additive bias per head. The controller does not run. In practice, this incurs negligible overhead, a cached bias add per head, with no measurable p50 latency shift. Our results suggest that length aware priors and late phase gain control preserve scarce improvements, especially in long span, noisy logit regimes, while keeping test time costs effectively unchanged.

item.title: A Guideline-Aware AI Agent for Zero-Shot Target Volume Auto-Delineation
item.content:encoded: Delineating the clinical target volume (CTV) in radiotherapy involves complex margins constrained by tumor location and anatomical barriers. While deep learning models automate this process, their rigid reliance on expert-annotated data requires costly retraining whenever clinical guidelines update. To overcome this limitation, we introduce OncoAgent, a novel guideline-aware AI agent framework that seamlessly converts textual clinical guidelines into three-dimensional target contours in a training-free manner. Evaluated on esophageal cancer cases, the agent achieves a zero-shot Dice similarity coefficient of 0.842 for the CTV and 0.880 for the planning target volume, demonstrating performance highly comparable to a fully supervised nnU-Net baseline. Notably, in a blinded clinical evaluation, physicians strongly preferred OncoAgent over the supervised baseline, rating it higher in guideline compliance, modification effort, and clinical acceptability. Furthermore, the framework generalizes zero-shot to alternative esophageal guidelines and other anatomical sites (e.g., prostate) without any retraining. Beyond mere volumetric overlap, our agent-based paradigm offers near-instantaneous adaptability to alternative guidelines, providing a scalable and transparent pathway toward interpretability in radiotherapy treatment planning.

item.title: EventVGGT: Exploring Cross-Modal Distillation for Consistent Event-based Depth Estimation
item.content:encoded: Event cameras offer superior sensitivity to high-speed motion and extreme lighting, making event-based monocular depth estimation a promising approach for robust 3D perception in challenging conditions. However, progress is severely hindered by the scarcity of dense depth annotations. While recent annotation-free approaches mitigate this by distilling knowledge from Vision Foundation Models (VFMs), a critical limitation persists: they process event streams as independent frames. By neglecting the inherent temporal continuity of event data, these methods fail to leverage the rich temporal priors encoded in VFMs, ultimately yielding temporally inconsistent and less accurate depth predictions. To address this, we introduce EventVGGT, a novel framework that explicitly models the event stream as a coherent video sequence. To the best of our knowledge, we are the first to distill spatio-temporal and multi-view geometric priors from the Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer (VGGT) into the event domain. We achieve this via a comprehensive tri-level distillation strategy: (i) Cross-Modal Feature Mixture (CMFM) bridges the modality gap at the output level by fusing RGB and event features to generate auxiliary depth predictions; (ii) Spatio-Temporal Feature Distillation (STFD) distills VGGT's powerful spatio-temporal representations at the feature level; and (iii) Temporal Consistency Distillation (TCD) enforces cross-frame coherence at the temporal level by aligning inter-frame depth changes. Extensive experiments demonstrate that EventVGGT consistently outperforms existing methods -- reducing the absolute mean depth error at 30m by over 53\% on EventScape (from 2.30 to 1.06) -- while exhibiting robust zero-shot generalization on the unseen DENSE and MVSEC datasets.

item.title: Surgical Repair of Collapsed Attention Heads in ALiBi Transformers
item.content:encoded: We identify a systematic attention collapse pathology in the BLOOM family of transformer language models, where ALiBi positional encoding causes 31-44% of attention heads to attend almost entirely to the beginning-of-sequence token. The collapse follows a predictable pattern across four model scales (560M to 7.1B parameters), concentrating in head indices where ALiBi's slope schedule imposes the steepest distance penalties. We introduce surgical reinitialization: targeted Q/K/V reinitialization with zeroed output projections and gradient-masked freezing of all non-surgical parameters. Applied to BLOOM-1b7 on a single consumer GPU, the technique recovers 98.7% operational head capacity (242 to 379 of 384 heads) in two passes. A controlled comparison with C4 training data confirms that reinitialization -- not corpus content -- drives recovery, and reveals two distinct post-surgical phenomena: early global functional redistribution that improves the model, and late local degradation that accumulates under noisy training signal. An extended experiment reinitializing mostly-healthy heads alongside collapsed ones produces a model that transiently outperforms stock BLOOM-1b7 by 25% on training perplexity (12.70 vs. 16.99), suggesting that pretrained attention configurations are suboptimal local minima. Code, checkpoints, and diagnostic tools are released as open-source software.

item.title: Test-time Ego-Exo-centric Adaptation for Action Anticipation via Multi-Label Prototype Growing and Dual-Clue Consistency
item.content:encoded: Efficient adaptation between Egocentric (Ego) and Exocentric (Exo) views is crucial for applications such as human-robot cooperation. However, the success of most existing Ego-Exo adaptation methods relies heavily on target-view data for training, thereby increasing computational and data collection costs. In this paper, we make the first exploration of a Test-time Ego-Exo Adaptation for Action Anticipation (TE$^{2}$A$^{3}$) task, which aims to adjust the source-view-trained model online during test time to anticipate target-view actions. It is challenging for existing Test-Time Adaptation (TTA) methods to address this task due to the multi-action candidates and significant temporal-spatial inter-view gap. Hence, we propose a novel Dual-Clue enhanced Prototype Growing Network (DCPGN), which accumulates multi-label knowledge and integrates cross-modality clues for effective test-time Ego-Exo adaptation and action anticipation. Specifically, we propose a Multi-Label Prototype Growing Module (ML-PGM) to balance multiple positive classes via multi-label assignment and confidence-based reweighting for class-wise memory banks, which are updated by an entropy priority queue strategy. Then, the Dual-Clue Consistency Module (DCCM) introduces a lightweight narrator to generate textual clues indicating action progressions, which complement the visual clues containing various objects. Moreover, we constrain the inferred textual and visual logits to construct dual-clue consistency for temporally and spatially bridging Ego and Exo views. Extensive experiments on the newly proposed EgoMe-anti and the existing EgoExoLearn benchmarks show the effectiveness of our method, which outperforms related state-of-the-art methods by a large margin. Code is available at \href{https://github.com/ZhaofengSHI/DCPGN}{https://github.com/ZhaofengSHI/DCPGN}.

item.title: Build, Borrow, or Just Fine-Tune? A Political Scientist's Guide to Choosing NLP Models
item.content:encoded: Political scientists increasingly face a consequential choice when adopting natural language processing tools: build a domain-specific model from scratch, borrow and adapt an existing one, or simply fine-tune a general-purpose model on task data? Each approach occupies a different point on the spectrum of performance, cost, and required expertise, yet the discipline has offered little empirical guidance on how to navigate this trade-off. This paper provides such guidance. Using conflict event classification as a test case, I fine-tune ModernBERT on the Global Terrorism Database (GTD) to create Confli-mBERT and systematically compare it against ConfliBERT, a domain-specific pretrained model that represents the current gold standard. Confli-mBERT achieves 75.46% accuracy compared to ConfliBERT's 79.34%. Critically, the four-percentage-point gap is not uniform: on high-frequency attack types such as Bombing/Explosion (F1 = 0.95 vs. 0.96) and Kidnapping (F1 = 0.92 vs. 0.91), the models are nearly indistinguishable. Performance differences concentrate in rare event categories comprising fewer than 2% of all incidents. I use these findings to develop a practical decision framework for political scientists considering any NLP-assisted research task: when does the research question demand a specialized model, and when does an accessible fine-tuned alternative suffice? The answer, I argue, depends not on which model is "better" in the abstract, but on the specific intersection of class prevalence, error tolerance, and available resources. The model, training code, and data are publicly available on Hugging Face.

item.title: 基于学习的MIPS方法：SupportNet与KeyNet的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出一种学习方法预测MIPS最优解，提升计算效率。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于学习的MIPS方法，通过训练神经网络直接预测最佳匹配向量，以降低计算成本。核心思想是将MIPS值函数视为键集合的支撑函数，并利用输入凸神经网络（SupportNet）和向量值网络（KeyNet）进行建模。SupportNet通过梯度计算获取最优键，而KeyNet则直接回归最优键。实验表明该方法在特定查询分布下具有高匹配率，为数据库压缩提供了新思路。

item.title: 语言模型在条件句预设投射中的表现研究
item.content:encoded:
研究语言模型处理条件句预设问题的能力。



摘要： 本文探讨了语言模型在处理条件句中预设投射问题时的表现，这是一个在语用学中尚未解决的问题。作者将该现象重新定义为自然语言推理任务，并引入一个诊断数据集来测试条件句中的预设投射。研究评估了RoBERTa、DeBERTa、LLaMA和Gemma等模型，并通过可解释性分析发现，这些模型虽然总体上与人类判断一致，但主要依赖浅层模式匹配而非语义或语用推理。本研究提出了首个针对预设问题的计算评估框架，并强调了需要采用诊断性和多方法手段来评估语言模型的语用能力和上下文相关意义理解。

item.title: 基于时间感知的流式视频理解框架StreamReady
item.content:encoded:
提出ARS指标和StreamReady框架提升视频实时理解能力。



摘要： 本文针对流式视频理解中时间敏感的问题，提出了一种基于回答准备度的评估方法——答案准备度分数（ARS），该方法通过不对称的早期和晚期惩罚机制，衡量模型在合适时机回答问题的能力。结合正确性，ARS定义了一个更有效的准确率指标。基于此，作者提出了StreamReady框架，通过轻量级准备机制实现时间推理与及时回答的统一。同时引入了ProReady-QA基准测试，包含标注的答案证据窗口和多轮主动问题。实验表明，StreamReady在多个基准上表现优异，验证了其在视频理解任务中的广泛适用性和有效性。

item.title: 视觉语言模型在酒店决策领域的应用与挑战
item.content:encoded:
研究视觉语言模型在酒店图像问答中的表现及信息有效性。



摘要： 本文探讨了视觉语言模型（VLMs）在酒店和设施图像的视觉问答任务中的表现，发现现有基准主要关注事实正确性，而忽视了用户实际需要的信息。为此，作者提出了一种信息量框架来评估图像-问题对的有用性，并构建了一个专门针对酒店领域的VQA数据集。实验表明，VLMs在决策相关任务中表现不佳，关键视觉信号未被充分利用，只有经过适度领域微调后才能实现可靠的信息推理。

item.title: PRISM：解决运动生成中的两大挑战
item.content:encoded:
PRISM改进运动生成的潜在空间设计并统一条件注入方法。



摘要： 文章介绍了PRISM模型，旨在解决文本到动作生成中的两个关键问题。首先，传统方法将每一帧压缩为单一的潜在向量，导致轨迹和关节旋转信息混杂，影响生成质量。PRISM采用联合因子化的潜在空间，每个关节独立表示，形成结构化的2D网格，提升了生成效果。其次，现有方法需要多个模型或任务特定机制，而PRISM通过噪声-free 条件注入，实现统一的文本到动作和姿态条件生成，并支持自回归序列生成。该模型在多个数据集和用户研究中取得领先表现。

item.title: 基于Mamba的舞蹈生成方法MambaDance
item.content:encoded:
MambaDance利用Mamba模型提升舞蹈生成效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为MambaDance的新舞蹈生成方法，采用基于Mamba的扩散模型替代传统Transformer，以更好地处理舞蹈的序列性、节奏性和音乐同步性。研究引入高斯波形表示来引导舞蹈解码，实验表明该方法在AIST++和FineDance数据集上能生成合理且符合音乐节奏的舞蹈动作，适用于不同长度的舞蹈生成。

item.title: LLM在高风险科研资助评审中的能力与局限性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究LLM在科研资助评审中的表现及局限。



摘要： 本文探讨了人工智能辅助的科研资助提案评审在面对大量提案时所面临的效率瓶颈，并评估了基于大语言模型（LLM）的评审系统在高风险评估中的能力与限制。通过六个EPSRC提案，作者构建了一个基于扰动的框架，从六个质量维度（资金、时间线、能力、一致性、清晰度和影响力）测试LLM的敏感性。研究比较了三种评审架构：单次评审、分部分分析以及模拟专家小组的‘人物委员会’集成方法。结果表明，分部分分析方法在检测率和评分可靠性上显著优于其他方法，而计算成本高的委员会方法并未表现出更优性能。不同类型的扰动对检测效果影响显著，其中一致性问题易于识别，但清晰度问题则被所有系统忽视。人类评估显示，LLM反馈在合规性检查方面有效，但在整体评估上存在偏差。研究结论指出，当前LLM可能在EPSRC评审中提供辅助价值，但存在较大变异性且评审重点不匹配。论文已发布代码和非受保护数据。

item.title: 基于无监督视觉与几何推理的开放世界3D目标定位方法
item.content:encoded:
提出UniGround实现无需训练的3D目标精确定位。



摘要： 本文研究从自然语言描述中理解并定位复杂3D环境中的物体，即3D视觉定位（3DVG），这是具身AI的基础挑战。传统方法依赖预训练模型，限制了对未见空间关系的泛化能力。为此，作者提出UniGround，通过无监督的视觉与几何推理实现开放世界3DVG。该方法分为两个阶段：全局候选过滤和局部精确定位。实验表明，UniGround在ScanRefer和EmbodiedScan数据集上分别达到46.1%/34.1%和28.7%的准确率，优于现有零样本方法，并在真实环境中表现出良好的鲁棒性。

item.title: Speed3R：高效3D重建模型提升推理速度
item.content:encoded:
Speed3R通过稀疏注意力机制提升3D重建效率。



摘要： 本文提出Speed3R，一种基于稀疏关键点的端到端3D重建模型。该模型借鉴Structure-from-Motion原理，采用双分支注意力机制，先生成粗略上下文先验，再对关键图像标记进行细粒度注意力计算，从而显著提升推理速度。实验表明，在1000视图序列中，Speed3R实现12.4倍的速度提升，同时保持较高的几何精度。该方法在多个基准测试中表现优异，为大规模场景建模提供了高效的解决方案。

item.title: 人类与AI在第一视角动作识别中的性能对比研究
item.content:encoded:
人类在复杂环境下动作识别优于AI，依赖关键语义线索。



摘要： 本研究通过大规模人类与AI对比实验，分析了在低分辨率、遮挡和视觉杂乱等现实条件下，人类与AI在第一视角动作识别中的表现差异。研究使用Epic ReduAct数据集，评估了3000多名人类参与者和Side4Video模型的识别能力。结果表明，人类在面对最小可识别区域（MIRCs）时表现出显著性能下降，依赖于稀疏但语义关键的线索，如手物交互；而AI模型则更依赖上下文和中低级特征，在空间缩减下仍保持较高信心。此外，人类对时间干扰具有较强鲁棒性，而AI模型表现出类别的时间敏感性差异。

item.title: 基于相对特征分布的物体搜索方法ProReFF
item.content:encoded:
ProReFF通过学习相对特征分布提升物体搜索效率。



摘要： 本文提出ProReFF，一种基于预训练视觉语言模型特征场的模型，用于预测自然场景中物体的相对特征分布。该方法通过学习无标签数据中的隐含关系，提升物体搜索效率。研究还引入了一种基于学习的策略，将不一致的观察对齐为连贯的相对分布。实验表明，该方法在Matterport3D模拟器中比基线模型高效20%，并达到人类表现的80%。

item.title: 融合文化知识与安全性的大型语言模型研究
item.content:encoded:
研究提出融合文化知识与安全性的方法提升LLM的文化敏感性。



摘要： 随着大型语言模型的广泛应用，尊重原住民文化成为确保模型文化安全和负责任全球应用的关键。现有研究分别关注文化安全与文化知识，但忽视了前者应以后者为基础，导致模型难以生成文化特定的尊重性回应。为此，本文提出联合建模文化安全与知识的方法，并构建了包含4.8K细粒度文化描述和48K验证查询的数据集AdaCultureSafe。通过评估三种主流LLM，发现其文化安全与知识能力之间无显著相关性，原因可能在于预训练与对齐阶段目标不同。最后，本文提出基于知识的方法，显著提升了模型的文化安全性。

item.title: 基于进化策略的音频量化校准方法ESC
item.content:encoded:
ESC解决音频量化中的信息丢失问题，提升模型性能。



摘要： 量化已成为语音处理系统高效部署的关键技术。然而，现有方法多针对视觉和自然语言处理架构，忽视了音频信号的特殊性。本文指出音频激活值范围大，导致标准校准方法造成信息损失。为此，提出ESC（基于进化策略的校准方法），将激活缩放建模为优化问题，并通过两步局部-全局方案求解。ESC在全INT8量化下保持性能不变，是首个在多个语音任务中实现近无损全INT4量化的校准方法。结合PTQ方法进一步降低性能损失，在AST模型上仅产生1%的相对准确率下降。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的微表情动作单元检测方法AULLM++
item.content:encoded:
AULLM++通过融合视觉与语义信息提升微表情检测效果。



摘要： 本文提出AULLM++，一种基于大语言模型的微表情动作单元（AU）检测框架。针对传统方法在视觉信息利用不足、特征粒度不匹配以及忽略AU间关联性的问题，AULLM++引入多粒度证据增强融合投影器和关系感知AU图神经网络，生成结构化文本提示，并通过反事实一致性正则化提升模型泛化能力。实验表明，该方法在标准基准上表现优异，具有出色的跨域适应性。

item.title: LeJOT-AutoML：基于大语言模型的自动机器学习框架提升云作业编排效率
item.content:encoded:
LeJOT-AutoML通过自动化特征提取提升云作业预测准确性，降低19%成本。



摘要： LeJOT-AutoML是一个基于大语言模型的自动机器学习框架，旨在优化Databricks作业编排系统，减少云成本。该框架通过整合领域知识库和多种工具链，自动分析作业日志、元数据和脚本，生成超过200个特征，并大幅缩短特征工程与评估周期。相比传统方法，其在保持预测准确性的前提下，将更新周期从数周缩短至20-30分钟。集成到LeJOT管道后，实现了持续模型更新，最终在实际部署中实现了19.01%的成本节约。

item.title: 基于自回归语言模型的全分辨率音频无损压缩研究
item.content:encoded:
自回归语言模型可实现高比特深度音频无损压缩，Trilobyte提升压缩效率。



摘要： 本文研究了基于自回归语言模型的音频无损压缩方法，验证其在8-bit、16-bit和24-bit音频中的可行性。传统样本级分词在高比特深度下因词汇量过大而不可行，为此提出Trilobyte字节级分词方案，将词汇规模从指数级降低到常数级，实现了首个可行的24-bit无损压缩。实验表明，LM在8-bit和16-bit下优于FLAC，但随着比特深度增加，压缩优势减弱。

item.title: 多模态数学推理研究现状与挑战
item.content:encoded:
多模态数学推理面临解析图表、对齐符号等挑战，需改进评估方法。



摘要： 多模态数学推理（MMR）在处理包含文本和视觉信息的数学问题上展现出潜力，但当前模型在实际应用中仍存在诸多问题，如误读图表、无法正确对齐数学符号与视觉证据、推理步骤不一致等。现有评估主要关注最终答案，而忽视中间步骤的正确性。为解决这些问题，研究者尝试通过结构化感知、显式对齐和可验证推理来提升性能。本文围绕四个核心问题对不同MMR方法进行系统分析：如何从多模态输入中提取信息、如何表示和对齐文本与视觉内容、如何执行推理、以及如何评估推理过程的正确性。最后，文章探讨了当前面临的挑战，并提出未来研究的方向。

item.title: 基于视觉-空间推理的航拍视觉-语言导航框架
item.content:encoded:
提出ViSA框架提升航拍VLN性能，成功率提升70.3%



摘要： 现有航拍视觉-语言导航方法多采用检测与规划流程，存在空间推理能力不足和语言歧义问题。本文提出ViSA增强框架，通过三阶段协同架构，使视觉语言模型直接在图像平面上进行推理，无需额外训练或复杂中间表示。在CityNav基准测试中，ViSA框架的成功率比最先进的方法提高了70.3%，展现出在航拍VLN系统中的巨大潜力。

item.title: AI金融智能基准评估大型语言模型的财务分析能力
item.content:encoded:
评估五种AI系统在金融分析中的表现，揭示其多维能力差异。



摘要： 本文引入了AI金融智能基准（AFIB），用于评估大型语言模型在财务分析中的五项关键能力：事实准确性、分析完整性、数据时效性、模型一致性及失败模式。通过95+个结构化财务分析问题，对GPT、Gemini、Perplexity、Claude和SuperInvesting五种AI系统进行测试。结果显示，SuperInvesting在整体表现上最佳，具有最高的事实准确性和分析完整性，同时表现出最低的幻觉率。而以检索为导向的Perplexity在数据时效性任务中表现优异，但在分析综合能力和一致性方面较弱。研究强调，金融智能是多维的，结合结构化数据访问与分析推理能力的系统在复杂投资研究中表现更可靠。

item.title: 基于UV空间的高质量3D服装纹理生成方法GarmentPainter
item.content:encoded:
GarmentPainter实现高效3D一致的服装纹理生成。



摘要： 本文提出GarmentPainter，一种在UV空间中生成高质量、3D感知服装纹理的框架。该方法利用UV位置图作为3D结构引导，确保纹理一致性，并引入类型选择模块，根据参考图像生成特定部件的纹理，无需对齐2D图像与3D网格。GarmentPainter将所有引导信号以空间对齐方式整合到扩散模型输入中，不修改底层UNet架构。实验表明，该方法在视觉保真度、3D一致性和计算效率方面均优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于可见性约束的图像合成阴影生成方法VSDiffusion
item.content:encoded:
提出VSDiffusion框架，提升图像合成中阴影的几何一致性。



摘要： 本文针对图像合成中插入前景物体的逼真阴影生成问题，提出了一种基于可见性约束的两阶段框架VSDiffusion。该方法通过引入可见性先验信息，缩小阴影生成的解空间。第一阶段预测粗略的阴影掩码，第二阶段则在光照和深度信息的引导下进行条件扩散，生成精确阴影。此外，还设计了可见性控制分支和软先验图以增强几何修正，并引入高频增强模块提升边界清晰度和纹理交互效果。实验表明，VSDiffusion在DESOBAv2数据集上取得了最先进的性能。

item.content:encoded:
提出一种改进的多智能体路径规划方法，提升求解质量。



摘要： 本文针对多智能体路径规划（MAPF）问题，提出一种改进的求解方法。现有方法依赖于静态引导路径，计算开销大且效果有限。本文利用LaCAM*在搜索过程中构建的动态轻量级交通图，提升了路径规划的效率和质量。实验结果表明，该方法在两种MAPF变体中均优于现有最优方法。

item.title: 连续时间线性平稳随机微分方程的边符号可识别性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究连续时间线性平稳随机微分方程的边符号可识别性。



摘要： 本文研究了在已知因果结构下，连续时间线性平稳随机微分方程的可识别性问题。不同于现有方法，文章放宽了对扩散矩阵已知的假设，尊重模型的内在尺度不变性。作者提出边符号可识别性的概念，即在给定因果结构下，判断某个漂移项的符号是否在所有与该结构兼容的观测协方差矩阵中唯一确定。基于一种“忠实性”假设，推导出适用于一般图的可识别性、不可识别性和部分可识别性的判定准则。通过应用于经典和新型循环因果结构，验证了这些准则的有效性，并在某些情况下给出了目标边符号的显式表达。

item.title: 基于多模态嵌入和命名实体注入的抽象摘要生成方法
item.content:encoded:
本文提出SBARThez框架提升摘要事实一致性。



摘要： 本文研究了抽象摘要生成中的信息准确性问题，提出一种基于多模态和多语言句子嵌入的改进方法。通过引入命名实体注入机制，增强模型生成摘要的事实一致性。该框架SBARThez适用于文本和语音输入，并支持跨语言摘要，在低资源语言中表现优于传统方法，生成更简洁且抽象的摘要。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的AI在急诊护理中的可行性研究
item.content:encoded:
AI系统AMIE在急诊护理中表现出良好的安全性和用户接受度。



摘要： 本研究评估了基于大语言模型的AI系统AMIE在真实临床环境中的可行性。100名成人患者在就诊前通过文本聊天与AMIE互动，用于病史采集和可能诊断的呈现。研究结果显示，AMIE在安全性、患者满意度及临床推理能力方面表现良好，且与初级保健医生（PCPs）相比，在诊断和管理计划质量上无显著差异。尽管PCPs在实用性与成本效益上优于AMIE，但AMIE仍显示出在实际临床应用中的潜力，为未来AI医疗系统的临床转化提供了重要依据。

item.title: ImprovedGS+：基于C++/CUDA的高效3D高斯点云重建方法
item.content:encoded:
ImprovedGS+提升3D高斯点云重建速度与质量。



摘要： 本文提出ImprovedGS+，一种在LichtFeld-Studio框架中实现的高性能3D高斯点云重建方法。通过将高阶Python逻辑转换为硬件优化的C++/CUDA内核，显著降低了主机与设备同步和训练延迟。该方法引入了长轴分割CUDA内核、基于拉普拉斯的重要性内核及非极大值抑制机制，并采用自适应指数尺度调度器。实验表明，ImprovedGS+在Mip-NeRF360数据集上表现出色，其1M预算版本相比MCMC基线训练时间减少26.8%，并减少13.3%的高斯点数量，同时保持高质量视觉效果；全版本则在PSNR上比ADC基线提高1.28 dB，参数复杂度降低38.4%。结果验证了ImprovedGS+在速度、质量和可用性方面的优越性能。

item.title: 基于相对似然的高效可信集预测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效可信集预测方法，用于处理安全关键场景下的不确定性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于相对似然的高效可信集预测方法，用于在安全关键场景中表示和处理不确定性。该方法通过预测每个类别标签的概率区间来构建可信集，采用去校准技术计算区间的上下界。实验表明，该方法在覆盖效率评估、分布外检测和上下文学习等任务中表现优异，并成功应用于TabPFN和CLIP等复杂模型，这些模型此前难以构建可信集。

item.title: 基于混合视觉Transformer的数学表达式识别方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的数学表达式识别方法，效果优于现有技术。



摘要： 本文针对数学表达式识别这一复杂问题，提出了一种基于混合视觉Transformer（HVT）的方法。该方法采用2D位置编码作为编码器，以捕捉符号之间的复杂关系，并使用覆盖注意力解码器来解决解析不准确的问题。实验结果表明，该方法在IM2LATEX-100K数据集上取得了89.94的BLEU分数，优于当前最先进的方法。

item.title: 基于难度感知的自适应推理方法CODA研究
item.content:encoded:
CODA通过动态分配计算资源提升推理效率与性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于难度感知的自适应推理方法CODA，旨在解决大模型在简单任务中过度推理的问题。CODA通过内部难度信号动态分配计算资源，使模型在简单任务中减少冗余计算，在复杂任务中增加深度推理。该方法无需外部标注或用户预算，在多个基准测试中实现了更高的效率和性能。

item.title: A Multi-Objective Optimization Approach for Sustainable AI-Driven Entrepreneurship in Resilient Economies
item.content:encoded: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies presents both unprecedented opportunities and significant challenges for sustainable economic development. While AI offers transformative potential for addressing environmental challenges and enhancing economic resilience, its deployment often involves substantial energy consumption and environmental costs. This research introduces the EcoAI-Resilience framework, a multi-objective optimization approach designed to maximize the sustainability benefits of AI deployment while minimizing environmental costs and enhancing economic resilience. The framework addresses three critical objectives through mathematical optimization: sustainability impact maximization, economic resilience enhancement, and environmental cost minimization. The methodology integrates diverse data sources, including energy consumption metrics, sustainability indicators, economic performance data, and entrepreneurship outcomes across 53 countries and 14 sectors from 2015-2024. Our experimental validation demonstrates exceptional performance with R scores exceeding 0.99 across all model components, significantly outperforming baseline methods, including Linear Regression (R = 0.943), Random Forest (R = 0.957), and Gradient Boosting (R = 0.989). The framework successfully identifies optimal AI deployment strategies featuring 100\% renewable energy integration, 80% efficiency improvement targets, and optimal investment levels of $202.48 per capita. Key findings reveal strong correlations between economic complexity and resilience (r = 0.82), renewable energy adoption and sustainability outcomes (r = 0.71), and demonstrate significant temporal improvements in AI readiness (+1.12 points/year) and renewable energy adoption (+0.67 year) globally.

item.title: VORL-EXPLORE: A Hybrid Learning Planning Approach to Multi-Robot Exploration in Dynamic Environments
item.content:encoded: Hierarchical multi-robot exploration commonly decouples frontier allocation from local navigation, which can make the system brittle in dense and dynamic environments. Because the allocator lacks direct awareness of execution difficulty, robots may cluster at bottlenecks, trigger oscillatory replanning, and generate redundant coverage. We propose VORL-EXPLORE, a hybrid learning and planning framework that addresses this limitation through execution fidelity, a shared estimate of local navigability that couples task allocation with motion execution. This fidelity signal is incorporated into a fidelity-coupled Voronoi objective with inter-robot repulsion to reduce contention before it emerges. It also drives a risk-aware adaptive arbitration mechanism between global A* guidance and a reactive reinforcement learning policy, balancing long-range efficiency with safe interaction in confined spaces. The framework further supports online self-supervised recalibration of the fidelity model using pseudo-labels derived from recent progress and safety outcomes, enabling adaptation to non-stationary obstacles without manual risk tuning. We evaluate this capability separately in a dedicated severe-traffic ablation. Extensive experiments in randomized grids and a Gazebo factory scenario show high success rates, shorter path length, lower overlap, and robust collision avoidance. The source code will be made publicly available upon acceptance.

item.title: EveryQuery: Zero-Shot Clinical Prediction via Task-Conditioned Pretraining over Electronic Health Records
item.content:encoded: Foundation models pretrained on electronic health records (EHR) have demonstrated zero-shot clinical prediction capabilities by generating synthetic patient futures and aggregating statistics over sampled trajectories. However, this autoregressive inference procedure is computationally expensive, statistically noisy, and not natively promptable because users cannot directly condition predictions on specific clinical questions. In this preliminary work, we introduce EveryQuery, an EHR foundation model that achieves zero-shot inference through task-conditioned pre-training. Rather than generating future events, EveryQuery takes as input a patient's history and a structured query specifying a clinical task, and directly estimates the likelihood of the outcome occurring in the future window via a single forward pass. EveryQuery realizes this capability by pre-training over randomly sampled combinations of query tasks and patient contexts, directly training the model to produce correct answers to arbitrary input prompts. This enables zero-shot prediction for any task in the query space without finetuning, linear probing, or trajectory generation. On MIMIC-IV, EveryQuery outperforms an autoregressive baseline on 82% of 39 randomly sampled prediction tasks, with a mean AUC improvement of +0.16 (95% CI: [0.10,0.22]). This advantage remains consistent on tasks that were explicitly held out from the pre-training distribution. Further, EveryQuery's performance gains are most pronounced for rare clinical events, affirming and demonstrating a solution to the fundamental limitation of autoregressive inference for low-prevalence outcomes. However, at present, EveryQuery underperforms on tasks requiring disjunctive reasoning over multiple codes, such as 30-day readmission, exposing a concrete expressiveness limitation of the current query language.

item.title: Context-free Self-Conditioned GAN for Trajectory Forecasting
item.content:encoded: In this paper, we present a context-free unsupervised approach based on a self-conditioned GAN to learn different modes from 2D trajectories. Our intuition is that each mode indicates a different behavioral moving pattern in the discriminator's feature space. We apply this approach to the problem of trajectory forecasting. We present three different training settings based on self-conditioned GAN, which produce better forecasters. We test our method in two data sets: human motion and road agents. Experimental results show that our approach outperforms previous context-free methods in the least representative supervised labels while performing well in the remaining labels. In addition, our approach outperforms globally in human motion, while performing well in road agents.

item.title: OSS-CRS: Liberating AIxCC Cyber Reasoning Systems for Real-World Open-Source Security
item.content:encoded: DARPA's AI Cyber Challenge (AIxCC) showed that cyber reasoning systems (CRSs) can go beyond vulnerability discovery to autonomously confirm and patch bugs: seven teams built such systems and open-sourced them after the competition. Yet all seven open-sourced CRSs remain largely unusable outside their original teams, each bound to the competition cloud infrastructure that no longer exists. We present OSS-CRS, an open, locally deployable framework for running and combining CRS techniques against real-world open-source projects, with budget-aware resource management. We ported the first-place system (Atlantis) and discovered 10 previously unknown bugs (three of high severity) across 8 OSS-Fuzz projects. OSS-CRS is publicly available.

item.title: Graph-Instructed Neural Networks for parametric problems with varying boundary conditions
item.content:encoded: This work addresses the accurate and efficient simulation of physical phenomena governed by parametric Partial Differential Equations (PDEs) characterized by varying boundary conditions, where parametric instances modify not only the physics of the problem but also the imposition of boundary constraints on the computational domain. In such scenarios, classical Galerkin projection-based reduced order techniques encounter a fundamental bottleneck. Parametric boundaries typically necessitate a re-formulation of the discrete problem for each new configuration, and often, these approaches are unsuitable for real-time applications. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel methodology based on Graph-Instructed Neural Networks (GINNs). The GINN framework effectively learns the mapping between the parametric description of the computational domain and the corresponding PDE solution. Our results demonstrate that the proposed GINN-based models, can efficiently represent highly complex parametric PDEs, serving as a robust and scalable asset for several applied-oriented settings when compared with fully connected architectures.

item.title: Computational modeling of early language learning from acoustic speech and audiovisual input without linguistic priors
item.content:encoded: Learning to understand speech appears almost effortless for typically developing infants, yet from an information-processing perspective, acquiring a language from acoustic speech is an enormous challenge. This chapter reviews recent developments in using computational models to understand early language acquisition from speech and audiovisual input. The focus is on self-supervised and visually grounded models of perceptual learning. We show how these models are becoming increasingly powerful in learning various aspects of speech without strong linguistic priors, and how many features of early language development can be explained through a shared set of learning principles-principles broadly compatible with multiple theories of language acquisition and human cognition. We also discuss how modern learning simulations are gradually becoming more realistic, both in terms of input data and in linking model behavior to empirical findings on infant language development.

item.title: Deterministic Differentiable Structured Pruning for Large Language Models
item.content:encoded: Structured pruning reduces LLM inference cost by removing low-importance architectural components. This can be viewed as learning a multiplicative gate for each component under an l0 sparsity constraint. Due to the discreteness of the l0 norm, prior work typically adopts stochastic hard-concrete relaxations to enable differentiable optimization; however, this stochasticity can introduce a train--test mismatch when sampled masks are discretized for deployment and restricts masks to a bounded, near-binary range. To address this, we propose Deterministic Differentiable Pruning (DDP), a mask-only optimization method that eliminates stochasticity by directly optimizing a deterministic soft surrogate of the discrete l0 objective. Compared with prior approaches, DDP offers greater expressiveness, reduced train--test mismatch, and faster convergence. We apply our method to several dense and MoE models, including Qwen3-32B and Qwen3-30B-A3B, achieving a performance loss as small as 1% on downstream tasks while outperforming previous methods at 20% sparsity. We further demonstrate end-to-end inference speedups in realistic deployment settings with vLLM.

item.title: An explainable hybrid deep learning-enabled intelligent fault detection and diagnosis approach for automotive software systems validation
item.content:encoded: Advancements in data-driven machine learning have emerged as a pivotal element in supporting automotive software systems (ASSs) engineering across various levels of the V-development process. Duringsystemverificationandvalidation,theintegrationofanintelligent fault detection anddiagnosis (FDD) model with test recordings analysis process serves as a powerful tool for efficiency ensuring functional safety. However, the lack of interpretability of the black-box FDD models developed not only hinders understanding of the cause underlying the prediction, but also prevents the model from being adapted based on the prediction result. This, in turn, increases the computational cost required for developingacomplexFDDmodelandlimitsconfidenceinreal-timesafety-criticalapplications.To address this challenge, a novel explainable method for fault detection, identification, and localization is proposed in this article with the aim of providing a clear understanding of the logic behind the prediction outcome. To this end, a hybrid 1dCNN-GRU-based intelligent model was developed to analyze the recordings from the real-time validation process of ASSs. The employment of explainable AI techniques, i.e., IGs, DeepLIFT, Gradient SHAP, and DeepLIFT SHAP, was instrumental in enabling model adaptation and facilitating the root cause analysis (RCA). The proposed approach is applied to the real time dataset collected during a virtual test drive performed by the user on hardware in the loop system.

item.title: SWE-Fuse: Empowering Software Agents via Issue-free Trajectory Learning and Entropy-aware RLVR Training
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) have transformed the software engineering landscape. Recently, numerous LLM-based agents have been developed to address real-world software issue fixing tasks. Despite their state-of-the-art performance, Despite achieving state-of-the-art performance, these agents face a significant challenge: \textbf{Insufficient high-quality issue descriptions.} Real-world datasets often exhibit misalignments between issue descriptions and their corresponding solutions, introducing noise and ambiguity that mislead automated agents and limit their problem-solving effectiveness. We propose \textbf{\textit{SWE-Fuse}}, an issue-description-aware training framework that fuses issue-description-guided and issue-free samples for training SWE agents. It consists of two key modules: (1) An issue-free-driven trajectory learning module for mitigating potentially misleading issue descriptions while enabling the model to learn step-by-step debugging processes; and (2) An entropy-aware RLVR training module, which adaptively adjusts training dynamics through entropy-driven clipping. It applies relaxed clipping under high entropy to encourage exploration, and stricter clipping under low entropy to ensure training stability. We evaluate SWE-Fuse on the widely studied SWE-bench Verified benchmark shows to demonstrate its effectiveness in solving real-world software problems. Specifically, SWE-Fuse outperforms the best 8B and 32B baselines by 43.0\% and 60.2\% in solve rate, respectively. Furthermore, integrating SWE-Fuse with test-time scaling (TTS) enables further performance improvements, achieving solve rates of 49.8\% and 65.2\% under TTS@8 for the 8B and 32B models, respectively.

item.title: Topologically Stable Hough Transform
item.content:encoded: We propose an alternative formulation of the well-known Hough transform to detect lines in point clouds. Replacing the discretized voting scheme of the classical Hough transform by a continuous score function, its persistent features in the sense of persistent homology give a set of candidate lines. We also devise and implement an algorithm to efficiently compute these candidate lines.

item.title: Retrieval-Augmented Gaussian Avatars: Improving Expression Generalization
item.content:encoded: Template-free animatable head avatars can achieve high visual fidelity by learning expression-dependent facial deformation directly from a subject's capture, avoiding parametric face templates and hand-designed blendshape spaces. However, since learned deformation is supervised only by the expressions observed for a single identity, these models suffer from limited expression coverage and often struggle when driven by motions that deviate from the training distribution. We introduce RAF (Retrieval-Augmented Faces), a simple training-time augmentation designed for template-free head avatars that learn deformation from data. RAF constructs a large unlabeled expression bank and, during training, replaces a subset of the subject's expression features with nearest-neighbor expressions retrieved from this bank while still reconstructing the subject's original frames. This exposes the deformation field to a broader range of expression conditions, encouraging stronger identity-expression decoupling and improving robustness to expression distribution shift without requiring paired cross-identity data, additional annotations, or architectural changes. We further analyze how retrieval augmentation increases expression diversity and validate retrieval quality with a user study showing that retrieved neighbors are perceptually closer in expression and pose. Experiments on the NeRSemble benchmark demonstrate that RAF consistently improves expression fidelity over the baseline, in both self-driving and cross-driving scenarios.

item.title: PIRA-Bench: A Transition from Reactive GUI Agents to GUI-based Proactive Intent Recommendation Agents
item.content:encoded: Current Graphical User Interface (GUI) agents operate primarily under a reactive paradigm: a user must provide an explicit instruction for the agent to execute a task. However, an intelligent AI assistant should be proactive, which is capable of anticipating user intentions directly from continuous visual inputs, such as mobile or desktop screenshots, and offering timely recommendations without explicit user prompting. Transitioning to this proactive paradigm presents significant challenges. Real-world screen activity is rarely linear; it consists of long-horizon trajectories fraught with noisy browsing, meaningless actions, and multithreaded task-switching. To address this gap, we introduce PIRA-Bench (Proactive Intent Recommendation Agent Benchmark), a novel benchmark for evaluating multimodal large language models (MLLMs) on continuous, weakly-supervised visual inputs. Unlike reactive datasets, PIRA-Bench features complex trajectories with multiple interleaved intents and noisy segments with various user profile contexts, challenging agents to detect actionable events while fitting to user preferences. Furthermore, we propose the PIRF baseline, a memory-aware, state-tracking framework that empowers general MLLMs to manage multiple task threads and handle misleading visual inputs. PIRA-Bench serves as an initial step toward robust and proactive GUI-based personal assistants.

item.title: Information Maximization for Long-Tailed Semi-Supervised Domain Generalization
item.content:encoded: Semi-supervised domain generalization (SSDG) has recently emerged as an appealing alternative to tackle domain generalization when labeled data is scarce but unlabeled samples across domains are abundant. In this work, we identify an important limitation that hampers the deployment of state-of-the-art methods on more challenging but practical scenarios. In particular, state-of-the-art SSDG severely suffers in the presence of long-tailed class distributions, an arguably common situation in real-world settings. To alleviate this limitation, we propose IMaX, a simple yet effective objective based on the well-known InfoMax principle adapted to the SSDG scenario, where the Mutual Information (MI) between the learned features and latent labels is maximized, constrained by the supervision from the labeled samples. Our formulation integrates an α-entropic objective, which mitigates the class-balance bias encoded in the standard marginal entropy term of the MI, thereby better handling arbitrary class distributions. IMaX can be seamlessly plugged into recent state-of-the-art SSDG, consistently enhancing their performance, as demonstrated empirically across two different image modalities.

item.title: Bootstrapping Audiovisual Speech Recognition in Zero-AV-Resource Scenarios with Synthetic Visual Data
item.content:encoded: Audiovisual speech recognition (AVSR) combines acoustic and visual cues to improve transcription robustness under challenging conditions but remains out of reach for most under-resourced languages due to the lack of labeled video corpora for training. We propose a zero-AV-resource AVSR framework that relies on synthetic visual streams generated by lip-syncing static facial images with real audio. We first evaluate synthetic visual augmentation on Spanish benchmarks, then apply it to Catalan, a language with no annotated audiovisual corpora. We synthesize over 700 hours of talking-head video and fine-tune a pre-trained AV-HuBERT model. On a manually annotated Catalan benchmark, our model achieves near state-of-the-art performance with much fewer parameters and training data, outperforms an identically trained audio-only baseline, and preserves multimodal advantages in noise. Scalable synthetic video thus offers a viable substitute for real recordings in zero-AV-resource AVSR.

item.title: Adaptive Loops and Memory in Transformers: Think Harder or Know More?
item.content:encoded: Chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting enables reasoning in language models but requires explicit verbalization of intermediate steps. Looped transformers offer an alternative by iteratively refining representations within hidden states. This parameter efficiency comes at a cost, as looped models lack the storage capacity of deeper models which use unique weights per layer. In this work, we investigate transformer models that feature both adaptive per-layer looping, where each transformer block learns to iterate its hidden state via a learned halting mechanism, and gated memory banks, that provide additional learned storage. We find that looping primarily benefits mathematical reasoning, while memory banks help recover performance on commonsense tasks compared to parameter and FLOP matched models. Combining both mechanisms yields a model that outperforms an iso-FLOP baseline -- with three times the number of layers -- on math benchmarks. Analysis of model internals reveals layer specialization: early layers learn to loop minimally and access memory sparingly, while later layers do both more heavily.

item.title: FVG-PT: Adaptive Foreground View-Guided Prompt Tuning for Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: CLIP-based prompt tuning enables pretrained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to efficiently adapt to downstream tasks. Although existing studies have made significant progress, they pay limited attention to changes in the internal attention representations of VLMs during the tuning process. In this paper, we attribute the failure modes of prompt tuning predictions to shifts in foreground attention of the visual encoder, and propose Foreground View-Guided Prompt Tuning (FVG-PT), an adaptive plug-and-play foreground attention guidance module, to alleviate the shifts. Concretely, FVG-PT introduces a learnable Foreground Reliability Gate to automatically enhance the foreground view quality, applies a Foreground Distillation Compensation module to guide visual attention toward the foreground, and further introduces a Prior Calibration module to mitigate generalization degradation caused by excessive focus on the foreground. Experiments on multiple backbone models and datasets show the effectiveness and compatibility of FVG-PT. Codes are available at: https://github.com/JREion/FVG-PT

item.title: Diffusion-Based Data Augmentation for Image Recognition: A Systematic Analysis and Evaluation
item.content:encoded: Diffusion-based data augmentation (DiffDA) has emerged as a promising approach to improving classification performance under data scarcity. However, existing works vary significantly in task configurations, model choices, and experimental pipelines, making it difficult to fairly compare methods or assess their effectiveness across different scenarios. Moreover, there remains a lack of systematic understanding of the full DiffDA workflow. In this work, we introduce UniDiffDA, a unified analytical framework that decomposes DiffDA methods into three core components: model fine-tuning, sample generation, and sample utilization. This perspective enables us to identify key differences among existing methods and clarify the overall design space. Building on this framework, we develop a comprehensive and fair evaluation protocol, benchmarking representative DiffDA methods across diverse low-data classification tasks. Extensive experiments reveal the relative strengths and limitations of different DiffDA strategies and offer practical insights into method design and deployment. All methods are re-implemented within a unified codebase, with full release of code and configurations to ensure reproducibility and to facilitate future research.

item.title: Aero-Promptness: Drag-Aware Aerodynamic Manipulability for Propeller-driven Vehicles
item.content:encoded: This work introduces the Drag-Aware Aerodynamic Manipulability (DAAM), a geometric framework for control allocation in redundant multirotors. By equipping the propeller spin-rate space with a Riemannian metric based on the remaining symmetric acceleration capacity of each motor, the formulation explicitly accounts for motor torque limits and aerodynamic drag. Mapping this metric through the nonlinear thrust law to the generalized force space yields a state-dependent manipulability volume. The log-determinant of this volume acts as a natural barrier function, strictly penalizing drag-induced saturation and low-spin thrust loss. Optimizing this volume along the allocation fibers provides a redundancy resolution strategy inherently invariant to arbitrary coordinate scaling in the generalized-force space. Analytically, we prove that the resulting optimal allocations locally form smooth embedded manifolds, and we geometrically characterize the global jump discontinuities that inevitably arise from physical actuator limits and spin-rate sign transitions.

item.title: Ares: Adaptive Reasoning Effort Selection for Efficient LLM Agents
item.content:encoded: Modern agents powered by thinking LLMs achieve high accuracy through long chain-of-thought reasoning but incur substantial inference costs. While many LLMs now support configurable reasoning levels (e.g., high/medium/low), static strategies are often ineffective: using low-effort modes at every step leads to significant performance degradation, while random selection fails to preserve accuracy or provide meaningful cost reduction. However, agents should reserve high reasoning effort for difficult steps like navigating complex website structures, while using lower-effort modes for simpler steps like opening a target URL. In this paper, we propose Ares, a framework for per-step dynamic reasoning effort selection tailored for multi-step agent tasks. Ares employs a lightweight router to predict the lowest appropriate reasoning level for each step based on the interaction history. To train this router, we develop a data generation pipeline that identifies the minimum reasoning effort required for successful step completion. We then fine-tune the router to predict these levels, enabling plug-and-play integration for any LLM agents. We evaluate Ares on a diverse set of agent tasks, including TAU-Bench for tool use agents, BrowseComp-Plus for deep-research agents, and WebArena for web agents. Experimental results show that Ares reduces reasoning token usage by up to 52.7% compared to fixed high-effort reasoning, while introducing minimal degradation in task success rates.

item.title: EdgeFLow：一种高效的边缘联邦学习框架
item.content:encoded:
EdgeFLow通过边缘计算减少通信开销，提升联邦学习效率。



摘要： 本文提出EdgeFLow，一种基于边缘计算的联邦学习框架，通过将传统云服务器替换为边缘基站间的模型迁移，实现模型聚合与传播在边缘集群中完成，从而消除云端传输，显著降低全局通信开销。研究提供了非凸目标和非独立同分布数据下的收敛性分析，并通过实验验证了其在多种配置下的有效性，证明其在保持准确率的同时大幅降低通信成本。EdgeFLow为未来物联网和边缘网络学习系统提供了基础架构创新。

item.title: 基于语义关键点的自主功能玩耍方法Tether
item.content:encoded:
Tether实现机器人自主玩耍，提升数据效率与任务多样性。



摘要： 本文提出Tether方法，使机器人能够通过自主功能玩耍进行学习。该方法设计了一种新的开环策略，通过将少量源演示动作映射到目标场景的语义关键点，实现高效且鲁棒的策略生成。同时，借助视觉-语言模型的视觉理解能力，机器人可自主完成任务选择、执行、评估与优化，生成高质量数据集。实验表明，在家庭多物体环境中，Tether可在仅需少量演示的情况下实现长时间自主多任务玩耍，持续提升闭环模仿策略性能，最终生成超过1000条专家级轨迹。

item.title: MADCrowner: Margin Aware Dental Crown Design with Template Deformation and Refinement
item.content:encoded: Dental crown restoration is one of the most common treatment modalities for tooth defect, where personalized dental crown design is critical. While computer-aided design (CAD) systems have notably enhanced the efficiency of dental crown design, extensive manual adjustments are still required in the clinic workflow. Recent studies have explored the application of learning-based methods for the automated generation of restorative dental crowns. Nevertheless, these approaches were challenged by inadequate spatial resolution, noisy outputs, and overextension of surface reconstruction. To address these limitations, we propose \totalframework, a margin-aware mesh generation framework comprising CrownDeformR and CrownSegger. Inspired by the clinic manual workflow of dental crown design, we designed CrownDeformR to deform an initial template to the target crown based on anatomical context, which is extracted by a multi-scale intraoral scan encoder. Additionally, we introduced \marginseg, a novel margin segmentation network, to extract the cervical margin of the target tooth. The performance of CrownDeformR improved with the cervical margin as an extra constraint. And it was also utilized as the boundary condition for the tailored postprocessing method, which removed the overextended area of the reconstructed surface. We constructed a large-scale intraoral scan dataset and performed extensive experiments. The proposed method significantly outperformed existing approaches in both geometric accuracy and clinical feasibility.

item.title: 基于小型语言模型的LTL规范生成方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出LTLGuard工具链，提升小型模型生成正确LTL规范的能力。



摘要： 本文针对自然语言到形式化规范转换中的挑战，特别是小型语言模型在时间逻辑理解上的不足，提出LTLGuard工具链。该工具结合约束生成与形式一致性检查，通过生成模型与轻量级自动推理工具的协作，迭代优化候选规范，消除冲突并提高规范的一致性。实验验证了该方法的有效性和实用性。

item.title: 基于脑功能连接网络的大型语言模型框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出FCN-LLM框架，使大模型理解脑功能连接网络。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为FCN-LLM的框架，通过图级多任务指令微调，使大型语言模型能够直接理解脑功能连接网络（FCN）。该方法采用多尺度FCN编码器，捕捉脑区、功能子网络和全脑特征，并将其投影到语言模型的语义空间中。设计了涵盖19个受试者属性的多范式指令任务，结合多阶段学习策略，提升模型对高阶语义信息的捕捉能力。实验表明，FCN-LLM在大规模多中心FCN数据库上表现出强大的零样本泛化能力，优于传统监督模型和基础模型。该研究为脑科学与大模型融合提供了新思路。

item.title: 基于参考的视频编辑方法与数据集构建
item.content:encoded:
提出新数据生成方法和模型，提升视频编辑精度。



摘要： 本文针对视频编辑中自然语言描述不够精确的问题，提出一种可扩展的数据生成流程，将现有视频编辑对转换为高质量训练四元组，并构建了RefVIE数据集用于指令-参考跟随任务。同时设计了Kiwi-Edit模型，结合可学习查询和潜在视觉特征进行参考语义引导，通过多阶段训练显著提升了指令遵循和参考保真度。实验表明该方法在可控视频编辑领域达到新高度。

item.title: 基于扩散模型与3D高斯的牙齿组合生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出DM-CFO方法提升3D牙齿生成的多视角一致性和真实感。



摘要： 本文针对3D牙齿建模中的布局与形状优化问题，提出一种名为DM-CFO的方法。该方法利用扩散模型生成牙齿布局，并在去噪阶段结合文本和图约束逐步恢复缺失牙齿的位置。随后通过分数蒸馏采样（SDS）交替更新每个牙齿及整个牙弓的高斯参数。为避免牙齿碰撞，引入基于邻近牙齿与基准牙齿间距离的正则化项。实验结果表明，该方法在三个牙齿设计数据集上显著提升了生成牙齿的多视角一致性和真实感。

item.title: 图霍普菲尔网络：结合记忆检索与图拉普拉斯平滑的节点分类方法
item.content:encoded:
图霍普菲尔网络通过联合能量函数提升节点分类性能。



摘要： 本文提出图霍普菲尔网络，其能量函数将关联记忆检索与图拉普拉斯平滑相结合，用于节点分类任务。通过梯度下降优化该联合能量函数，实现霍普菲尔检索与拉普拉斯传播的迭代更新。实验表明，记忆检索在稀疏引用网络中可提升2.0~pp，在特征遮蔽下增加5 pp的鲁棒性。即使禁用记忆模块，该架构仍优于标准基线。此外，通过调参可实现异质性基准的图锐化，无需改变架构。

item.title: 基于任务扩展的高效多任务模型强化学习算法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效多任务MBRL算法，提升机器人学习效率。



摘要： 本文探讨了在具身AI中开发能够掌握多种技能的通用机器人所面临的挑战。作者认为，有效的在线学习应侧重于任务数量的扩展，而非每个任务样本数量的增加。通过引入高效零多任务（EZ-M）算法，该研究展示了在多任务环境下模型基础强化学习（MBRL）的优势。实验表明，该方法在HumanoidBench基准测试中表现出色，具有更高的样本效率。研究强调了任务扩展在可扩展机器人学习中的重要性。

item.title: 基于mHC框架的聚类引导HSI分类模型研究
item.content:encoded:
提出mHC-HSI模型提升高光谱图像分类精度与可解释性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于manifold-constrained hyper-connection（mHC）框架的聚类引导Mamba模型（mHC-HSI），用于增强高光谱图像（HSI）分类性能。该方法通过设计聚类引导的Mamba模块，同时学习空间和光谱信息；引入改进的残差矩阵实现HSI的软聚类划分，提高模型可解释性；并利用物理意义的光谱波段分组作为并行流，进一步提升模型的可解释性。实验结果表明，该模型在基准数据集上优于现有方法，在准确性和可解释性方面均有提升。

item.title: 生产环境RAG系统中检索融合效果的实证分析
item.content:encoded:
检索融合在生产环境中未能提升问答质量，反而增加延迟。



摘要： 本文评估了在企业知识库上的生产型RAG系统中检索融合技术的效果。尽管检索融合能提高原始召回率，但在实际应用中由于重排序和截断，这些优势被抵消。实验显示，融合方法在KB级Top-k准确率上不如单查询基线，且引入额外延迟。研究指出，在考虑重排序限制和上下文预算后，以召回为导向的融合技术效果递减，建议建立综合考虑检索质量、系统效率和下游影响的评估框架。

item.title: 空间推理能力的评估与诊断框架研究
item.content:encoded:
文章提出SpatialText框架，用于评估模型是否具备真正的空间推理能力。



摘要： 文章探讨了当前大型语言模型在空间推理方面的局限性，指出它们依赖语言统计规律而非构建真实的内部空间模型。为此，作者提出SpatialText框架，通过结合真实3D环境描述和代码生成场景，系统评估模型的空间推理能力。结果显示，尽管模型能处理显性空间事实，但在视角转换和局部参考系推理上存在明显不足，表明其仍依赖语言共现策略。该框架为诊断人工智能的空间智能提供了有效工具。

item.title: 扩散语言模型的记忆行为分析与比较
item.content:encoded:
研究扩散语言模型的记忆特性及其与自回归模型的差异。



摘要： 本文系统研究了扩散语言模型（DLMs）的记忆行为，探讨其与自回归语言模型（ARMs）在数据再现方面的异同。通过提出一个统一的概率提取框架，作者揭示了采样分辨率与记忆概率之间的单调关系，并验证了理论预测。实验表明，在相同条件下，DLMs在个人身份信息泄露方面显著优于ARMs，为模型安全性提供了新的见解。

item.title: 方向性CDNV在自监督表示中的关键作用
item.content:encoded:
方向性CDNV影响自监督学习的少样本迁移与多任务干扰。



摘要： 本文探讨了方向性CDNV（决策轴方差）在自监督表示中的核心作用，指出其在提升少样本任务迁移能力和降低多任务间干扰方面具有关键意义。研究证明，当类别分离方向上的方差较小时，可获得更强的泛化能力，并推导出包含有限样本修正项的多类分类界。此外，小方向性CDNV促使不同任务的决策轴趋于正交，从而减少干扰。实验表明，在自监督预训练中方向性CDNV会显著下降，且与实际少样本误差高度相关。在合成多任务数据上也验证了决策轴的正交性。

item.title: 极性在信息几何中的应用与推广
item.content:encoded:
本文研究极性在凸体和信息几何中的应用，提出极性散度概念。



摘要： 本文探讨了n维射影几何中的极性概念，并将其与Legendre-Fenchel变换联系起来。通过二次极性泛函，作者展示了极性可以表示为变形的Legendre极性或变形凸体的Legendre极性，并通过线性代数方法进行高效操作。进一步地，文章定义了极性散度，证明其可推广Fenchel-Young散度或Bregman散度。该研究深化了对信息几何中核心对偶性的理解，并揭示了总Bregman散度作为总极性Fenchel-Young散度的新视角。

item.title: 面向真实临床流程的中文医疗大模型基准测试：ClinConsensus
item.content:encoded:
引入ClinConsensus评估医疗大模型在真实临床场景中的表现。



摘要： 本文介绍了ClinConsensus，一个由临床专家精心构建、验证和质量控制的中文医疗基准。该基准包含2500个开放性病例，覆盖从预防到长期随访的完整护理流程，涵盖36个医学专科和12种常见临床任务类型。为可靠评估复杂场景，研究采用基于评分标准的评估协议，并提出临床适用一致性评分（CACS@k）。同时引入双评审框架，结合高能力LLM与本地可部署的微调模型，实现与医生判断一致的可扩展评估。通过ClinConsensus对多个先进LLM进行全面评估，发现其在任务主题、护理阶段和医学专科方面存在显著差异，临床可操作治疗计划仍是主要瓶颈。ClinConsensus作为可扩展基准，旨在支持具备临床基础、适应真实部署的医疗大模型发展。

item.title: 基于混合信念强化学习的多智能体协同探索框架
item.content:encoded:
提出HBRL框架提升多智能体在异质需求中的任务表现。



摘要： 本文提出一种混合信念强化学习（HBRL）框架，用于协调多个自主代理在空间异质需求中进行探索和任务执行。该框架分为两个阶段：第一阶段通过Log-Gaussian Cox Process构建空间信念，并使用PathMI规划器生成信息驱动轨迹；第二阶段将轨迹控制转移给SAC智能体，通过双通道知识迁移进行热启动。该方法在多无人机无线服务任务中验证，结果显示其累计奖励提高10.8%，收敛速度加快38%。消融实验表明双通道迁移优于单一通道。

item.title: 改进DETR架构在多时刻搜索查询中的性能
item.content:encoded:
研究DETR在搜索查询中的性能下降问题并提出改进方法。



摘要： 本文研究了基于caption的查询训练的DETR架构在搜索查询任务中的性能下降问题。通过修改三个公开数据集的文本查询，作者引入了三个新基准，并发现两个关键挑战：语言差距和多时刻差距。此外，识别出解码器-查询坍塌是导致多时刻实例泛化能力差的主要原因。通过调整架构提升活跃解码器查询数量，实验表明该方法在搜索查询上提升了14.82% mAP_m，在多时刻搜索查询上提升了21.83% mAP_m。相关代码、模型和数据已公开。

item.title: 构建统一的时尚智能框架OmniFashion
item.content:encoded:
OmniFashion统一多任务时尚智能，提升跨任务泛化能力。



摘要： 文章介绍了OmniFashion，一个基于FashionX数据集的统一视觉-语言框架，旨在解决时尚智能任务中因监督碎片化和标注不全导致的语义结构不一致问题。该框架通过统一的时尚对话范式，整合了检索、推荐、识别和对话等任务，实现了多任务推理与交互式对话。实验表明，OmniFashion在多个子任务和检索基准上表现出色，为通用、对话导向的时尚智能提供了可扩展路径。

item.title: RouteGoT：一种可控制预算的图结构推理框架
item.content:encoded:
RouteGoT提升推理效率并降低token消耗。



摘要： 本文提出RouteGoT，一种基于图结构的推理框架，通过动态分配强模型和轻量模型来优化推理过程。该框架在规划和最终合成阶段使用强模型，在子任务中使用轻量模型，从而减少token消耗并提高准确性。实验表明，RouteGoT在多个基准测试中表现优于现有方法，平均准确率提升8.1个百分点，输出token减少79.1%。同时，它在不同预算目标下保持良好的性能-成本平衡，展现出更强的鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于元数据的低秩自适应框架GLoRIA提升方言语音识别效果
item.content:encoded:
GLoRIA通过元数据优化低秩更新，提升方言ASR性能。



摘要： 本文提出GLoRIA，一种参数高效的方言语音识别自适应框架。该方法利用元数据（如坐标）调节预训练编码器中的低秩更新，在GCND语料库上表现优于全微调、LoRA及其他方法，实现更优词错误率且仅更新10%以下参数。GLoRIA还能泛化到未见方言，并支持可解释的地理可视化适配模式，为方言ASR提供高效、可解释的解决方案。

item.title: 基于量化感知的高效数据蒸馏方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出QuADD框架，提升数据蒸馏的精度与效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种新的数据蒸馏方法——量化感知数据蒸馏（QuADD），在固定比特预算下同时优化数据集的紧凑性和精度。该方法通过在蒸馏过程中引入可微分的量化模块，实现合成样本和量化参数的端到端联合优化。从率失真视角分析了样本数量与精度之间的比特分配对学习性能的影响，并支持统一和自适应非均匀量化。实验表明，QuADD在图像分类和3GPP波束管理任务中均优于现有方法，提升了每比特的准确率，为信息高效的蒸馏提供了新标准。

item.title: 基于仿真数据的单麦克风语音检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于仿真的单麦克风语音检测方法，提升助听器性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于仿真的单麦克风语音检测（OVD）方法，以提高助听器的用户舒适度和语音清晰度。传统方法依赖多麦克风或额外传感器，增加设备复杂性和成本。本研究通过模拟声学传递函数（ATFs）进行数据增强，使模型适应多种空间传播条件。采用基于Transformer的分类器，先在分析生成的ATFs上训练，再逐步微调为更详细的头部和躯干模型。实验结果显示，在模拟头部和躯干测试数据中准确率达95.52%，在短时条件下保持90.02%的准确率，真实助听器录音中未微调情况下也达到80%。这表明模型具备从仿真到现实的泛化能力，具有实际应用潜力。

item.title: AI辅助决策中信任机制的实验研究
item.content:encoded:
研究探讨AI辅助决策中的信任机制与工作流程影响。



摘要： 本文研究了AI辅助决策中用户对AI建议的信任问题，分析了不同决策流程（1步与2步设置）、解释信息以及用户领域知识和AI经验对信任和行为的影响。研究发现，2步流程并未有效减少过度依赖AI的情况，且决策流程对自我报告的信任无直接影响，但与领域知识和解释存在交互作用。此外，研究证实了自我报告的信任与实际行为依赖是两个独立的构念，应分别评估。

item.title: 通过多激活稀疏性训练提升深度神经网络泛化能力
item.content:encoded:
研究提出多激活稀疏性训练策略以提升模型泛化性能。



摘要： 本文探讨了深度神经网络的泛化问题，受生物系统启发，提出鲁棒内部表示应在密集和稀疏激活状态下均有效。研究引入了一种简单的训练策略，通过全局top-k约束隐藏层激活，并通过渐进压缩和周期性重置使模型在多个激活预算间循环。实验使用CIFAR-10数据集和WRN-28-4模型，在无数据增强的情况下，两种自适应保持率控制策略均优于密集基线训练。结果表明，跨不同激活稀疏性 regimes 的联合训练可能是一种提升泛化能力的有效方法。

item.title: 基于单目视频的像素级3D轨迹估计方法Track4World
item.content:encoded:
提出Track4World模型实现单目视频中像素级3D轨迹高效跟踪。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Track4World的前馈模型，用于从单目视频中高效地进行世界坐标系下的像素级3D轨迹跟踪。该模型基于VGGT风格的ViT构建全局3D场景表示，并采用新颖的3D相关机制，同时估计任意帧对之间的像素级2D和3D密集流。结合估计的场景流与重建的3D几何信息，实现了视频中每个像素的高效3D跟踪。在多个基准测试中，该方法在2D/3D流估计和3D跟踪任务中均优于现有方法，展示了其在真实4D重建任务中的鲁棒性和可扩展性。

item.title: 基于局部到全局匹配的开放世界目标检测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出L2G-Det框架，提升开放世界中目标检测与分割性能。



摘要： 本文研究了在开放世界环境中检测和分割新目标实例的问题。传统基于提议的方法对提议质量敏感，容易在遮挡和背景杂乱情况下失效。为此，作者提出L2G-Det框架，通过模板与查询图像之间的密集块级匹配，避免显式目标提议。局部匹配块生成候选点，经过候选选择模块过滤后，用于提示增强版的Segment Anything Model，从而实现完整实例掩码的可靠重建。实验表明该方法在复杂开放世界场景中表现优于传统方法。

item.title: 基于规范空间的3D语义分割方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新方法实现3D语义分割的稳定性和可迁移性。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为\methodName{}的方法，用于解决开放世界下3D语义分割的不稳定性问题。该方法通过引入隐式规范参考框架，使模型能够从数据中学习到统一的规范空间表示。研究通过LLM引导的类别内和跨类别对齐构建了一个包含200个类别的规范数据集，揭示了跨类别的空间规律。通过双分支架构和规范图锚定与规范框校准机制，模型将姿态变化和对称性转化为稳定的规范嵌入，从而提升了分割结果的稳定性与可迁移性。实验表明，该方法在开放世界3D语义分割任务中达到了最新水平。

item.title: 基于PEFT框架的隐蔽后门注入方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出SFT-then-GRPO方法实现模型后门注入。



摘要： 本文研究了开放权重大语言模型在代理AI中的应用风险，提出一种通过多阶段参数高效微调（PEFT）框架进行隐蔽后门注入的新方法。该方法分为两个步骤：首先使用LoRA进行监督微调植入“潜伏代理”能力，随后通过专门设计的奖励函数进行组相对策略优化，以强化两种行为：触发特异性与操作隐蔽性。实验表明，这些被污染的模型在良性任务上仍保持先进性能，从而鼓励其采用。研究揭示了对齐过程中的一个关键失败模式，即强化学习被用来隐藏而非消除灾难性漏洞，并讨论了识别策略，包括标准基准的差异和随机探测。
